annotations.label: region
annotations.definition: A sequence_feature with an extent greater than zero. A nucleotide region is composed of bases and a polypeptide region is composed of amino acids.

annotations.label: sequence_secondary_structure
annotations.definition: A folded sequence.

annotations.label: G_quartet
annotations.definition: G-quartets are unusual nucleic acid structures consisting of a planar arrangement where each guanine is hydrogen bonded by hoogsteen pairing to another guanine in the quartet.

annotations.label: interior_coding_exon
annotations.definition: A coding exon that is not the most 3-prime or the most 5-prime in a given transcript.

annotations.label: satellite_DNA
annotations.definition: The many tandem repeats (identical or related) of a short basic repeating unit; many have a base composition or other property different from the genome average that allows them to be separated from the bulk (main band) genomic DNA.

annotations.label: PCR_product
annotations.definition: A region amplified by a PCR reaction.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. This term is now located in OBI, with the following ID OBI_0000406.

annotations.label: read_pair
annotations.definition: One of a pair of sequencing reads in which the two members of the pair are related by originating at either end of a clone insert.

annotations.label: protein_coding
annotations.definition: A gene which, when transcribed, can be translated into a protein.

annotations.label: non_protein_coding
annotations.definition: A gene which can be transcribed, but will not be translated into a protein.

annotations.label: scRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: The primary transcript of any one of several small cytoplasmic RNA molecules present in the cytoplasm and sometimes nucleus of a Eukaryote.

annotations.label: scRNA
annotations.definition: A small non coding RNA sequence, present in the cytoplasm.

annotations.label: INR_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters required for the correct positioning of the polymerase for the start of transcription. Overlaps the TSS. The mammalian consensus sequence is YYAN(T|A)YY; the Drosophila consensus sequence is TCA(G|T)t(T|C). In each the A is at position +1 with respect to the TSS. Functionally similar to the TATA box element.
annotations.comment: Binds TAF1, TAF2.

annotations.label: DPE_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters; Positioned from +28 to +32 with respect to the TSS (+1). Experimental results suggest that the DPE acts in conjunction with the INR_motif to provide a binding site for TFIID in the absence of a TATA box to mediate transcription of TATA-less promoters. Consensus sequence (A|G)G(A|T)(C|T)(G|A|C).
annotations.comment: Binds TAF6, TAF9.

annotations.label: BREu_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, located immediately upstream of some TATA box elements at -37 to -32 with respect to the TSS (+1). Consensus sequence is (G|C)(G|C)(G|A)CGCC. Binds TFIIB.
annotations.comment: Binds TFIIB.

annotations.label: PSE_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of the promoters of snRNA genes transcribed by RNA polymerase II or by RNA polymerase III. Located between -45 and -60 relative to the TSS. The human PSE_motif consensus sequence is TCACCNTNA(C|G)TNAAAAG(T|G). The basal transcription factor, snRNA-activating protein complex (SNAPc), binds the PSE_motif and is required for the transcription of both RNA polymerase II and III transcribed small-nuclear RNA genes.

annotations.label: linkage_group
annotations.definition: A group of loci that can be grouped in a linear order representing the different degrees of linkage among the genes concerned.

annotations.label: RNA_internal_loop
annotations.definition: A region of double stranded RNA where the bases do not conform to WC base pairing. The loop is closed on both sides by canonical base pairing. If the interruption to base pairing occurs on one strand only, it is known as a bulge.

annotations.label: asymmetric_RNA_internal_loop
annotations.definition: An internal RNA loop where one of the strands includes more bases than the corresponding region on the other strand.

annotations.label: A_minor_RNA_motif
annotations.definition: A region forming a motif, composed of adenines, where the minor groove edges are inserted into the minor groove of another helix.

annotations.label: K_turn_RNA_motif
annotations.definition: The kink turn (K-turn) is an RNA structural motif that creates a sharp (~120 degree) bend between two continuous helices.

annotations.label: sarcin_like_RNA_motif
annotations.definition: A loop in ribosomal RNA containing the sites of attack for ricin and sarcin.

annotations.label: symmetric_RNA_internal_loop
annotations.definition: An internal RNA loop where the extent of the loop on both stands is the same size.

annotations.label: base_pair
annotations.definition: Two bases paired opposite each other by hydrogen bonds creating a secondary structure.

annotations.label: WC_base_pair
annotations.definition: The canonical base pair, where two bases interact via WC edges, with glycosidic bonds oriented cis relative to the axis of orientation.

annotations.label: sugar_edge_base_pair
annotations.definition: A type of non-canonical base-pairing.

annotations.label: aptamer
annotations.definition: DNA or RNA molecules that have been selected from random pools based on their ability to bind other molecules.

annotations.label: DNA_aptamer
annotations.definition: DNA molecules that have been selected from random pools based on their ability to bind other molecules.

annotations.label: RNA_aptamer
annotations.definition: RNA molecules that have been selected from random pools based on their ability to bind other molecules.

annotations.label: morpholino_oligo
annotations.definition: Morpholino oligos are synthesized from four different Morpholino subunits, each of which contains one of the four genetic bases (A, C, G, T) linked to a 6-membered morpholine ring. Eighteen to 25 subunits of these four subunit types are joined in a specific order by non-ionic phosphorodiamidate intersubunit linkages to give a Morpholino.

annotations.label: riboswitch
annotations.definition: A riboswitch is a part of an mRNA that can act as a direct sensor of small molecules to control their own expression. A riboswitch is a cis element in the 5' end of an mRNA, that acts as a direct sensor of metabolites.

annotations.label: matrix_attachment_site
annotations.definition: A DNA region that is required for the binding of chromatin to the nuclear matrix.

annotations.label: locus_control_region
annotations.definition: A DNA region that includes DNAse hypersensitive sites located near a gene that confers the high-level, position-independent, and copy number-dependent expression to that gene.
annotations.comment: Definition updated Nov 10 2020, Colin Logie from GREEKC helped us realize that LCRs can also be located 3' to a gene.

annotations.label: match_part
annotations.definition: A part of a match, for example an hsp from blast is a match_part.

annotations.label: genomic_clone
annotations.definition: A clone of a DNA region of a genome.

annotations.label: processed_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene created via retrotranposition of the mRNA of a functional protein-coding parent gene followed by accumulation of deleterious mutations lacking introns and promoters, often including a polyA tail.
annotations.comment: Please not the synonym R psi M uses the spelled out form of the greek letter.

annotations.label: pseudogene_by_unequal_crossing_over
annotations.definition: A pseudogene caused by unequal crossing over at recombination.

annotations.label: probe
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence used experimentally to detect the presence or absence of a complementary nucleic acid.

annotations.label: aneuploid
annotations.definition: A kind of chromosome variation where the chromosome complement is not an exact multiple of the haploid number.

annotations.label: hyperploid
annotations.definition: A kind of chromosome variation where the chromosome complement is not an exact multiple of the haploid number as extra chromosomes are present.

annotations.label: hypoploid
annotations.definition: A kind of chromosome variation where the chromosome complement is not an exact multiple of the haploid number as some chromosomes are missing.

annotations.label: operator
annotations.definition: A regulatory element of an operon to which activators or repressors bind thereby effecting translation of genes in that operon.
annotations.comment: Moved to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) from gene_group_regulatory_region (SO:0000752) on 11 Feb 2021 when SO:0000752 was merged into SO:0001055. See GitHub Issue #529.

annotations.label: nuclease_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, of a nucleotide molecule, that interacts selectively and non-covalently with polypeptide residues of a nuclease.

annotations.label: compound_chromosome_arm
annotations.definition: One arm of a compound chromosome.
annotations.comment: FLAG - this term is should probably be a part of rather than an is_a.

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the nucleotide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with polypeptide residues of a restriction enzyme.
annotations.comment: A region of a molecule that binds to a restriction enzyme.

annotations.label: deficient_intrachromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An intrachromosomal transposition whereby a translocation in which one of the four broken ends loses a segment before re-joining.

annotations.label: deficient_interchromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An interchromosomal transposition whereby a translocation in which one of the four broken ends loses a segment before re-joining.

annotations.label: free_chromosome_arm
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variation whereby an arm exists as an individual chromosome element.

annotations.label: overlapping
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a gene that has a sequence that overlaps the sequence of another gene.

annotations.label: inside_intron
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a gene when it is located within the intron of another gene.

annotations.label: inside_intron_antiparallel
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a gene when it is located within the intron of another gene and on the opposite strand.

annotations.label: inside_intron_parallel
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a gene when it is located within the intron of another gene and on the same strand.

annotations.label: five_prime_three_prime_overlap
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a gene when the five prime region overlaps with another gene's 3' region.

annotations.label: five_prime_five_prime_overlap
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a gene when the five prime region overlaps with another gene's five prime region.

annotations.label: three_prime_three_prime_overlap
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a gene when the 3' region overlaps with another gene's 3' region.

annotations.label: three_prime_five_prime_overlap
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a gene when the 3' region overlaps with another gene's 5' region.

annotations.label: antisense
annotations.definition: A region sequence that is complementary to a sequence of messenger RNA.

annotations.label: polycistronic_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that is polycistronic.

annotations.label: dicistronic_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that is dicistronic.

annotations.label: operon_member
annotations.definition: A gene that is a member of an operon, which is a set of genes transcribed together as a unit.

annotations.label: macronuclear_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the macronuclei of ciliates.

annotations.label: micronuclear_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the micronuclei of a cell.

annotations.label: nuclear_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from nuclear sequence.

annotations.label: mt_gene
annotations.definition: A gene located in mitochondrial sequence.

annotations.label: kinetoplast_gene
annotations.definition: A gene located in kinetoplast sequence.

annotations.label: plastid_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from plastid sequence.

annotations.label: apicoplast_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from apicoplast sequence.

annotations.label: ct_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from chloroplast sequence.

annotations.label: chromoplast_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from chromoplast_sequence.

annotations.label: cyanelle_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from cyanelle sequence.

annotations.label: leucoplast_gene
annotations.definition: A plastid gene from leucoplast sequence.

annotations.label: proplastid_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from proplastid sequence.

annotations.label: nucleomorph_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from nucleomorph sequence.

annotations.label: plasmid_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from plasmid sequence.

annotations.label: proviral_gene
annotations.definition: A gene from proviral sequence.

annotations.label: endogenous_retroviral_gene
annotations.definition: A proviral gene with origin endogenous retrovirus.

annotations.label: transposable_element
annotations.definition: A transposon or insertion sequence. An element that can insert in a variety of DNA sequences.

annotations.label: expressed_sequence_match
annotations.definition: A match to an EST or cDNA sequence.

annotations.label: clone_insert_end
annotations.definition: The end of the clone insert.

annotations.label: polypeptide
annotations.definition: A sequence of amino acids linked by peptide bonds which may lack appreciable tertiary structure and may not be liable to irreversible denaturation.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology. The term 'protein' was merged with 'polypeptide'. Although 'protein' was a sequence_attribute and therefore meant to describe the quality rather than an actual feature, it was being used erroneously. It is replaced by 'peptidyl' as the polymer attribute.

annotations.label: chromosome_arm
annotations.definition: A region of the chromosome between the centromere and the telomere. Human chromosomes have two arms, the p arm (short) and the q arm (long) which are separated from each other by the centromere.

annotations.label: sequencing_primer
annotations.definition: A single stranded oligo used for polymerase chain reaction.

annotations.label: mRNA_with_frameshift
annotations.definition: An mRNA with a frameshift.

annotations.label: sequence_feature
annotations.definition: Any extent of continuous biological sequence.

annotations.label: transposable_element_gene
annotations.definition: A gene encoded within a transposable element. For example gag, int, env and pol are the transposable element genes of the TY element in yeast.

annotations.label: primer
annotations.definition: An oligo to which new deoxyribonucleotides can be added by DNA polymerase.

annotations.label: proviral_region
annotations.definition: A viral sequence which has integrated into a host genome.

annotations.label: methylated_cytosine
annotations.definition: A methylated deoxy-cytosine.

annotations.label: edited
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that is modified by editing.

annotations.label: transcript_with_translational_frameshift
annotations.definition: A transcript with a translational frameshift.

annotations.label: regulated
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a sequence that is regulated.

annotations.label: protein_coding_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript that, at least in part, encodes one or more proteins.
annotations.comment: May contain introns.

annotations.label: forward_primer
annotations.definition: A single stranded oligo used for polymerase chain reaction.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: RNA_sequence_secondary_structure
annotations.definition: A folded RNA sequence.

annotations.label: transcriptionally_regulated
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a gene that is regulated at transcription.
annotations.comment: By:<protein_id>.

annotations.label: transcriptionally_constitutive
annotations.definition: Expressed in relatively constant amounts without regard to cellular environmental conditions such as the concentration of a particular substrate.

annotations.label: transcriptionally_induced
annotations.definition: An inducer molecule is required for transcription to occur.

annotations.label: transcriptionally_repressed
annotations.definition: A repressor molecule is required for transcription to stop.

annotations.label: silenced_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is silenced.

annotations.label: gene_silenced_by_DNA_modification
annotations.definition: A gene that is silenced by DNA modification.

annotations.label: gene_silenced_by_DNA_methylation
annotations.definition: A gene that is silenced by DNA methylation.

annotations.label: post_translationally_regulated
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a gene that is regulated after it has been translated.

annotations.label: translationally_regulated
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a gene that is regulated as it is translated.

annotations.label: reverse_primer
annotations.definition: A single stranded oligo used for polymerase chain reaction.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: epigenetically_modified
annotations.definition: This attribute describes a gene where heritable changes other than those in the DNA sequence occur. These changes include: modification to the DNA (such as DNA methylation, the covalent modification of cytosine), and post-translational modification of histones.

annotations.label: genomically_imprinted
annotations.definition: Imprinted genes are epigenetically modified genes that are expressed monoallelically according to their parent of origin.

annotations.label: maternally_imprinted
annotations.definition: The maternal copy of the gene is modified, rendering it transcriptionally silent.

annotations.label: paternally_imprinted
annotations.definition: The paternal copy of the gene is modified, rendering it transcriptionally silent.

annotations.label: allelically_excluded
annotations.definition: Allelic exclusion is a process occurring in diploid organisms, where a gene is inactivated and not expressed in that cell.
annotations.comment: Examples are x-inactivation and immunoglobulin formation.

annotations.label: gene_rearranged_at_DNA_level
annotations.definition: An epigenetically modified gene, rearranged at the DNA level.

annotations.label: ribosome_entry_site
annotations.definition: Region in mRNA where ribosome assembles.

annotations.label: attenuator
annotations.definition: A sequence segment located within the five prime end of an mRNA that causes premature termination of translation.

annotations.label: terminator
annotations.definition: The sequence of DNA located either at the end of the transcript that causes RNA polymerase to terminate transcription.
annotations.comment: Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: DNA_sequence_secondary_structure
annotations.definition: A folded DNA sequence.

annotations.label: assembly_component
annotations.definition: A region of known length which may be used to manufacture a longer region.

annotations.label: recoded_codon
annotations.definition: A codon that has been redefined at translation. The redefinition may be as a result of translational bypass, translational frameshifting or stop codon readthrough.

annotations.label: capped
annotations.definition: An attribute describing when a sequence, usually an mRNA is capped by the addition of a modified guanine nucleotide at the 5' end.

annotations.label: exon
annotations.definition: A region of the transcript sequence within a gene which is not removed from the primary RNA transcript by RNA splicing.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: supercontig
annotations.definition: One or more contigs that have been ordered and oriented using end-read information. Contains gaps that are filled with N's.

annotations.label: contig
annotations.definition: A contiguous sequence derived from sequence assembly. Has no gaps, but may contain N's from unavailable bases.

annotations.label: read
annotations.definition: A sequence obtained from a single sequencing experiment. Typically a read is produced when a base calling program interprets information from a chromatogram trace file produced from a sequencing machine.

annotations.label: clone
annotations.definition: A piece of DNA that has been inserted in a vector so that it can be propagated in a host bacterium or some other organism.

annotations.label: YAC
annotations.definition: Yeast Artificial Chromosome, a vector constructed from the telomeric, centromeric, and replication origin sequences needed for replication in yeast cells.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: BAC
annotations.definition: Bacterial Artificial Chromosome, a cloning vector that can be propagated as mini-chromosomes in a bacterial host.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: PAC
annotations.definition: The P1-derived artificial chromosome are DNA constructs that are derived from the DNA of P1 bacteriophage. They can carry large amounts (about 100-300 kilobases) of other sequences for a variety of bioengineering purposes. It is one type of vector used to clone DNA fragments (100- to 300-kb insert size; average, 150 kb) in Escherichia coli cells.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology. Drosophila melanogaster PACs carry an average insert size of 80 kb. The library represents a 6-fold coverage of the genome.

annotations.label: plasmid
annotations.definition: A self replicating, using the hosts cellular machinery, often circular nucleic acid molecule that is distinct from a chromosome in the organism.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: cosmid
annotations.definition: A cloning vector that is a hybrid of lambda phages and a plasmid that can be propagated as a plasmid or packaged as a phage,since they retain the lambda cos sites.
annotations.comment: Paper: vans GA et al. High efficiency vectors for cosmid microcloning and genomic analysis. Gene 1989; 79(1):9-20. This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: phagemid
annotations.definition: A plasmid which carries within its sequence a bacteriophage replication origin. When the host bacterium is infected with "helper" phage, a phagemid is replicated along with the phage DNA and packaged into phage capsids.

annotations.label: fosmid
annotations.definition: A cloning vector that utilizes the E. coli F factor.
annotations.comment: Birren BW et al. A human chromosome 22 fosmid resource: mapping and analysis of 96 clones. Genomics 1996.

annotations.label: deletion
annotations.definition: The point at which one or more contiguous nucleotides were excised.

annotations.label: methylated_adenine
annotations.definition: A modified base in which adenine has been methylated.

annotations.label: splice_site
annotations.definition: Consensus region of primary transcript bordering junction of splicing. A region that overlaps exactly 2 base and adjacent_to splice_junction.
annotations.comment: With spliceosomal introns, the splice sites bind the spliceosomal machinery.

annotations.label: five_prime_cis_splice_site
annotations.definition: Intronic 2 bp region bordering the exon, at the 5' edge of the intron. A splice_site that is downstream_adjacent_to exon and starts intron.

annotations.label: three_prime_cis_splice_site
annotations.definition: Intronic 2 bp region bordering the exon, at the 3' edge of the intron. A splice_site that is upstream_adjacent_to exon and finishes intron.

annotations.label: enhancer
annotations.definition: A cis-acting sequence that increases the utilization of (some) eukaryotic promoters, and can function in either orientation and in any location (upstream or downstream) relative to the promoter.
annotations.comment: An enhancer may participate in an enhanceosome GO:0034206. A protein-DNA complex formed by the association of a distinct set of general and specific transcription factors with a region of enhancer DNA. The cooperative assembly of an enhanceosome confers specificity of transcriptional regulation. This comment is a place holder should we start to make cross products with GO.

annotations.label: enhancer_bound_by_factor
annotations.definition: An enhancer bound by a factor.

annotations.label: promoter
annotations.definition: A regulatory_region composed of the TSS(s) and binding sites for TF_complexes of the core transcription machinery. A region (DNA) to which RNA polymerase binds, to begin transcription.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology. The region on a DNA molecule involved in RNA polymerase binding to initiate transcription. Moved from is_a: SO:0001055 transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region as per request from GREEKC initiative in August 2020. Merged with RNA_polymerase_promoter (SO:0001203) Aug 2020. Moved up one level from is_a CRM (SO:0000727) to is_a transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) as part of the GREEKC work January 2021. Pascale Gaudet from Gene Ontology pointed out that CRM can be located upstream of the promoter and therefore cannot include the promoter.

annotations.label: RNApol_I_promoter
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence in eukaryotic DNA to which RNA polymerase I binds, to begin transcription.
annotations.comment: parent term RNA_polymerase_promoter SO:0001203 was obsoleted in Aug 2020, so term has been moved to eukaryotic_promoter SO:0002221.

annotations.label: RNApol_II_promoter
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence in eukaryotic DNA to which RNA polymerase II binds, to begin transcription.
annotations.comment: parent term RNA_polymerase_promoter SO:0001203 was obsoleted in Aug 2020, so term has been moved to eukaryotic_promoter SO:0002221.

annotations.label: RNApol_III_promoter
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence in eukaryotic DNA to which RNA polymerase III binds, to begin transcription.
annotations.comment: parent term RNA_polymerase_promoter SO:0001203 was obsoleted in Aug 2020, so term has been moved to eukaryotic_promoter SO:0002221.

annotations.label: CAAT_signal
annotations.definition: Part of a conserved sequence located about 75-bp upstream of the start point of eukaryotic transcription units which may be involved in RNA polymerase binding; consensus=GG(C|T)CAATCT.

annotations.label: GC_rich_promoter_region
annotations.definition: A conserved GC-rich region located upstream of the start point of eukaryotic transcription units which may occur in multiple copies or in either orientation; consensus=GGGCGG.

annotations.label: TATA_box
annotations.definition: A conserved AT-rich septamer found about 25-bp before the start point of many eukaryotic RNA polymerase II transcript units; may be involved in positioning the enzyme for correct initiation; consensus=TATA(A|T)A(A|T).
annotations.comment: Binds TBP.

annotations.label: minus_10_signal
annotations.definition: A conserved region about 10-bp upstream of the start point of bacterial transcription units which may be involved in binding RNA polymerase; consensus=TAtAaT. This region is associated with sigma factor 70.
annotations.comment: Changed from is_a SO:0000713 DNA_motif to is_a SO:0002312 core_prokaryotic_promoter_element in response to GREEKC Initiative Dave Sant Aug 2020. Changed from is_a SO:0002312 core_prokaryotic_promoter_element back to is_a SO:0000713 DNA_motif to be consistent with minus_12_signal and minus_24_signal on 12 July 2021.

annotations.label: minus_35_signal
annotations.definition: A conserved hexamer about 35-bp upstream of the start point of bacterial transcription units; consensus=TTGACa or TGTTGACA. This region is associated with sigma factor 70.
annotations.comment: Changed from is_a SO:0000713 DNA_motif to is_a SO:0002312 core_prokaryotic_promoter_element in response to GREEKC Initiative Dave Sant Aug 2020. Changed from is_a SO:0002312 core_prokaryotic_promoter_element back to is_a SO:0000713 DNA_motif to be consistent with minus_12_signal and minus_24_signal on 12 July 2021.

annotations.label: cross_genome_match
annotations.definition: A nucleotide match against a sequence from another organism.

annotations.label: operon
annotations.definition: The DNA region of a group of adjacent genes whose transcription is coordinated on one or several mutually overlapping transcription units transcribed in the same direction and sharing at least one gene.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology. Definition updated with per Mejia-Almonte et.al Redefining fundamental concepts of transcription initiation in prokaryotes Aug 5 2020.

annotations.label: clone_insert_start
annotations.definition: The start of the clone insert.

annotations.label: retrotransposon
annotations.definition: A transposable element that is incorporated into a chromosome by a mechanism that requires reverse transcriptase.

annotations.label: translated_nucleotide_match
annotations.definition: A match against a translated sequence.

annotations.label: DNA_transposon
annotations.definition: A transposon where the mechanism of transposition is via a DNA intermediate.

annotations.label: non_transcribed_region
annotations.definition: A region of the gene which is not transcribed.

annotations.label: U2_intron
annotations.definition: A major type of spliceosomal intron spliced by the U2 spliceosome, that includes U1, U2, U4/U6 and U5 snRNAs.
annotations.comment: May have either GT-AG or AT-AG 5' and 3' boundaries.

annotations.label: primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that in its initial state requires modification to be functional.
annotations.comment: See "precursor_RNA" (SO:0002393) for RNA which may be the result of post-transcriptional processing. If the RNA in question is known not to have been processed, use this class ("primary_transcript"). On 9 Sep 2025, Removed "has_exact_synonym : INSDC_feature:precursor_RNA" and "has_exact_synonym : precursor RNA" from the annotation for this class at the request of Terence Murphy of NCBI. See GitHub Issue #655.

annotations.label: LTR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: A retrotransposon flanked by long terminal repeat sequences.

annotations.label: intron
annotations.definition: A region of a primary transcript that is transcribed, but removed from within the transcript by splicing together the sequences (exons) on either side of it.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: non_LTR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: A retrotransposon without long terminal repeat sequences.

annotations.label: five_prime_intron
annotations.definition: An intron that is the most 5-prime in a given transcript.

annotations.label: interior_intron
annotations.definition: An intron that is not the most 3-prime or the most 5-prime in a given transcript.

annotations.label: three_prime_intron
annotations.definition: An intron that is the most 3-prime in a given transcript.

annotations.label: RFLP_fragment
annotations.definition: A DNA fragment used as a reagent to detect the polymorphic genomic loci by hybridizing against the genomic DNA digested with a given restriction enzyme.

annotations.label: LINE_element
annotations.definition: A dispersed repeat family with many copies, each from 1 to 6 kb long. New elements are generated by retroposition of a transcribed copy. Typically the LINE contains 2 ORF's one of which is reverse transcriptase, and 3'and 5' direct repeats.

annotations.label: coding_exon
annotations.definition: An exon whereby at least one base is part of a codon (here, 'codon' is inclusive of the stop_codon).

annotations.label: five_prime_coding_exon_coding_region
annotations.definition: The sequence of the five_prime_coding_exon that codes for protein.

annotations.label: three_prime_coding_exon_coding_region
annotations.definition: The sequence of the three_prime_coding_exon that codes for protein.

annotations.label: noncoding_exon
annotations.definition: An exon that does not contain any codons.

annotations.label: translocation
annotations.definition: A region of nucleotide sequence that has translocated to a new position. The observed adjacency of two previously separated regions.

annotations.label: five_prime_coding_exon
annotations.definition: The 5' most coding exon.

annotations.label: interior_exon
annotations.definition: An exon that is bounded by 5' and 3' splice sites.

annotations.label: three_prime_coding_exon
annotations.definition: The coding exon that is most 3-prime on a given transcript.

annotations.label: UTR
annotations.definition: Messenger RNA sequences that are untranslated and lie five prime or three prime to sequences which are translated.

annotations.label: five_prime_UTR
annotations.definition: A region at the 5' end of a mature transcript (preceding the initiation codon) that is not translated into a protein.

annotations.label: three_prime_UTR
annotations.definition: A region at the 3' end of a mature transcript (following the stop codon) that is not translated into a protein.

annotations.label: SINE_element
annotations.definition: A repetitive element, a few hundred base pairs long, that is dispersed throughout the genome. A common human SINE is the Alu element.

annotations.label: simple_sequence_length_variation
annotations.definition: SSLP are a kind of sequence alteration where the number of repeated sequences in intergenic regions may differ.

annotations.label: terminal_inverted_repeat_element
annotations.definition: A DNA transposable element defined as having termini with perfect, or nearly perfect short inverted repeats, generally 10 - 40 nucleotides long.

annotations.label: rRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a ribosomal RNA.

annotations.label: tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a transfer RNA (SO:0000253).

annotations.label: alanine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding alanyl tRNA.

annotations.label: arginine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding arginyl tRNA (SO:0000255).

annotations.label: asparagine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding asparaginyl tRNA (SO:0000256).

annotations.label: aspartic_acid_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding aspartyl tRNA (SO:0000257).

annotations.label: cysteine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding cysteinyl tRNA (SO:0000258).

annotations.label: glutamic_acid_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding glutaminyl tRNA (SO:0000260).

annotations.label: glutamine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding glutamyl tRNA (SO:0000260).

annotations.label: glycine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding glycyl tRNA (SO:0000263).

annotations.label: histidine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding histidyl tRNA (SO:0000262).

annotations.label: isoleucine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding isoleucyl tRNA (SO:0000263).

annotations.label: leucine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding leucyl tRNA (SO:0000264).

annotations.label: lysine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding lysyl tRNA (SO:0000265).

annotations.label: methionine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding methionyl tRNA (SO:0000266).

annotations.label: phenylalanine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding phenylalanyl tRNA (SO:0000267).

annotations.label: proline_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding prolyl tRNA (SO:0000268).

annotations.label: serine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding seryl tRNA (SO:000269).

annotations.label: threonine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding threonyl tRNA (SO:000270).

annotations.label: tryptophan_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding tryptophanyl tRNA (SO:000271).

annotations.label: tyrosine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding tyrosyl tRNA (SO:000272).

annotations.label: valine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding valyl tRNA (SO:000273).

annotations.label: snRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a small nuclear RNA (SO:0000274).

annotations.label: snoRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding one or more small nucleolar RNAs (SO:0000275).
annotations.comment: This definition was broadened 26 Jan 2021 to reflect that a single transcript can encode one or more snoRNAs. Brought to our attention by FlyBase. GitHub Issue #520 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/520).

annotations.label: mature_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript which has undergone the necessary modifications, if any, for its function. In eukaryotes this includes, for example, processing of introns, cleavage, base modification, and modifications to the 5' and/or the 3' ends, other than addition of bases. In bacteria functional mRNAs are usually not modified.
annotations.comment: A processed transcript cannot contain introns.

annotations.label: mRNA
annotations.definition: Messenger RNA is the intermediate molecule between DNA and protein. It includes UTR and coding sequences. It does not contain introns.
annotations.comment: An mRNA does not contain introns as it is a processed_transcript. The equivalent kind of primary_transcript is protein_coding_primary_transcript (SO:0000120) which may contain introns. This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: TF_binding_site
annotations.definition: A DNA site where a transcription factor binds.
annotations.comment: Definition updated along with definitions in Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585. Added relationship part_of SO:0000727 CRM in place of previous CRM relationship has_part TF_binding_site August 2020 in response to requests from GREEKC initiative. Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: ORF
annotations.definition: The in-frame interval between the stop codons of a reading frame which when read as sequential triplets, has the potential of encoding a sequential string of amino acids. TER(NNN)nTER.
annotations.comment: The definition was modified by Rama. ORF is defined by the sequence, whereas the CDS is defined according to whether a polypeptide is made. This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: transcript_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a transcript.

annotations.label: foldback_element
annotations.definition: A transposable element with extensive secondary structure, characterized by large modular imperfect long inverted repeats.

annotations.label: flanking_region
annotations.definition: The sequences extending on either side of a specific region.

annotations.label: chromosome_variation
annotations.definition: A deviation in chromosome structure or number.

annotations.label: internal_UTR
annotations.definition: A UTR bordered by the terminal and initial codons of two CDSs in a polycistronic transcript. Every UTR is either 5', 3' or internal.

annotations.label: untranslated_region_polycistronic_mRNA
annotations.definition: The untranslated sequence separating the 'cistrons' of multicistronic mRNA.

annotations.label: internal_ribosome_entry_site
annotations.definition: Sequence element that recruits a ribosomal subunit to internal mRNA for translation initiation.

annotations.label: polyadenylated
annotations.definition: A attribute describing the addition of a poly A tail to the 3' end of a mRNA molecule.

annotations.label: sequence_length_alteration
annotations.definition: A kind of kind of sequence alteration where the copies of a region present varies across a population.

annotations.label: modified_RNA_base_feature
annotations.definition: A post_transcriptionally modified base.

annotations.label: rRNA
annotations.definition: rRNA is an RNA component of a ribosome that can provide both structural scaffolding and catalytic activity.
annotations.comment: Definition updated 10 June 2021 as part of restructuring rRNA terms and reforming definitions to have similar structures. Request from EBI. See GitHub Issue #493

annotations.label: tRNA
annotations.definition: Transfer RNA (tRNA) molecules are approximately 80 nucleotides in length. Their secondary structure includes four short double-helical elements and three loops (D, anti-codon, and T loops). Further hydrogen bonds mediate the characteristic L-shaped molecular structure. Transfer RNAs have two regions of fundamental functional importance: the anti-codon, which is responsible for specific mRNA codon recognition, and the 3' end, to which the tRNA's corresponding amino acid is attached (by aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases). Transfer RNAs cope with the degeneracy of the genetic code in two manners: having more than one tRNA (with a specific anti-codon) for a particular amino acid; and 'wobble' base-pairing, i.e. permitting non-standard base-pairing at the 3rd anti-codon position.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: alanyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has an alanine anticodon, and a 3' alanine binding region.

annotations.label: rRNA_small_subunit_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a small ribosomal subunit RNA.

annotations.label: asparaginyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has an asparagine anticodon, and a 3' asparagine binding region.

annotations.label: aspartyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has an aspartic acid anticodon, and a 3' aspartic acid binding region.

annotations.label: cysteinyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a cysteine anticodon, and a 3' cysteine binding region.

annotations.label: glutaminyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a glutamine anticodon, and a 3' glutamine binding region.

annotations.label: glutamyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a glutamic acid anticodon, and a 3' glutamic acid binding region.

annotations.label: glycyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a glycine anticodon, and a 3' glycine binding region.

annotations.label: histidyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a histidine anticodon, and a 3' histidine binding region.

annotations.label: isoleucyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has an isoleucine anticodon, and a 3' isoleucine binding region.

annotations.label: leucyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a leucine anticodon, and a 3' leucine binding region.

annotations.label: lysyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a lysine anticodon, and a 3' lysine binding region.

annotations.label: methionyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a methionine anticodon, and a 3' methionine binding region.

annotations.label: phenylalanyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a phenylalanine anticodon, and a 3' phenylalanine binding region.

annotations.label: prolyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a proline anticodon, and a 3' proline binding region.

annotations.label: seryl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a serine anticodon, and a 3' serine binding region.

annotations.label: threonyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a threonine anticodon, and a 3' threonine binding region.

annotations.label: tryptophanyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a tryptophan anticodon, and a 3' tryptophan binding region.

annotations.label: tyrosyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a tyrosine anticodon, and a 3' tyrosine binding region.

annotations.label: valyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a valine anticodon, and a 3' valine binding region.

annotations.label: snRNA
annotations.definition: A small nuclear RNA molecule involved in pre-mRNA splicing and processing.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: snoRNA
annotations.definition: Small nucleolar RNAs (snoRNAs) are short non-coding RNAs enriched in the nucleolus as components of small nucleolar ribonucleoproteins. They guide ribose methylation and pseudouridylation of rRNAs and snRNAs, and a subgroup regulate excision of rRNAs from rRNA precursor transcripts. snoRNAs may also guide rRNA acetylation and tRNA methylation, and regulate mRNA abundance and alternative splicing.
annotations.comment: Updated the definition of snoRNA (SO:0000275) from "A snoRNA (small nucleolar RNA) is any one of a class of small RNAs that are associated with the eukaryotic nucleus as components of small nucleolar ribonucleoproteins. They participate in the processing or modifications of many RNAs, mostly ribosomal RNAs (rRNAs) though snoRNAs are also known to target other classes of RNA, including spliceosomal RNAs, tRNAs, and mRNAs via a stretch of sequence that is complementary to a sequence in the targeted RNA." to "Small nucleolar RNAs (snoRNAs) are short non-coding RNAs enriched in the nucleolus as components of small nucleolar ribonucleoproteins. They guide ribose methylation and pseudouridylation of rRNAs and snRNAs, and a subgroup regulate excision of rRNAs from rRNA precursor transcripts. snoRNAs may also guide rRNA acetylation and tRNA methylation, and regulate mRNA abundance and alternative splicing." to acknowledge that some snoRNAs functionally localize to other compartments (cytoplasm or even secreted). See GitHub Issue #578.

annotations.label: miRNA
annotations.definition: Small, ~22-nt, RNA molecule that is the endogenous transcript of a miRNA gene (or the product of other non coding RNA genes). Micro RNAs are produced from precursor molecules (SO:0001244) that can form local hairpin structures, which ordinarily are processed (usually via the Dicer pathway) such that a single miRNA molecule accumulates from one arm of a hairpin precursor molecule. Micro RNAs may trigger the cleavage of their target molecules or act as translational repressors.

annotations.label: bound_by_factor
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that is bound by another molecule.
annotations.comment: Formerly called transcript_by_bound_factor.

annotations.label: transcript_bound_by_nucleic_acid
annotations.definition: A transcript that is bound by a nucleic acid.
annotations.comment: Formerly called transcript_by_bound_nucleic_acid.

annotations.label: transcript_bound_by_protein
annotations.definition: A transcript that is bound by a protein.
annotations.comment: Formerly called transcript_by_bound_protein.

annotations.label: engineered_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is engineered.

annotations.label: engineered_foreign_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is engineered and foreign.

annotations.label: mRNA_with_minus_1_frameshift
annotations.definition: An mRNA with a minus 1 frameshift.

annotations.label: engineered_foreign_transposable_element_gene
annotations.definition: A transposable_element that is engineered and foreign.

annotations.label: foreign_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is foreign.

annotations.label: long_terminal_repeat
annotations.definition: A sequence directly repeated at both ends of a defined sequence, of the sort typically found in retroviruses.

annotations.label: fusion_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is a fusion.

annotations.label: engineered_fusion_gene
annotations.definition: A fusion gene that is engineered.

annotations.label: microsatellite
annotations.definition: A repeat_region containing repeat_units of 2 to 10 bp repeated in tandem.

annotations.label: dinucleotide_repeat_microsatellite_feature
annotations.definition: A region of a repeating dinucleotide sequence (two bases).

annotations.label: trinucleotide_repeat_microsatellite_feature
annotations.definition: A region of a repeating trinucleotide sequence (three bases).

annotations.label: engineered_foreign_repetitive_element
annotations.definition: A repetitive element that is engineered and foreign.

annotations.label: inverted_repeat
annotations.definition: The sequence is complementarily repeated on the opposite strand. It is a palindrome, and it may, or may not be hyphenated. Examples: GCTGATCAGC, or GCTGA-----TCAGC.

annotations.label: U12_intron
annotations.definition: A type of spliceosomal intron spliced by the U12 spliceosome, that includes U11, U12, U4atac/U6atac and U5 snRNAs.
annotations.comment: May have either GT-AC or AT-AC 5' and 3' boundaries.

annotations.label: origin_of_replication
annotations.definition: A region of nucleic acid from which replication initiates; includes sequences that are recognized by replication proteins, the site from which the first separation of complementary strands occurs, and specific replication start sites.

annotations.label: D_loop
annotations.definition: Displacement loop; a region within mitochondrial DNA in which a short stretch of RNA is paired with one strand of DNA, displacing the original partner DNA strand in this region; also used to describe the displacement of a region of one strand of duplex DNA by a single stranded invader in the reaction catalyzed by RecA protein.
annotations.comment: Moved from is_a: SO:0000296 origin_of_replication to is_a: SO:0001411 biological_region after Terrence Murphy (INSDC) pointed out that the D loop can also refer to a loop in DNA repair, which is not an origin of replication. See GitHub Issue #417 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/417)

annotations.label: recombination_feature
annotations.definition: A feature where there has been exchange of genetic material in the event of mitosis or meiosis

annotations.label: specific_recombination_site
annotations.definition: A location where recombination or occurs during mitosis or meiosis.

annotations.label: recombination_feature_of_rearranged_gene
annotations.definition: A location where a gene is rearranged due to recombination during mitosis or meiosis.

annotations.label: vertebrate_immune_system_gene_recombination_feature
annotations.definition: A feature where recombination has occurred for the purpose of generating a diversity in the immune system.

synonyms.text: vertebrate immune system gene recombination feature

annotations.label: J_gene_recombination_feature
annotations.definition: Recombination signal including J-heptamer, J-spacer and J-nonamer in 5' of J-region of a J-gene or J-sequence.

annotations.label: clip
annotations.definition: Part of the primary transcript that is clipped off during processing.

annotations.label: modified_DNA_base
annotations.definition: A modified nucleotide, i.e. a nucleotide other than A, T, C. G.
annotations.comment: Modified base:<modified_base>.

annotations.label: methylated_DNA_base_feature
annotations.definition: A nucleotide modified by methylation.

annotations.label: CpG_island
annotations.definition: Regions of a few hundred to a few thousand bases in vertebrate genomes that are relatively GC and CpG rich; they are typically unmethylated and often found near the 5' ends of genes.

annotations.label: experimentally_determined
annotations.definition: Attribute to describe a feature that has been experimentally verified.

annotations.label: stem_loop
annotations.definition: A double-helical region of nucleic acid formed by base-pairing between adjacent (inverted) complementary sequences.

annotations.label: direct_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat where the same sequence is repeated in the same direction. Example: GCTGA-followed by-GCTGA.

annotations.label: TSS
annotations.definition: The first base where RNA polymerase begins to synthesize the RNA transcript.
annotations.comment: Added relationship is_a SO:0002309 core_promoter_element with the creation of core_promoter_element as part of GREEKC initiative August 2020 - Dave Sant.

annotations.label: CDS
annotations.definition: A contiguous sequence which begins with, and includes, a start codon and ends with, and includes, a stop codon.

annotations.label: cDNA_clone
annotations.definition: Complementary DNA; A piece of DNA copied from an mRNA and spliced into a vector for propagation in a suitable host.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: start_codon
annotations.definition: First codon to be translated by a ribosome.

annotations.label: stop_codon
annotations.definition: In mRNA, a set of three nucleotides that indicates the end of information for protein synthesis. In genomic sequence, a stop codon may be only one or two nucleotides where the additional nucleotides are provided in the RNA via polyadenylation.

annotations.label: intronic_splice_enhancer
annotations.definition: Sequences within the intron that modulate splice site selection for some introns.

annotations.label: mRNA_with_plus_1_frameshift
annotations.definition: An mRNA with a plus 1 frameshift.

annotations.label: nuclease_hypersensitive_site
annotations.definition: A region of nucleotide sequence targeted by a nuclease enzyme that is found cleaved more than would be expected by chance.
annotations.comment: Relationship to accessible_DNA_region added 11 Feb 2021. GREEKC pointed out that this is an assay based term, but we need a biological term for the accessible DNA. See GitHub Issue #531.

annotations.label: coding_start
annotations.definition: The first base to be translated into protein.

annotations.label: tag
annotations.definition: A nucleotide sequence that may be used to identify a larger sequence.

annotations.label: rRNA_large_subunit_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a large ribosomal subunit RNA.

annotations.label: SAGE_tag
annotations.definition: A short diagnostic sequence tag, serial analysis of gene expression (SAGE), that allows the quantitative and simultaneous analysis of a large number of transcripts.

annotations.label: coding_end
annotations.definition: The last base to be translated into protein. It does not include the stop codon.

annotations.label: microarray_oligo
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence used experimentally to detect the presence or absence of a complementary nucleic acid.

annotations.label: mRNA_with_plus_2_frameshift
annotations.definition: An mRNA with a plus 2 frameshift.

annotations.label: conserved_region
annotations.definition: Region of sequence similarity by descent from a common ancestor.

annotations.label: STS
annotations.definition: Short (typically a few hundred base pairs) DNA sequence that has a single occurrence in a genome and whose location and base sequence are known.

annotations.label: coding_conserved_region
annotations.definition: Coding region of sequence similarity by descent from a common ancestor.

annotations.label: exon_junction
annotations.definition: The boundary between two exons in a processed transcript.

annotations.label: nc_conserved_region
annotations.definition: Non-coding region of sequence similarity by descent from a common ancestor.

annotations.label: mRNA_with_minus_2_frameshift
annotations.definition: A mRNA with a minus 2 frameshift.

annotations.label: pseudogene
annotations.definition: A sequence that closely resembles a known functional gene, at another locus within a genome, that is non-functional as a consequence of (usually several) mutations that prevent either its transcription or translation (or both). In general, pseudogenes result from either reverse transcription of a transcript of their "normal" paralog (SO:0000043) (in which case the pseudogene typically lacks introns and includes a poly(A) tail) or from recombination (SO:0000044) (in which case the pseudogene is typically a tandem duplication of its "normal" paralog).

annotations.label: RNAi_reagent
annotations.definition: A double stranded RNA duplex, at least 20bp long, used experimentally to inhibit gene function by RNA interference.

annotations.label: MITE
annotations.definition: A highly repetitive and short (100-500 base pair) transposable element with terminal inverted repeats (TIR) and target site duplication (TSD). MITEs do not encode proteins.

annotations.label: recombination_hotspot
annotations.definition: A region in a genome which promotes recombination.

annotations.label: chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a nucleic acid molecule which controls its own replication through the interaction of specific proteins at one or more origins of replication.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: chromosome_band
annotations.definition: A cytologically distinguishable feature of a chromosome, often made visible by staining, and usually alternating light and dark.

annotations.label: site_specific_recombination_target_region
annotations.definition: A region specifically recognised by a recombinase where recombination can occur during mitosis or meiosis.

annotations.label: match
annotations.definition: A region of sequence, aligned to another sequence with some statistical significance, using an algorithm such as BLAST or SIM4.

annotations.label: splice_enhancer
annotations.definition: Region of a transcript that regulates splicing.

annotations.label: EST
annotations.definition: A tag produced from a single sequencing read from a cDNA clone or PCR product; typically a few hundred base pairs long.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: loxP_site
annotations.definition: Cre-Recombination target sequence.

annotations.label: nucleotide_match
annotations.definition: A match against a nucleotide sequence.

annotations.label: nucleic_acid
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence consisting of nucleobases bound to repeating units. The forms found in nature are deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA), where the repeating units are 2-deoxy-D-ribose rings connected to a phosphate backbone, and ribonucleic acid (RNA), where the repeating units are D-ribose rings connected to a phosphate backbone.

annotations.label: protein_match
annotations.definition: A match against a protein sequence.

annotations.label: FRT_site
annotations.definition: An inversion site found on the Saccharomyces cerevisiae 2 micron plasmid.

annotations.label: synthetic_sequence
annotations.definition: An attribute to decide a sequence of nucleotides, nucleotide analogs, or amino acids that has been designed by an experimenter and which may, or may not, correspond with any natural sequence.

annotations.label: DNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence consisting of nucleobases bound to a repeating unit made of a 2-deoxy-D-ribose ring connected to a phosphate backbone.

annotations.label: sequence_assembly
annotations.definition: A sequence of nucleotides that has been algorithmically derived from an alignment of two or more different sequences.

annotations.label: group_1_intron_homing_endonuclease_target_region
annotations.definition: A region of intronic nucleotide sequence targeted by a nuclease enzyme.

synonyms.text: group 1 intron homing endonuclease target region

annotations.label: haplotype_block
annotations.definition: A region of the genome which is co-inherited as the result of the lack of historic recombination within it.

annotations.label: RNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence consisting of nucleobases bound to a repeating unit made of a D-ribose ring connected to a phosphate backbone.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: flanked
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a region that is bounded either side by a particular kind of region.

annotations.label: floxed
annotations.definition: An attribute describing sequence that is flanked by Lox-P sites.

annotations.label: codon
annotations.definition: A set of (usually) three nucleotide bases in a DNA or RNA sequence, which together code for a unique amino acid or the termination of translation and are contained within the CDS.

annotations.label: FRT_flanked
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe sequence that is flanked by the FLP recombinase recognition site, FRT.

annotations.label: invalidated_by_chimeric_cDNA
annotations.definition: A cDNA clone constructed from more than one mRNA. Usually an experimental artifact.

annotations.label: floxed_gene
annotations.definition: A transgene that is floxed.

annotations.label: transposable_element_flanking_region
annotations.definition: The region of sequence surrounding a transposable element.

annotations.label: integron
annotations.definition: A region encoding an integrase which acts at a site adjacent to it (attI_site) to insert DNA which must include but is not limited to an attC_site.

annotations.label: insertion_site
annotations.definition: The junction where an insertion occurred.

annotations.label: attI_site
annotations.definition: A region within an integron, adjacent to an integrase, at which site specific recombination involving an attC_site takes place.

annotations.label: transposable_element_insertion_site
annotations.definition: The junction in a genome where a transposable_element has inserted.

annotations.label: small_regulatory_ncRNA
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA less than 200 nucleotides long, usually with a specific secondary structure, that acts to regulate gene expression. These include short ncRNAs such as piRNA, miRNA and siRNAs (among others).

annotations.label: conjugative_transposon
annotations.definition: A transposon that encodes function required for conjugation.

annotations.label: enzymatic_RNA
annotations.definition: An RNA sequence that has catalytic activity with or without an associated ribonucleoprotein.
annotations.comment: This was moved to be a child of transcript (SO:0000673) because some enzymatic RNA regions are part of primary transcripts and some are part of processed transcripts. Moved under ncRNA on 18 Nov 2021. See GitHub Issue #533.

annotations.label: recombinationally_inverted_gene
annotations.definition: A recombinationally rearranged gene by inversion.

annotations.label: ribozyme
annotations.definition: An RNA with catalytic activity.

annotations.label: cytosolic_5_8S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 5.8S rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of cytosolic ribosomes in eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Dave Sant removed '5_8S rRNA is also found in archaea.' from definition due to lack of references mentioning this on 1 Feb 2021. See GitHub Issue #505. Renamed from rRNA_5_8S to cytosolic_5_8S_rRNA on 10 June 2021 with the restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Requested by EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: RNA_6S
annotations.definition: A small (184-nt in E. coli) RNA that forms a hairpin type structure. 6S RNA associates with RNA polymerase in a highly specific manner. 6S RNA represses expression from a sigma70-dependent promoter during stationary phase.

annotations.label: CsrB_RsmB_RNA
annotations.definition: An enterobacterial RNA that binds the CsrA protein. The CsrB RNAs contain a conserved motif CAGGXXG that is found in up to 18 copies and has been suggested to bind CsrA. The Csr regulatory system has a strong negative regulatory effect on glycogen biosynthesis, glyconeogenesis and glycogen catabolism and a positive regulatory effect on glycolysis. In other bacteria such as Erwinia caratovara the RsmA protein has been shown to regulate the production of virulence determinants, such extracellular enzymes. RsmA binds to RsmB regulatory RNA which is also a member of this family.

annotations.label: DsrA_RNA
annotations.definition: DsrA RNA regulates both transcription, by overcoming transcriptional silencing by the nucleoid-associated H-NS protein, and translation, by promoting efficient translation of the stress sigma factor, RpoS. These two activities of DsrA can be separated by mutation: the first of three stem-loops of the 85 nucleotide RNA is necessary for RpoS translation but not for anti-H-NS action, while the second stem-loop is essential for antisilencing and less critical for RpoS translation. The third stem-loop, which behaves as a transcription terminator, can be substituted by the trp transcription terminator without loss of either DsrA function. The sequence of the first stem-loop of DsrA is complementary with the upstream leader portion of RpoS messenger RNA, suggesting that pairing of DsrA with the RpoS message might be important for translational regulation.

annotations.label: GcvB_RNA
annotations.definition: A small untranslated RNA involved in expression of the dipeptide and oligopeptide transport systems in Escherichia coli.

annotations.label: hammerhead_ribozyme
annotations.definition: A small catalytic RNA motif that catalyzes self-cleavage reaction. Its name comes from its secondary structure which resembles a carpenter's hammer. The hammerhead ribozyme is involved in the replication of some viroid and some satellite RNAs.

annotations.label: group_IIA_intron
annotations.definition: A group II intron that recognizes IBS1/EBS1 and IBS2/EBS2 for the 5-prime exon and gamma/gamma-prime for the 3-prime exon.

annotations.label: group_IIB_intron
annotations.definition: A group II intron that recognizes IBS1/EBS1 and IBS2/EBS2 for the 5-prime exon and IBS3/EBS3 for the 3-prime exon.

annotations.label: MicF_RNA
annotations.definition: A non-translated 93 nt antisense RNA that binds its target ompF mRNA and regulates ompF expression by inhibiting translation and inducing degradation of the message.

annotations.label: OxyS_RNA
annotations.definition: A small untranslated RNA which is induced in response to oxidative stress in Escherichia coli. Acts as a global regulator to activate or repress the expression of as many as 40 genes, including the fhlA-encoded transcriptional activator and the rpoS-encoded sigma(s) subunit of RNA polymerase. OxyS is bound by the Hfq protein, that increases the OxyS RNA interaction with its target messages.

annotations.label: RNase_MRP_RNA
annotations.definition: The RNA molecule essential for the catalytic activity of RNase MRP, an enzymatically active ribonucleoprotein with two distinct roles in eukaryotes. In mitochondria it plays a direct role in the initiation of mitochondrial DNA replication. In the nucleus it is involved in precursor rRNA processing, where it cleaves the internal transcribed spacer 1 between 18S and 5.8S rRNAs.
annotations.comment: Moved under enzymatic_RNA on 18 Nov 2021. See GitHub Issue #533.

annotations.label: RNase_P_RNA
annotations.definition: The RNA component of Ribonuclease P (RNase P), a ubiquitous endoribonuclease, found in archaea, bacteria and eukarya as well as chloroplasts and mitochondria. Its best characterized activity is the generation of mature 5 prime ends of tRNAs by cleaving the 5 prime leader elements of precursor-tRNAs. Cellular RNase Ps are ribonucleoproteins. RNA from bacterial RNase Ps retains its catalytic activity in the absence of the protein subunit, i.e. it is a ribozyme. Isolated eukaryotic and archaeal RNase P RNA has not been shown to retain its catalytic function, but is still essential for the catalytic activity of the holoenzyme. Although the archaeal and eukaryotic holoenzymes have a much greater protein content than the bacterial ones, the RNA cores from all the three lineages are homologous. Helices corresponding to P1, P2, P3, P4, and P10/11 are common to all cellular RNase P RNAs. Yet, there is considerable sequence variation, particularly among the eukaryotic RNAs.
annotations.comment: Moved under enzymatic_RNA on 18 Nov 2021. See GitHub Issue #533.

annotations.label: RprA_RNA
annotations.definition: Translational regulation of the stationary phase sigma factor RpoS is mediated by the formation of a double-stranded RNA stem-loop structure in the upstream region of the rpoS messenger RNA, occluding the translation initiation site. Clones carrying rprA (RpoS regulator RNA) increased the translation of RpoS. The rprA gene encodes a 106 nucleotide regulatory RNA. As with DsrA Rfam:RF00014, RprA is predicted to form three stem-loops. Thus, at least two small RNAs, DsrA and RprA, participate in the positive regulation of RpoS translation. Unlike DsrA, RprA does not have an extensive region of complementarity to the RpoS leader, leaving its mechanism of action unclear. RprA is non-essential.

annotations.label: RRE_RNA
annotations.definition: The Rev response element (RRE) is encoded within the HIV-env gene. Rev is an essential regulatory protein of HIV that binds an internal loop of the RRE leading, encouraging further Rev-RRE binding. This RNP complex is critical for mRNA export and hence for expression of the HIV structural proteins.

annotations.label: spot_42_RNA
annotations.definition: A 109-nucleotide RNA of E. coli that seems to have a regulatory role on the galactose operon. Changes in Spot 42 levels are implicated in affecting DNA polymerase I levels.

annotations.label: telomerase_RNA
annotations.definition: The RNA component of telomerase, a reverse transcriptase that synthesizes telomeric DNA.

annotations.label: U1_snRNA
annotations.definition: U1 is a small nuclear RNA (snRNA) component of the spliceosome (involved in pre-mRNA splicing). Its 5' end forms complementary base pairs with the 5' splice junction, thus defining the 5' donor site of an intron. There are significant differences in sequence and secondary structure between metazoan and yeast U1 snRNAs, the latter being much longer (568 nucleotides as compared to 164 nucleotides in human). Nevertheless, secondary structure predictions suggest that all U1 snRNAs share a 'common core' consisting of helices I, II, the proximal region of III, and IV.

annotations.label: U2_snRNA
annotations.definition: U2 is a small nuclear RNA (snRNA) component of the spliceosome (involved in pre-mRNA splicing). Complementary binding between U2 snRNA (in an area lying towards the 5' end but 3' to hairpin I) and the branchpoint sequence (BPS) of the intron results in the bulging out of an unpaired adenine, on the BPS, which initiates a nucleophilic attack at the intronic 5' splice site, thus starting the first of two transesterification reactions that mediate splicing.

annotations.label: U4_snRNA
annotations.definition: U4 small nuclear RNA (U4 snRNA) is a component of the major U2-dependent spliceosome. It forms a duplex with U6, and with each splicing round, it is displaced from U6 (and the spliceosome) in an ATP-dependent manner, allowing U6 to refold and create the active site for splicing catalysis. A recycling process involving protein Prp24 re-anneals U4 and U6.

annotations.label: U4atac_snRNA
annotations.definition: An snRNA required for the splicing of the minor U12-dependent class of eukaryotic nuclear introns. It forms a base paired complex with U6atac_snRNA (SO:0000397).

annotations.label: U5_snRNA
annotations.definition: U5 RNA is a component of both types of known spliceosome. The precise function of this molecule is unknown, though it is known that the 5' loop is required for splice site selection and p220 binding, and that both the 3' stem-loop and the Sm site are important for Sm protein binding and cap methylation.

annotations.label: U6_snRNA
annotations.definition: U6 snRNA is a component of the spliceosome which is involved in splicing pre-mRNA. The putative secondary structure consensus base pairing is confined to a short 5' stem loop, but U6 snRNA is thought to form extensive base-pair interactions with U4 snRNA.

annotations.label: U6atac_snRNA
annotations.definition: U6atac_snRNA is an snRNA required for the splicing of the minor U12-dependent class of eukaryotic nuclear introns. It forms a base paired complex with U4atac_snRNA (SO:0000394).

annotations.label: U11_snRNA
annotations.definition: U11 snRNA plays a role in splicing of the minor U12-dependent class of eukaryotic nuclear introns, similar to U1 snRNA in the major class spliceosome it base pairs to the conserved 5' splice site sequence.

annotations.label: U12_snRNA
annotations.definition: The U12 small nuclear (snRNA), together with U4atac/U6atac, U5, and U11 snRNAs and associated proteins, forms a spliceosome that cleaves a divergent class of low-abundance pre-mRNA introns.

annotations.label: sequence_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute describes a quality of sequence.

annotations.label: gene_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a gene.

annotations.label: U14_snoRNA
annotations.definition: U14 small nucleolar RNA (U14 snoRNA) is required for early cleavages of eukaryotic precursor rRNAs. In yeasts, this molecule possess a stem-loop region (known as the Y-domain) which is essential for function. A similar structure, but with a different consensus sequence, is found in plants, but is absent in vertebrates.
annotations.comment: An evolutionarily conserved eukaryotic low molecular weight RNA capable of intermolecular hybridization with both homologous and heterologous 18S rRNA.

annotations.label: vault_RNA
annotations.definition: A family of RNAs are found as part of the enigmatic vault ribonucleoprotein complex. The complex consists of a major vault protein (MVP), two minor vault proteins (VPARP and TEP1), and several small untranslated RNA molecules. It has been suggested that the vault complex is involved in drug resistance.

annotations.label: Y_RNA
annotations.definition: Y RNAs are components of the Ro ribonucleoprotein particle (Ro RNP), in association with Ro60 and La proteins. The Y RNAs and Ro60 and La proteins are well conserved, but the function of the Ro RNP is not known. In humans the RNA component can be one of four small RNAs: hY1, hY3, hY4 and hY5. These small RNAs are predicted to fold into a conserved secondary structure containing three stem structures. The largest of the four, hY1, contains an additional hairpin.

annotations.label: twintron
annotations.definition: An intron within an intron. Twintrons are group II or III introns, into which another group II or III intron has been transposed.

annotations.label: cytosolic_18S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 18S rRNA is an RNA component of the small subunit of cytosolic ribosomes in eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Renamed to cytosolic_18S_rRNA from rRNA_18S on 10 June 2021 as per restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Request from EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: binding_site
annotations.definition: A biological_region of sequence that, in the molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with other molecules. A region on the surface of a molecule that may interact with another molecule. When applied to polypeptides: Amino acids involved in binding or interactions. It can also apply to an amino acid bond which is represented by the positions of the two flanking amino acids.
annotations.comment: See GO:0005488 : binding.

annotations.label: protein_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with polypeptide molecules.
annotations.comment: See GO:0042277 : peptide binding.

annotations.label: rescue_region
annotations.definition: A region that rescues.

annotations.label: restriction_fragment
annotations.definition: A region of polynucleotide sequence produced by digestion with a restriction endonuclease.

annotations.label: sequence_difference
annotations.definition: A region where the sequence differs from that of a specified sequence.

annotations.label: invalidated_by_genomic_contamination
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that is invalidated due to genomic contamination.

annotations.label: invalidated_by_genomic_polyA_primed_cDNA
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that is invalidated due to polyA priming.

synonyms.text: invalidated by genomic polyA primed cDNA

annotations.label: invalidated_by_partial_processing
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that is invalidated due to partial processing.

annotations.label: polypeptide_domain
annotations.definition: A structurally or functionally defined protein region. In proteins with multiple domains, the combination of the domains determines the function of the protein. A region which has been shown to recur throughout evolution.
annotations.comment: Range. Old definition from before biosapiens: A region of a single polypeptide chain that folds into an independent unit and exhibits biological activity. A polypeptide chain may have multiple domains.

annotations.label: signal_peptide
annotations.definition: The signal_peptide is a short region of the peptide located at the N-terminus that directs the protein to be secreted or part of membrane components.
annotations.comment: Old def before biosapiens:The sequence for an N-terminal domain of a secreted protein; this domain is involved in attaching nascent polypeptide to the membrane leader sequence.
