annotations.label: mature_protein_region
annotations.definition: The polypeptide sequence that remains when the cleaved peptide regions have been cleaved from the immature peptide.
annotations.comment: This term mature peptide, merged with the biosapiens term mature protein region and took that to be the new name. Old def: The coding sequence for the mature or final peptide or protein product following post-translational modification.

annotations.label: five_prime_terminal_inverted_repeat
annotations.definition: An inverted repeat (SO:0000294) occurring at the 5-prime termini of a DNA transposon.

annotations.label: three_prime_terminal_inverted_repeat
annotations.definition: An inverted repeat (SO:0000294) occurring at the 3-prime termini of a DNA transposon.

annotations.label: U5_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The U5 segment of the long terminal repeats.

annotations.label: R_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The R segment of the long terminal repeats.

annotations.label: U3_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The U3 segment of the long terminal repeats.

annotations.label: five_prime_LTR
annotations.definition: The long terminal repeat found at the five-prime end of the sequence to be inserted into the host genome.

annotations.label: three_prime_LTR
annotations.definition: The long terminal repeat found at the three-prime end of the sequence to be inserted into the host genome.

annotations.label: R_five_prime_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The R segment of the three-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: U5_five_prime_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The U5 segment of the three-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: U3_five_prime_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The U3 segment of the three-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: R_three_prime_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The R segment of the three-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: U3_three_prime_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The U3 segment of the three-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: U5_three_prime_LTR_region
annotations.definition: The U5 segment of the three-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: non_LTR_retrotransposon_polymeric_tract
annotations.definition: A polymeric tract, such as poly(dA), within a non_LTR_retrotransposon.

annotations.label: target_site_duplication
annotations.definition: A sequence of the target DNA that is duplicated when a transposable element or phage inserts; usually found at each end the insertion.

annotations.label: RR_tract
annotations.definition: A polypurine tract within an LTR_retrotransposon.

annotations.label: ARS
annotations.definition: A sequence that can autonomously replicate, as a plasmid, when transformed into a bacterial host.

annotations.label: inverted_ring_chromosome
annotations.definition: A ring chromosome is a chromosome whose arms have fused together to form a ring in an inverted fashion, often with the loss of the ends of the chromosome.

annotations.label: vector_replicon
annotations.definition: A replicon that has been modified to act as a vector for foreign sequence.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: ss_oligo
annotations.definition: A single stranded oligonucleotide.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: ds_oligo
annotations.definition: A double stranded oligonucleotide.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: polymer_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe the kind of biological sequence.

annotations.label: three_prime_noncoding_exon
annotations.definition: Non-coding exon in the 3' UTR.

annotations.label: five_prime_noncoding_exon
annotations.definition: Non-coding exon in the 5' UTR.

annotations.label: UTR_intron
annotations.definition: Intron located in the untranslated region.

annotations.label: five_prime_UTR_intron
annotations.definition: An intron located in the 5' UTR.

annotations.label: three_prime_UTR_intron
annotations.definition: An intron located in the 3' UTR.

annotations.label: random_sequence
annotations.definition: A sequence of nucleotides or amino acids which, by design, has a "random" order of components, given a predetermined input frequency of these components.

annotations.label: interband
annotations.definition: A light region between two darkly staining bands in a polytene chromosome.

annotations.label: gene_with_polyadenylated_mRNA
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a polyadenylated mRNA.

annotations.label: chromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variant whereby a region of a chromosome has been transferred to another position. Among interchromosomal rearrangements, the term transposition is reserved for that class in which the telomeres of the chromosomes involved are coupled (that is to say, form the two ends of a single DNA molecule) as in wild-type.

annotations.label: rasiRNA
annotations.definition: A 17-28-nt, small interfering RNA derived from transcripts of repetitive elements.
annotations.comment: Changed parent term from ncRNA (SO:0000655) to piRNA (SO:0001035). See GitHub Issue #573.

annotations.label: gene_with_mRNA_with_frameshift
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes an mRNA with a frameshift.

annotations.label: recombinationally_rearranged_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is recombinationally rearranged.

annotations.label: interchromosomal_duplication
annotations.definition: A chromosome duplication involving an insertion from another chromosome.

annotations.label: D_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Germline genomic DNA including D-region with 5' UTR and 3' UTR, also designated as D-segment.

annotations.label: gene_with_trans_spliced_transcript
annotations.definition: A gene with a transcript that is trans-spliced.

annotations.label: vertebrate_immunoglobulin_T_cell_receptor_segment
annotations.definition: Germline genomic DNA with the sequence for a V, D, C, or J portion of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor.
annotations.comment: I am using the term segment instead of gene here to avoid confusion with the region 'gene'.

synonyms.text: vertebrate immunoglobulin T cell receptor segment

annotations.label: inversion_derived_bipartite_deficiency
annotations.definition: A chromosomal deletion whereby a chromosome generated by recombination between two inversions; has a deficiency at each end of the inversion.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_region
annotations.definition: A non-functional descendant of a functional entity.

annotations.label: encodes_alternately_spliced_transcripts
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes more than one transcript.

annotations.label: decayed_exon
annotations.definition: A non-functional descendant of an exon.
annotations.comment: Does not have to be part of a pseudogene.

annotations.label: inversion_derived_deficiency_plus_duplication
annotations.definition: A chromosome deletion whereby a chromosome is generated by recombination between two inversions; there is a deficiency at one end of the inversion and a duplication at the other end of the inversion.

annotations.label: V_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Germline genomic DNA including L-part1, V-intron and V-exon, with the 5' UTR and 3' UTR.

annotations.label: post_translationally_regulated_by_protein_stability
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a gene sequence where the resulting protein is regulated by the stability of the resulting protein.

synonyms.text: post translationally regulated by protein stability

annotations.label: golden_path_fragment
annotations.definition: One of the pieces of sequence that make up a golden path.

annotations.label: post_translationally_regulated_by_protein_modification
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a gene sequence where the resulting protein is modified to regulate it.

synonyms.text: post translationally regulated by protein modification

annotations.label: J_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Germline genomic DNA of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene including J-region with 5' UTR (SO:0000204) and 3' UTR (SO:0000205), also designated as J-segment.

annotations.label: autoregulated
annotations.definition: The gene product is involved in its own transcriptional regulation.

annotations.label: tiling_path
annotations.definition: A set of regions which overlap with minimal polymorphism to form a linear sequence.

annotations.label: negatively_autoregulated
annotations.definition: The gene product is involved in its own transcriptional regulation where it decreases transcription.

annotations.label: tiling_path_fragment
annotations.definition: A piece of sequence that makes up a tiling_path (SO:0000472).

annotations.label: positively_autoregulated
annotations.definition: The gene product is involved in its own transcriptional regulation, where it increases transcription.

annotations.label: contig_read
annotations.definition: A DNA sequencer read which is part of a contig.

annotations.label: C_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene including C-region (and introns if present) with 5' UTR (SO:0000204) and 3' UTR (SO:0000205).

annotations.label: trans_spliced_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that is trans-spliced.

annotations.label: tiling_path_clone
annotations.definition: A clone which is part of a tiling path. A tiling path is a set of sequencing substrates, typically clones, which have been selected in order to efficiently cover a region of the genome in preparation for sequencing and assembly.

annotations.label: terminal_inverted_repeat
annotations.definition: An inverted repeat (SO:0000294) occurring at the termini of a DNA transposon.

annotations.label: vertebrate_immunoglobulin_T_cell_receptor_gene_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration.

synonyms.text: vertebrate immunoglobulin T cell receptor gene cluster

annotations.label: nc_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript that is never translated into a protein.

annotations.label: three_prime_coding_exon_noncoding_region
annotations.definition: The sequence of the 3' exon that is not coding.

synonyms.text: three prime coding exon noncoding region

annotations.label: DJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one DJ-gene, and one J-gene.

annotations.label: five_prime_coding_exon_noncoding_region
annotations.definition: The sequence of the 5' exon preceding the start codon.

synonyms.text: five prime coding exon noncoding region

annotations.label: VDJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one VDJ-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: VDJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one VDJ-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: VJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one VJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: VJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one VJ-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: VJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one VJ-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: D_gene_recombination_feature
annotations.definition: Recombination signal including D-heptamer, D-spacer and D-nonamer in 5' of D-region of a D-gene or D-sequence.

annotations.label: three_prime_D_heptamer
annotations.definition: 7 nucleotide recombination site like CACAGTG, part of a 3' D-recombination signal sequence of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: three_prime_D_nonamer
annotations.definition: A 9 nucleotide recombination site (e.g. ACAAAAACC), part of a 3' D-recombination signal sequence of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: three_prime_D_spacer
annotations.definition: A 12 or 23 nucleotide spacer between the 3'D-HEPTAMER and 3'D-NONAMER of a 3'D-RS.

annotations.label: five_prime_D_heptamer
annotations.definition: 7 nucleotide recombination site (e.g. CACTGTG), part of a 5' D-recombination signal sequence (SO:0000556) of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: five_prime_D_nonamer
annotations.definition: 9 nucleotide recombination site (e.g. GGTTTTTGT), part of a five_prime_D-recombination signal sequence (SO:0000556) of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: five_prime_D_spacer
annotations.definition: 12 or 23 nucleotide spacer between the 5' D-heptamer (SO:0000496) and 5' D-nonamer (SO:0000497) of a 5' D-recombination signal sequence (SO:0000556) of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: virtual_sequence
annotations.definition: A continuous piece of sequence similar to the 'virtual contig' concept of the Ensembl database.

annotations.label: Hoogsteen_base_pair
annotations.definition: A type of non-canonical base-pairing. This is less energetically favourable than watson crick base pairing. Hoogsteen GC base pairs only have two hydrogen bonds.

annotations.label: reverse_Hoogsteen_base_pair
annotations.definition: A type of non-canonical base-pairing.

annotations.label: D_DJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one D-gene, one DJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: D_DJ_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one D-gene and one DJ-gene.

annotations.label: D_DJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one D-gene, one DJ-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_exon
annotations.definition: A non functional descendant of an exon, part of a pseudogene.
annotations.comment: This is the analog of the exon of a functional gene. The term was requested by Rama - SGD to allow the annotation of the parts of a pseudogene. Non-functional is defined as either its transcription or translation (or both) are prevented due to one or more mutations.

annotations.label: D_DJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one D-gene, one DJ-gene, and one J-gene.

annotations.label: D_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including at least one D-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: VD_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in partially rearranged genomic DNA including L-part1, V-intron and V-D-exon, with the 5' UTR (SO:0000204) and 3' UTR (SO:0000205).

annotations.label: J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including at least one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: inversion_derived_deficiency_plus_aneuploid
annotations.definition: A chromosomal deletion whereby a chromosome generated by recombination between two inversions; has a deficiency at one end and presumed to have a deficiency or duplication at the other end of the inversion.

annotations.label: J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including more than one J-gene.

annotations.label: J_nonamer
annotations.definition: 9 nucleotide recombination site (e.g. GGTTTTTGT), part of a J-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: J_heptamer
annotations.definition: 7 nucleotide recombination site (e.g. CACAGTG), part of a J-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_transcript
annotations.definition: A non functional descendant of a transcript, part of a pseudogene.
annotations.comment: This is the analog of the transcript of a functional gene. The term was requested by Rama - SGD to allow the annotation of the parts of a pseudogene. Non-functional is defined as either its transcription or translation (or both) are prevented due to one or more mutations.

annotations.label: J_spacer
annotations.definition: 12 or 23 nucleotide spacer between the J-nonamer and the J-heptamer of a J-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: V_DJ_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene and one DJ-gene.

annotations.label: V_DJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one DJ-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: V_VDJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one VDJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_VDJ_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene and one VDJ-gene.

annotations.label: V_VDJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one VDJ-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: V_VJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one VJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_VJ_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene and one VJ-gene.

annotations.label: V_VJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one VJ-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: V_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including more than one V-gene.

annotations.label: V_D_DJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one D-gene, one DJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_D_DJ_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one D-gene, one DJ-gene.

annotations.label: V_D_DJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one D-gene, one DJ-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

synonyms.text: V D DJ J C cluster

annotations.label: V_D_DJ_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one D-gene, one DJ-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: V_D_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including at least one V-gene, one D-gene and one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_D_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including at least one V-gene, one D-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: V_heptamer
annotations.definition: 7 nucleotide recombination site (e.g. CACAGTG), part of V-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: V_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including at least one V-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: V_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including at least one V-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_nonamer
annotations.definition: 9 nucleotide recombination site (e.g. ACAAAAACC), part of V-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: V_spacer
annotations.definition: 12 or 23 nucleotide spacer between the V-heptamer and the V-nonamer of a V-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: V_gene_recombination_feature
annotations.definition: Recombination signal including V-heptamer, V-spacer and V-nonamer in 3' of V-region of a V-gene or V-sequence of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene.

annotations.label: DJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one DJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: DJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA in rearranged configuration including at least one D-J-GENE, one J-GENE and one C-GENE.

annotations.label: VDJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one VDJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_DJ_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one DJ-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: helitron
annotations.definition: A rolling circle transposon. Autonomous helitrons encode a 5'-to-3' DNA helicase and nuclease/ligase similar to those encoded by known rolling-circle replicons.

annotations.label: recoding_pseudoknot
annotations.definition: The pseudoknots involved in recoding are unique in that, as they play their role as a structure, they are immediately unfolded and their now linear sequence serves as a template for decoding.

annotations.label: designed_sequence
annotations.definition: An oligonucleotide sequence that was designed by an experimenter that may or may not correspond with any natural sequence.

annotations.label: inversion_derived_bipartite_duplication
annotations.definition: A chromosome generated by recombination between two inversions; there is a duplication at each end of the inversion.

annotations.label: gene_with_edited_transcript
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a transcript that is edited.

annotations.label: inversion_derived_duplication_plus_aneuploid
annotations.definition: A chromosome generated by recombination between two inversions; has a duplication at one end and presumed to have a deficiency or duplication at the other end of the inversion.

annotations.label: aneuploid_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome structural variation whereby either a chromosome exists in addition to the normal chromosome complement or is lacking.
annotations.comment: Examples are Nullo-4, Haplo-4 and triplo-4 in Drosophila.

annotations.label: polyA_signal_sequence
annotations.definition: The recognition sequence necessary for endonuclease cleavage of an RNA transcript that is followed by polyadenylation; consensus=AATAAA.
annotations.comment: Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: Shine_Dalgarno_sequence
annotations.definition: A region in the 5' UTR that pairs with the 16S rRNA during formation of the preinitiation complex.
annotations.comment: Not found in Eukaryotic sequence.

annotations.label: polyA_site
annotations.definition: The site on an RNA transcript to which will be added adenine residues by post-transcriptional polyadenylation. The boundary between the UTR and the polyA sequence.

annotations.label: five_prime_clip
annotations.definition: 5' most region of a precursor transcript that is clipped off during processing.

annotations.label: five_prime_D_recombination_signal_sequence
annotations.definition: Recombination signal of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene, including the 5' D-nonamer (SO:0000497), 5' D-spacer (SO:0000498), and 5' D-heptamer (SO:0000396) in 5' of the D-region of a D-gene, or in 5' of the D-region of DJ-gene.

synonyms.text: five prime D recombination signal sequence

annotations.label: three_prime_clip
annotations.definition: 3'-most region of a precursor transcript that is clipped off during processing.

annotations.label: C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene including more than one C-gene.

annotations.label: D_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including more than one D-gene.

annotations.label: D_J_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in germline configuration including at least one D-gene and one J-gene.

annotations.label: heptamer_of_recombination_feature_of_vertebrate_immune_system_gene
annotations.definition: Seven nucleotide recombination site (e.g. CACAGTG), part of V-gene, D-gene or J-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin or T-cell receptor gene.

synonyms.text: heptamer of recombination feature of vertebrate immune system gene

annotations.label: nonamer_of_recombination_feature_of_vertebrate_immune_system_gene
annotations.definition: Nine nucleotide recombination site, part of V-gene, D-gene or J-gene recombination feature of an immunoglobulin or T-cell receptor gene.

synonyms.text: nonamer of recombination feature of vertebrate immune system gene

annotations.label: vertebrate_immune_system_gene_recombination_spacer
annotations.definition: A 12 or 23 nucleotide spacer between two regions of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene that may be rearranged by recombinase.

synonyms.text: vertebrate immune system gene recombination spacer

annotations.label: V_DJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one DJ-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_VDJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one VDJ-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: V_VJ_J_C_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration including at least one V-gene, one VJ-gene, one J-gene and one C-gene.

annotations.label: inversion_derived_aneuploid_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome may be generated by recombination between two inversions; presumed to have a deficiency or duplication at each end of the inversion.

annotations.label: bidirectional_promoter
annotations.definition: A promoter that can allow for transcription in both directions.
annotations.comment: Definition updated in Aug 2020 by Dave Sant.

annotations.label: retrotransposed
annotations.definition: An attribute of a feature that occurred as the product of a reverse transcriptase mediated event.
annotations.comment: GO:0003964 RNA-directed DNA polymerase activity.

annotations.label: three_prime_D_recombination_signal_sequence
annotations.definition: Recombination signal of an immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene, including the 3' D-heptamer (SO:0000493), 3' D-spacer, and 3' D-nonamer (SO:0000494) in 3' of the D-region of a D-gene.

synonyms.text: three prime D recombination signal sequence

annotations.label: miRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a microRNA (miRNA).

annotations.label: DJ_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in partially rearranged genomic DNA including D-J-region with 5' UTR and 3' UTR, also designated as D-J-segment.

annotations.label: rRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a ribosomal RNA (rRNA).

annotations.label: VDJ_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Rearranged genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene including L-part1, V-intron and V-D-J-exon, with the 5'UTR (SO:0000204) and 3'UTR (SO:0000205).

annotations.label: scRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a small cytoplasmic RNA (scRNA).

annotations.label: VJ_gene_segment
annotations.definition: Rearranged genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene including L-part1, V-intron and V-J-exon, with the 5'UTR (SO:0000204) and 3'UTR (SO:0000205).

annotations.label: centromere
annotations.definition: A region of chromosome where the spindle fibers attach during mitosis and meiosis.

annotations.label: snoRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a small nucleolar RNA (snoRNA).

annotations.label: edited_transcript_feature
annotations.definition: A locatable feature on a transcript that is edited.

annotations.label: methylation_guide_snoRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a methylation guide small nucleolar RNA.

annotations.label: cap
annotations.definition: A structure consisting of a 7-methylguanosine in 5'-5' triphosphate linkage with the first nucleotide of an mRNA. It is added post-transcriptionally, and is not encoded in the DNA.

annotations.label: rRNA_cleavage_snoRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding an rRNA cleavage snoRNA.

annotations.label: pre_edited_region
annotations.definition: The region of a transcript that will be edited.

annotations.label: tmRNA
annotations.definition: A tmRNA liberates a mRNA from a stalled ribosome. To accomplish this part of the tmRNA is used as a reading frame that ends in a translation stop signal. The broken mRNA is replaced in the ribosome by the tmRNA and translation of the tmRNA leads to addition of a proteolysis tag to the incomplete protein enabling recognition by a protease. Recently a number of permuted tmRNAs genes have been found encoded in two parts. TmRNAs have been identified in eubacteria and some chloroplasts but are absent from archeal and Eukaryote nuclear genomes.

annotations.label: C_D_box_snoRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: snoRNA that is associated with guiding methylation of nucleotides. It contains two short conserved sequence motifs: C (RUGAUGA) near the 5-prime end and D (CUGA) near the 3-prime end.

annotations.label: tmRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a tmRNA (SO:0000584).

annotations.label: group_I_intron
annotations.definition: Group I catalytic introns are large self-splicing ribozymes. They catalyze their own excision from mRNA, tRNA and rRNA precursors in a wide range of organisms. The core secondary structure consists of 9 paired regions (P1-P9). These fold to essentially two domains, the P4-P6 domain (formed from the stacking of P5, P4, P6 and P6a helices) and the P3-P9 domain (formed from the P8, P3, P7 and P9 helices). Group I catalytic introns often have long ORFs inserted in loop regions.

annotations.label: autocatalytically_spliced_intron
annotations.definition: A self spliced intron.

annotations.label: SRP_RNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a signal recognition particle RNA.

annotations.label: SRP_RNA
annotations.definition: The signal recognition particle (SRP) is a universally conserved ribonucleoprotein. It is involved in the co-translational targeting of proteins to membranes. The eukaryotic SRP consists of a 300-nucleotide 7S RNA and six proteins: SRPs 72, 68, 54, 19, 14, and 9. Archaeal SRP consists of a 7S RNA and homologues of the eukaryotic SRP19 and SRP54 proteins. In most eubacteria, the SRP consists of a 4.5S RNA and the Ffh protein (a homologue of the eukaryotic SRP54 protein). Eukaryotic and archaeal 7S RNAs have very similar secondary structures, with eight helical elements. These fold into the Alu and S domains, separated by a long linker region. Eubacterial SRP is generally a simpler structure, with the M domain of Ffh bound to a region of the 4.5S RNA that corresponds to helix 8 of the eukaryotic and archaeal SRP S domain. Some Gram-positive bacteria (e.g. Bacillus subtilis), however, have a larger SRP RNA that also has an Alu domain. The Alu domain is thought to mediate the peptide chain elongation retardation function of the SRP. The universally conserved helix which interacts with the SRP54/Ffh M domain mediates signal sequence recognition. In eukaryotes and archaea, the SRP19-helix 6 complex is thought to be involved in SRP assembly and stabilizes helix 8 for SRP54 binding.

annotations.label: pseudoknot
annotations.definition: A tertiary structure in RNA where nucleotides in a loop form base pairs with a region of RNA downstream of the loop.

annotations.label: H_pseudoknot
annotations.definition: A pseudoknot which contains two stems and at least two loops.

annotations.label: C_D_box_snoRNA
annotations.definition: Most box C/D snoRNAs also contain long (>10 nt) sequences complementary to rRNA. Boxes C and D, as well as boxes C' and D', are usually located in close proximity, and form a structure known as the box C/D motif. This motif is important for snoRNA stability, processing, nucleolar targeting and function. A small number of box C/D snoRNAs are involved in rRNA processing; most, however, are known or predicted to serve as guide RNAs in ribose methylation of rRNA. Targeting involves direct base pairing of the snoRNA at the rRNA site to be modified and selection of a rRNA nucleotide a fixed distance from box D or D'.
annotations.comment: Added 'SNORD' as a synonym of C_D_box_snoRNA (SO:0000593) and 'SNORA' as a synonym of H_ACA_box_snoRNA (SO:0000594). See GitHub Issue #577.

annotations.label: H_ACA_box_snoRNA
annotations.definition: Members of the box H/ACA family contain an ACA triplet, exactly 3 nt upstream from the 3' end and an H-box in a hinge region that links two structurally similar functional domains of the molecule. Both boxes are important for snoRNA biosynthesis and function. A few box H/ACA snoRNAs are involved in rRNA processing; most others are known or predicted to participate in selection of uridine nucleosides in rRNA to be converted to pseudouridines. Site selection is mediated by direct base pairing of the snoRNA with rRNA through one or both targeting domains.
annotations.comment: Added 'SNORD' as a synonym of C_D_box_snoRNA (SO:0000593) and 'SNORA' as a synonym of H_ACA_box_snoRNA (SO:0000594). See GitHub Issue #577.

annotations.label: C_D_box_snoRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a small nucleolar RNA of the box C/D family.

annotations.label: H_ACA_box_snoRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a small nucleolar RNA of the box H/ACA family.

synonyms.text: H ACA box snoRNA primary transcript

annotations.label: guide_RNA
annotations.definition: A short 3'-uridylated RNA that can form a duplex (except for its post-transcriptionally added oligo_U tail (SO:0000609)) with a stretch of mature edited mRNA.

annotations.label: group_II_intron
annotations.definition: Group II introns are found in rRNA, tRNA and mRNA of organelles in fungi, plants and protists, and also in mRNA in bacteria. They are large self-splicing ribozymes and have 6 structural domains (usually designated dI to dVI). A subset of group II introns also encode essential splicing proteins in intronic ORFs. The length of these introns can therefore be up to 3kb. Splicing occurs in almost identical fashion to nuclear pre-mRNA splicing with two transesterification steps. The 2' hydroxyl of a bulged adenosine in domain VI attacks the 5' splice site, followed by nucleophilic attack on the 3' splice site by the 3' OH of the upstream exon. Protein machinery is required for splicing in vivo, and long range intron to intron and intron-exon interactions are important for splice site positioning. Group II introns are further sub-classified into groups IIA and IIB which differ in splice site consensus, distance of bulged A from 3' splice site, some tertiary interactions, and intronic ORF phylogeny.

annotations.label: editing_block
annotations.definition: Edited mRNA sequence mediated by a single guide RNA (SO:0000602).

annotations.label: intergenic_region
annotations.definition: A region containing or overlapping no genes that is bounded on either side by a gene, or bounded by a gene and the end of the chromosome.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: editing_domain
annotations.definition: Edited mRNA sequence mediated by two or more overlapping guide RNAs (SO:0000602).

annotations.label: unedited_region
annotations.definition: The region of an edited transcript that will not be edited.

annotations.label: H_ACA_box_snoRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: snoRNA that is associated with guiding polyuridylation. It contains two short conserved sequence motifs: H box (ANANNA) and ACA (ACA).

annotations.label: oligo_U_tail
annotations.definition: The string of non-encoded U's at the 3' end of a guide RNA (SO:0000602).

annotations.label: polyA_sequence
annotations.definition: Sequence of about 100 nucleotides of A added to the 3' end of most eukaryotic mRNAs.

annotations.label: branch_site
annotations.definition: A pyrimidine rich sequence near the 3' end of an intron to which the 5'end becomes covalently bound during nuclear splicing. The resulting structure resembles a lariat.

annotations.label: polypyrimidine_tract
annotations.definition: The polypyrimidine tract is one of the cis-acting sequence elements directing intron removal in pre-mRNA splicing.

annotations.label: bacterial_RNApol_promoter
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence to which bacterial RNA polymerase binds, to begin transcription.
annotations.comment: former parent RNA_polymerase_promoter SO:0001203 was merged with promoter SO:0000167 in Aug 2020 as part of GREEKC.

annotations.label: bacterial_terminator
annotations.definition: A terminator signal for bacterial transcription.
annotations.comment: Moved to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) from gene_group_regulatory_region (SO:0000752) on 11 Feb 2021 when SO:0000752 was merged into SO:0001055. See GitHub Issue #529.

annotations.label: terminator_of_type_2_RNApol_III_promoter
annotations.definition: A terminator signal for RNA polymerase III transcription.

synonyms.text: terminator of type 2 RNApol III promoter

annotations.label: transcription_end_site
annotations.definition: The base where transcription ends.

annotations.label: RNApol_III_promoter_type_1
annotations.definition: This type of promoter recruits RNA pol III. This promoter is intragenic and includes an A box, an intermediate element, and a C box. This is well conserved in the 5s rRNA promoters across species.

annotations.label: RNApol_III_promoter_type_2
annotations.definition: This type of promoter recruits RNA pol III to transcribe genes mainly for t-RNA. This promoter is intragenic and includes an A box and a B box.

annotations.label: A_box
annotations.definition: A variably distant linear promoter region recognized by TFIIIC, with consensus sequence TGGCnnAGTGG.
annotations.comment: Binds TFIIIC.

annotations.label: B_box
annotations.definition: A variably distant linear promoter region recognized by TFIIIC, with consensus sequence AGGTTCCAnnCC.
annotations.comment: Binds TFIIIC.

annotations.label: RNApol_III_promoter_type_3
annotations.definition: This type of promoter recruits RNA pol III to transcribe predominantly noncoding RNAs. This promoter contains a proximal sequence element (PSE) and a TATA box upstream of the gene that it regulates. Transcription can also be activated by a distal sequence element (DSE), which is located further upstream.

annotations.label: C_box
annotations.definition: An RNA polymerase III type 1 promoter with consensus sequence CAnnCCn.

annotations.label: snRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a small nuclear RNA (snRNA).

annotations.label: telomere
annotations.definition: A specific structure at the end of a linear chromosome, required for the integrity and maintenance of the end.

annotations.label: silencer
annotations.definition: A regulatory region which upon binding of transcription factors, suppress the transcription of the gene or genes they control.

annotations.label: chromosomal_regulatory_element
annotations.definition: Regions of the chromosome that are important for regulating binding of chromosomes to the nuclear matrix.

annotations.label: insulator
annotations.definition: A regulatory region that 1) when located between a CRM and a gene's promoter prevents the CRM from modulating that genes expression and 2) acts as a chromatin boundary element or barrier that can block the encroachment of condensed chromatin from an adjacent region.
annotations.comment: moved from is_a: SO:0001055 transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region as per request from GREEKC initiative in August 2020.

annotations.label: chromosomal_structural_element
annotations.definition: Regions of the chromosome that are important for structural elements.

annotations.label: five_prime_open_reading_frame
annotations.definition: An open reading frame found within the 5' UTR that can be translated and stall the translation of the downstream open reading frame.

annotations.label: upstream_AUG_codon
annotations.definition: A start codon upstream of the ORF.

annotations.label: polycistronic_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding for more than one gene product.

annotations.label: monocistronic_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding for one gene product.

annotations.label: monocistronic_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA with either a single protein product, or for which the regions encoding all its protein products overlap.

annotations.label: polycistronic_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA that encodes multiple proteins from at least two non-overlapping regions.

annotations.label: mini_exon_donor_RNA
annotations.definition: A primary transcript that donates the spliced leader to other mRNA.

annotations.label: spliced_leader_RNA
annotations.definition: Snall nuclear RNAs that are incorporated into the pre-mRNAs to replace the 5' end in some eukaryotes.

annotations.label: engineered_plasmid
annotations.definition: A plasmid that is engineered.

annotations.label: transcribed_spacer_region
annotations.definition: Part of an rRNA transcription unit that is transcribed but discarded during maturation, not giving rise to any part of rRNA.

annotations.label: internal_transcribed_spacer_region
annotations.definition: Non-coding regions of DNA sequence that separate genes coding for the 28S, 5.8S, and 18S ribosomal RNAs.

annotations.label: external_transcribed_spacer_region
annotations.definition: Non-coding regions of DNA that precede the sequence that codes for the ribosomal RNA.

annotations.label: tetranucleotide_repeat_microsatellite_feature
annotations.definition: A region of a repeating tetranucleotide sequence (four bases).

annotations.label: SRP_RNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a signal recognition particle RNA (SRP RNA).

annotations.label: minisatellite
annotations.definition: A repeat region containing tandemly repeated sequences having a unit length of 10 to 40 bp.

annotations.label: antisense_RNA
annotations.definition: Antisense RNA is RNA that is transcribed from the coding, rather than the template, strand of DNA. It is therefore complementary to mRNA.

annotations.label: antisense_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: The reverse complement of the primary transcript.

annotations.label: siRNA
annotations.definition: A small RNA molecule that is the product of a longer exogenous or endogenous dsRNA, which is either a bimolecular duplex or very long hairpin, processed (via the Dicer pathway) such that numerous siRNAs accumulate from both strands of the dsRNA. siRNAs trigger the cleavage of their target molecules.

annotations.label: miRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a micro RNA.

annotations.label: cytosolic_SSU_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic SSU rRNA is an RNA component of the small subunit of cytosolic ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Renamed to cytosolic_SSU_rRNA from small_subunit_rRNA on 10 June 2021 as per restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Request from EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: cytosolic_LSU_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic LSU rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of cytosolic ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Renamed to cytosolic_LSU_rRNA from large_subunit_rRNA on 10 June 2021 as per restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Request from EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: cytosolic_5S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 5S rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of cytosolic ribosomes in both prokaryotes and eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Renamed from rRNA_5S to cytosolic_5S_rRNA on 27 May 2021 with the restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Requested by EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: cytosolic_28S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 28S rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of cytosolic ribosomes in metazoan eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Renamed from rRNA_28S to cytosolic_28S_rRNA on 27 May 2021 with the restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Requested by EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: maxicircle_gene
annotations.definition: A mitochondrial gene located in a maxicircle.

annotations.label: ncRNA
annotations.definition: An RNA transcript that does not encode for a protein rather the RNA molecule is the gene product.
annotations.comment: A ncRNA is a processed_transcript, so it may not contain parts such as transcribed_spacer_regions that are removed in the act of processing. For the corresponding primary_transcripts, please see term SO:0000483 nc_primary_transcript.

annotations.label: stRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a small temporal RNA (stRNA). Found in roundworm development.

annotations.label: repeat_region
annotations.definition: A region of sequence containing one or more repeat units.

annotations.label: dispersed_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat that is located at dispersed sites in the genome.

annotations.label: tmRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a transfer-messenger RNA (tmRNA).

annotations.label: spliceosomal_intron
annotations.definition: An intron which is spliced by the spliceosome.

annotations.label: tRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A region that can be transcribed into a transfer RNA (tRNA).

annotations.label: introgressed_chromosome_region
annotations.definition: A region of a chromosome that has been introduced by backcrossing with a separate species.

annotations.label: monocistronic_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that is monocistronic.

annotations.label: mobile_intron
annotations.definition: An intron (mitochondrial, chloroplast, nuclear or prokaryotic) that encodes a double strand sequence specific endonuclease allowing for mobility.

annotations.label: insertion
annotations.definition: The sequence of one or more nucleotides added between two adjacent nucleotides in the sequence.

annotations.label: EST_match
annotations.definition: A match against an EST sequence.

annotations.label: sequence_rearrangement_feature
annotations.definition: A feature where a segment of DNA has been rearranged from what it was in the parent cell.

annotations.label: chromosome_breakage_sequence
annotations.definition: A sequence within the micronuclear DNA of ciliates at which chromosome breakage and telomere addition occurs during nuclear differentiation.

annotations.label: internal_eliminated_sequence
annotations.definition: A sequence eliminated from the genome of ciliates during nuclear differentiation.

annotations.label: macronucleus_destined_segment
annotations.definition: A sequence that is conserved, although rearranged relative to the micronucleus, in the macronucleus of a ciliate genome.

annotations.label: transcript
annotations.definition: An RNA synthesized on a DNA or RNA template by an RNA polymerase.
annotations.comment: Added relationship overlaps SO:0002300 unit_of_gene_expression with Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585 Aug 5, 2020.

annotations.label: canonical_three_prime_splice_site
annotations.definition: The canonical 3' splice site has the sequence "AG".

annotations.label: canonical_five_prime_splice_site
annotations.definition: The canonical 5' splice site has the sequence "GT".

annotations.label: non_canonical_three_prime_splice_site
annotations.definition: A 3' splice site that does not have the sequence "AG".

synonyms.text: non canonical three prime splice site

annotations.label: non_canonical_five_prime_splice_site
annotations.definition: A 5' splice site which does not have the sequence "GT".

synonyms.text: non canonical five prime splice site

annotations.label: non_canonical_start_codon
annotations.definition: A start codon that is not the usual AUG sequence.

annotations.label: aberrant_processed_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that has been processed "incorrectly", for example by the failure of splicing of one or more exons.

annotations.label: exonic_splice_enhancer
annotations.definition: Exonic splicing enhancers (ESEs) facilitate exon definition by assisting in the recruitment of splicing factors to the adjacent intron.

annotations.label: nuclease_sensitive_site
annotations.definition: A region of nucleotide sequence targeted by a nuclease enzyme.

annotations.label: DNaseI_hypersensitive_site
annotations.definition: DNA region representing open chromatin structure that is hypersensitive to digestion by DNase I.

annotations.label: translocation_element
annotations.definition: A chromosomal translocation whereby the chromosomes carrying non-homologous centromeres may be recovered independently. These chromosomes are described as translocation elements. This occurs for some translocations, particularly but not exclusively, reciprocal translocations.

annotations.label: deletion_junction
annotations.definition: The space between two bases in a sequence which marks the position where a deletion has occurred.

annotations.label: golden_path
annotations.definition: A set of subregions selected from sequence contigs which when concatenated form a nonredundant linear sequence.

annotations.label: cDNA_match
annotations.definition: A match against cDNA sequence.

annotations.label: gene_with_polycistronic_transcript
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a polycistronic transcript.

annotations.label: cleaved_initiator_methionine
annotations.definition: The initiator methionine that has been cleaved from a mature polypeptide sequence.

annotations.label: gene_with_dicistronic_transcript
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a dicistronic transcript.

annotations.label: gene_with_recoded_mRNA
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes an mRNA that is recoded.

annotations.label: SNP
annotations.definition: SNPs are single base pair positions in genomic DNA at which different sequence alternatives exist in normal individuals in some population(s), wherein the least frequent variant has an abundance of 1% or greater.

annotations.label: reagent
annotations.definition: A sequence used in experiment.
annotations.comment: Requested by Lynn Crosby, jan 2006.

annotations.label: oligo
annotations.definition: A short oligonucleotide sequence, of length on the order of 10's of bases; either single or double stranded.

annotations.label: gene_with_stop_codon_read_through
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a transcript with stop codon readthrough.

synonyms.text: gene with stop codon read through

annotations.label: gene_with_stop_codon_redefined_as_pyrrolysine
annotations.definition: A gene encoding an mRNA that has the stop codon redefined as pyrrolysine.

synonyms.text: gene with stop codon redefined as pyrrolysine

annotations.label: junction
annotations.definition: A sequence_feature with an extent of zero.
annotations.comment: A junction is a boundary between regions. A boundary has an extent of zero.

annotations.label: remark
annotations.definition: A comment about the sequence.

annotations.label: possible_base_call_error
annotations.definition: A region of sequence where the validity of the base calling is questionable.

annotations.label: possible_assembly_error
annotations.definition: A region of sequence where there may have been an error in the assembly.

annotations.label: experimental_result_region
annotations.definition: A region of sequence implicated in an experimental result.

annotations.label: gene
annotations.definition: A region (or regions) that includes all of the sequence elements necessary to encode a functional transcript. A gene may include regulatory regions, transcribed regions and/or other functional sequence regions.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology. A gene may be considered as a unit of inheritance.

annotations.label: tandem_repeat
annotations.definition: Two or more adjacent copies of a region (of length greater than 1).

annotations.label: trans_splice_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: The 3' splice site of the acceptor primary transcript.
annotations.comment: This region contains a polypyridine tract and AG dinucleotide in some organisms and is UUUCAG in C. elegans.

annotations.label: trans_splice_donor_site
annotations.definition: The 5' five prime splice site region of the donor RNA.
annotations.comment: SL RNA contains a donor site.

annotations.label: SL1_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A trans_splicing_acceptor_site which appends the 22nt SL1 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of most mRNAs.

annotations.label: SL2_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A trans_splicing_acceptor_site which appends the 22nt SL2 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of mRNAs. SL2 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: gene_with_stop_codon_redefined_as_selenocysteine
annotations.definition: A gene encoding an mRNA that has the stop codon redefined as selenocysteine.

synonyms.text: gene with stop codon redefined as selenocysteine

annotations.label: gene_with_mRNA_recoded_by_translational_bypass
annotations.definition: A gene with mRNA recoded by translational bypass.

synonyms.text: gene with mRNA recoded by translational bypass

annotations.label: gene_with_transcript_with_translational_frameshift
annotations.definition: A gene encoding a transcript that has a translational frameshift.

synonyms.text: gene with transcript with translational frameshift

annotations.label: DNA_motif
annotations.definition: A motif that is active in the DNA form of the sequence.

annotations.label: nucleotide_motif
annotations.definition: A region of nucleotide sequence corresponding to a known motif.

annotations.label: RNA_motif
annotations.definition: A motif that is active in RNA sequence.

annotations.label: dicistronic_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA that has the quality dicistronic.

annotations.label: reading_frame
annotations.definition: A nucleic acid sequence that when read as sequential triplets, has the potential of encoding a sequential string of amino acids. It need not contain the start or stop codon.
annotations.comment: This term was added after a request by SGD. August 2004. Modified after SO meeting in Cambridge to not include start or stop.

annotations.label: blocked_reading_frame
annotations.definition: A reading_frame that is interrupted by one or more stop codons; usually identified through inter-genomic sequence comparisons.
annotations.comment: Term requested by Rama from SGD.

annotations.label: ultracontig
annotations.definition: An ordered and oriented set of scaffolds based on somewhat weaker sets of inferential evidence such as one set of mate pair reads together with supporting evidence from ESTs or location of markers from SNP or microsatellite maps, or cytogenetic localization of contained markers.

annotations.label: foreign_transposable_element
annotations.definition: A transposable element that is foreign.
annotations.comment: requested by Michael on 19 Nov 2004.

annotations.label: gene_with_dicistronic_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a dicistronic primary transcript.
annotations.comment: Requested by Michael, 19 nov 2004.

annotations.label: gene_with_dicistronic_mRNA
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a polycistronic mRNA.
annotations.comment: Requested by MA nov 19 2004.

annotations.label: iDNA
annotations.definition: Genomic sequence removed from the genome, as a normal event, by a process of recombination.

annotations.label: oriT
annotations.definition: A region of a DNA molecule where transfer is initiated during the process of conjugation or mobilization.

annotations.label: transit_peptide
annotations.definition: The transit_peptide is a short region at the N-terminus of the peptide that directs the protein to an organelle (chloroplast, mitochondrion, microbody or cyanelle).
annotations.comment: Added to bring SO inline with the EMBL, DDBJ, GenBank feature table. Old definition before biosapiens: The coding sequence for an N-terminal domain of a nuclear-encoded organellar protein. This domain is involved in post translational import of the protein into the organelle.

annotations.label: repeat_unit
annotations.definition: The simplest repeated component of a repeat region. A single repeat.
annotations.comment: Added to comply with the feature table. A single repeat.

annotations.label: cis_regulatory_module
annotations.definition: A regulatory region where transcription factor binding sites are clustered to regulate various aspects of transcription activities. (CRMs can be located a few kb to hundreds of kb upstream of the core promoter, in the coding sequence, within introns, or in the untranslated regions (UTR) sequences, and even on a different chromosome). A single gene can be regulated by multiple CRMs to give precise control of its spatial and temporal expression. CRMs function as nodes in large, intertwined regulatory network. CRM DNA accessibility is subject to regulation by dbTFs and transcription co-TFs.
annotations.comment: Requested by Stephen Grossmann Dec 2004. Changed relationship from has_part SO:0000235 TF_binding site to TF_binding_site is part_of SO:0000727 CRM in response to requests from GREEKC initiative in Aug 2020. Removed 3' from definition because 5' UTRs are included as well, notified by Colin Logie of GREEKC. Nov 9 2020. DS Updated name from 'CRM' to 'cis_regulatory_module' on 08 Feb 2021. See GitHub Issue #526. DS Added final sentence to definition as part of GREEKC Feb 16, 2021. See GitHub Issue #534.

annotations.label: intein
annotations.definition: A region of a peptide that is able to excise itself and rejoin the remaining portions with a peptide bond.
annotations.comment: Intein-mediated protein splicing occurs after mRNA has been translated into a protein.

annotations.label: intein_containing
annotations.definition: An attribute of protein-coding genes where the initial protein product contains an intein.

annotations.label: gap
annotations.definition: A gap in the sequence of known length. The unknown bases are filled in with N's.

annotations.label: fragmentary
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that is incomplete.
annotations.comment: Term added because of request by MO people.

annotations.label: predicted
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an unverified region.

annotations.label: feature_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a located_sequence_feature.

annotations.label: exemplar_mRNA
annotations.definition: An exemplar is a representative cDNA sequence for each gene. The exemplar approach is a method that usually involves some initial clustering into gene groups and the subsequent selection of a representative from each gene group.
annotations.comment: Added for the MO people.

annotations.label: sequence_location
annotations.definition: The location of a sequence.

annotations.label: organelle_sequence
annotations.definition: A sequence of DNA that originates from a an organelle.

annotations.label: mitochondrial_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a mitochondria.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: nuclear_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the nuclear genome of cell.
annotations.comment: Moved from is_a SO:0000736 (organelle_sequence) when brought to our attention by GitHub issue #489.

annotations.label: nucleomorphic_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a plastid such as a chloroplast. The nucleomorph is the nuclei of the plastic.

annotations.label: plastid_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a plastid such as a chloroplast.

annotations.label: kinetoplast
annotations.definition: A kinetoplast is an interlocked network of thousands of minicircles and tens of maxicircles, located near the base of the flagellum of some protozoan species.

annotations.label: maxicircle
annotations.definition: A maxicircle is a replicon, part of a kinetoplast, that contains open reading frames and replicates via a rolling circle method.

annotations.label: apicoplast_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of an apicoplast, a non-photosynthetic plastid.

annotations.label: chromoplast_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a chromoplast, a colored plastid for synthesis and storage of pigments.

annotations.label: chloroplast_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a chloroplast, a green plastid for photosynthesis.

annotations.label: cyanelle_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a cyanelle, a photosynthetic plastid found in algae.

annotations.label: leucoplast_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a leucoplast, a colorless plastid generally containing starch or oil.

annotations.label: proplastid_sequence
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a proplastid such as an immature chloroplast.

annotations.label: plasmid_location
annotations.definition: The location of DNA that has come from a plasmid sequence.

annotations.label: amplification_origin
annotations.definition: An origin_of_replication that is used for the amplification of a chromosomal nucleic acid sequence.

annotations.label: proviral_location
annotations.definition: The location of DNA that has come from a viral origin.

annotations.label: clone_insert
annotations.definition: The region of sequence that has been inserted and is being propagated by the clone.

annotations.label: lambda_vector
annotations.definition: The lambda bacteriophage is the vector for the linear lambda clone. The genes involved in the lysogenic pathway are removed from the from the viral DNA. Up to 25 kb of foreign DNA can then be inserted into the lambda genome.

annotations.label: plasmid_vector
annotations.definition: A plasmid that has been generated to act as a vector for foreign sequence.

annotations.label: cDNA
annotations.definition: DNA synthesized by reverse transcriptase using RNA as a template.

annotations.label: single_stranded_cDNA
annotations.definition: DNA synthesized from RNA by reverse transcriptase, single stranded.

annotations.label: double_stranded_cDNA
annotations.definition: DNA synthesized from RNA by reverse transcriptase that has been copied by PCR to make it double stranded.

annotations.label: pyrrolysyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a pyrrolysine anticodon, and a 3' pyrrolysine binding region.

annotations.label: episome
annotations.definition: A plasmid that may integrate with a chromosome.

annotations.label: tmRNA_coding_piece
annotations.definition: The region of a two-piece tmRNA that bears the reading frame encoding the proteolysis tag. The tmRNA gene undergoes circular permutation in some groups of bacteria. Processing of the transcripts from such a gene leaves the mature tmRNA in two pieces, base-paired together.
annotations.comment: Added in response to comment from Kelly Williams from Indiana. Nov 2005.

annotations.label: tmRNA_acceptor_piece
annotations.definition: The acceptor region of a two-piece tmRNA that when mature is charged at its 3' end with alanine. The tmRNA gene undergoes circular permutation in some groups of bacteria; processing of the transcripts from such a gene leaves the mature tmRNA in two pieces, base-paired together.
annotations.comment: Added in response to Kelly Williams from Indiana. Date: Nov 2005.

annotations.label: QTL
annotations.definition: A quantitative trait locus (QTL) is a polymorphic locus which contains alleles that differentially affect the expression of a continuously distributed phenotypic trait. Usually it is a marker described by statistical association to quantitative variation in the particular phenotypic trait that is thought to be controlled by the cumulative action of alleles at multiple loci.
annotations.comment: Added in respose to request by Simon Twigger November 14th 2005.

annotations.label: genomic_island
annotations.definition: A genomic island is an integrated mobile genetic element, characterized by size (over 10 Kb). It that has features that suggest a foreign origin. These can include nucleotide distribution (oligonucleotides signature, CG content etc.) that differs from the bulk of the chromosome and/or genes suggesting DNA mobility.
annotations.comment: Genomic islands are transmissible elements characterized by large size (>10kb).

annotations.label: pathogenic_island
annotations.definition: Mobile genetic elements that contribute to rapid changes in virulence potential. They are present on the genomes of pathogenic strains but absent from the genomes of non pathogenic members of the same or related species.
annotations.comment: Nature Reviews Microbiology 2, 414-424 (2004); doi:10.1038 micro 884 GENOMIC ISLANDS IN PATHOGENIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL MICROORGANISMS Ulrich Dobrindt, Bianca Hochhut, Ute Hentschel & Jorg Hacker.

annotations.label: metabolic_island
annotations.definition: A transmissible element containing genes involved in metabolism, analogous to the pathogenicity islands of gram negative bacteria.
annotations.comment: Genes for phenolic compound degradation in Pseudomonas putida are found on metabolic islands.

annotations.label: adaptive_island
annotations.definition: An adaptive island is a genomic island that provides an adaptive advantage to the host.
annotations.comment: The iron-uptake ability of many pathogens are conveyed by adaptive islands. Nature Reviews Microbiology 2, 414-424 (2004); doi:10.1038 micro 884 GENOMIC ISLANDS IN PATHOGENIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL MICROORGANISMS Ulrich Dobrindt, Bianca Hochhut, Ute Hentschel & Jorg Hacker.

annotations.label: symbiosis_island
annotations.definition: A transmissible element containing genes involved in symbiosis, analogous to the pathogenicity islands of gram negative bacteria.
annotations.comment: Nitrogen fixation in Rhizobiaceae species is encoded by symbiosis islands. Evolution of rhizobia by acquisition of a 500-kb symbiosis island that integrates into a phe-tRNA gene. John T. Sullivan and Clive W. Ronso PNAS 1998 Apr 28 95 (9) 5145-5149.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_rRNA
annotations.definition: A non functional descendant of an rRNA.
annotations.comment: Added Jan 2006 to allow the annotation of the pseudogenic rRNA by flybase. Non-functional is defined as its transcription is prevented due to one or more mutatations.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_tRNA
annotations.definition: A non functional descendent of a tRNA.
annotations.comment: Added Jan 2006 to allow the annotation of the pseudogenic tRNA by flybase. Non-functional is defined as its transcription is prevented due to one or more mutatations.

annotations.label: engineered_episome
annotations.definition: An episome that is engineered.
annotations.comment: Requested by Lynn Crosby Jan 2006.

annotations.label: transgenic
annotations.definition: Attribute describing sequence that has been integrated with foreign sequence.

annotations.label: natural
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a feature that occurs in nature.

annotations.label: engineered
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a region that was modified in vitro.

annotations.label: foreign
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a region from another species.

annotations.label: cloned_region
annotations.definition: The region of sequence that has been inserted and is being propagated by the clone.
annotations.comment: Added in response to Lynn Crosby. A clone insert may be composed of many cloned regions.

annotations.label: validated
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that has been proven.

annotations.label: invalidated
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a feature that is invalidated.

annotations.label: engineered_rescue_region
annotations.definition: A rescue region that is engineered.

annotations.label: rescue_mini_gene
annotations.definition: A mini_gene that rescues.

annotations.label: transgenic_transposable_element
annotations.definition: TE that has been modified in vitro, including insertion of DNA derived from a source other than the originating TE.
annotations.comment: Modified as requested by Lynn - FB. May 2007.

annotations.label: natural_transposable_element
annotations.definition: TE that exists (or existed) in nature.

annotations.label: engineered_transposable_element
annotations.definition: TE that has been modified by manipulations in vitro.

annotations.label: engineered_foreign_transposable_element
annotations.definition: A transposable_element that is engineered and foreign.

annotations.label: assortment_derived_duplication
annotations.definition: A multi-chromosome duplication aberration generated by reassortment of other aberration components.

annotations.label: assortment_derived_deficiency_plus_duplication
annotations.definition: A multi-chromosome aberration generated by reassortment of other aberration components; presumed to have a deficiency and a duplication.

annotations.label: assortment_derived_deficiency
annotations.definition: A multi-chromosome deficiency aberration generated by reassortment of other aberration components.

annotations.label: assortment_derived_aneuploid
annotations.definition: A multi-chromosome aberration generated by reassortment of other aberration components; presumed to have a deficiency or a duplication.

annotations.label: engineered_region
annotations.definition: A region that is engineered.

annotations.label: engineered_foreign_region
annotations.definition: A region that is engineered and foreign.

annotations.label: fusion
annotations.definition: When two regions of DNA are joined together that are not normally together.

annotations.label: engineered_tag
annotations.definition: A tag that is engineered.

annotations.label: validated_cDNA_clone
annotations.definition: A cDNA clone that has been validated.

annotations.label: invalidated_cDNA_clone
annotations.definition: A cDNA clone that is invalid.

annotations.label: chimeric_cDNA_clone
annotations.definition: A cDNA clone invalidated because it is chimeric.

annotations.label: genomically_contaminated_cDNA_clone
annotations.definition: A cDNA clone invalidated by genomic contamination.

annotations.label: polyA_primed_cDNA_clone
annotations.definition: A cDNA clone invalidated by polyA priming.

annotations.label: partially_processed_cDNA_clone
annotations.definition: A cDNA invalidated clone by partial processing.

annotations.label: rescue
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a region's ability, when introduced to a mutant organism, to re-establish (rescue) a phenotype.

annotations.label: mini_gene
annotations.definition: By definition, minigenes are short open-reading frames (ORF), usually encoding approximately 9 to 20 amino acids, which are expressed in vivo (as distinct from being synthesized as peptide or protein ex vivo and subsequently injected). The in vivo synthesis confers a distinct advantage: the expressed sequences can enter both antigen presentation pathways, MHC I (inducing CD8+ T- cells, which are usually cytotoxic T-lymphocytes (CTL)) and MHC II (inducing CD4+ T-cells, usually 'T-helpers' (Th)); and can encounter B-cells, inducing antibody responses. Three main vector approaches have been used to deliver minigenes: viral vectors, bacterial vectors and plasmid DNA.

annotations.label: rescue_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that rescues.

annotations.label: wild_type
annotations.definition: An attribute describing sequence with the genotype found in nature and/or standard laboratory stock.

annotations.label: wild_type_rescue_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that rescues.

annotations.label: mitochondrial_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a mitochondria.

annotations.label: chloroplast_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a chloroplast.

annotations.label: chromoplast_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a chromoplast.

annotations.label: cyanelle_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a cyanelle.

annotations.label: leucoplast_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome with origin in a leucoplast.

annotations.label: macronuclear_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a macronucleus.

annotations.label: micronuclear_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a micronucleus.

annotations.label: nuclear_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a nucleus.

annotations.label: nucleomorphic_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in a nucleomorph.

annotations.label: chromosome_part
annotations.definition: A region of a chromosome.
annotations.comment: This is a manufactured term, that serves the purpose of allow the parts of a chromosome to have an is_a path to the root.

annotations.label: gene_member_region
annotations.definition: A region of a gene.
annotations.comment: A manufactured term used to allow the parts of a gene to have an is_a path to the root.

annotations.label: transcript_region
annotations.definition: A region of a transcript.
annotations.comment: This term was added to provide a grouping term for the region parts of transcript, thus giving them an is_a path back to the root.

annotations.label: mature_transcript_region
annotations.definition: A region of a mature transcript.
annotations.comment: A manufactured term to collect together the parts of a mature transcript and give them an is_a path to the root.

annotations.label: primary_transcript_region
annotations.definition: A part of a primary transcript.
annotations.comment: This term was added to provide a grouping term for the region parts of primary_transcript, thus giving them an is_a path back to the root.

annotations.label: mRNA_region
annotations.definition: A region of an mRNA.
annotations.comment: This term was added to provide a grouping term for the region parts of mRNA, thus giving them an is_a path back to the root.

annotations.label: UTR_region
annotations.definition: A region of UTR.
annotations.comment: A region of UTR. This term is a grouping term to allow the parts of UTR to have an is_a path to the root.

annotations.label: rRNA_primary_transcript_region
annotations.definition: A region of an rRNA primary transcript.
annotations.comment: To allow transcribed_spacer_region to have a path to the root.

annotations.label: polypeptide_region
annotations.definition: Biological sequence region that can be assigned to a specific subsequence of a polypeptide.
annotations.comment: Added to allow the polypeptide regions to have is_a paths back to the root.

annotations.label: repeat_component
annotations.definition: A region of a repeated sequence.
annotations.comment: A manufactured to group the parts of repeats, to give them an is_a path back to the root.

annotations.label: spliceosomal_intron_region
annotations.definition: A region within an intron.
annotations.comment: A terms added to allow the parts of introns to have is_a paths to the root.

annotations.label: gene_component_region
annotations.definition: A region of a gene that has a specific function.

annotations.label: tmRNA_region
annotations.definition: A region of a tmRNA.
annotations.comment: This term was added to provide a grouping term for the region parts of tmRNA, thus giving them an is_a path back to the root.

annotations.label: LTR_component
annotations.definition: The long terminal repeat found at the ends of the sequence to be inserted into the host genome.

annotations.label: three_prime_LTR_component
annotations.definition: A component of the three-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: five_prime_LTR_component
annotations.definition: A component of the five-prime long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: CDS_region
annotations.definition: A region of a CDS.

annotations.label: exon_region
annotations.definition: A region of an exon.

annotations.label: homologous_region
annotations.definition: A region that is homologous to another region.

annotations.label: paralogous_region
annotations.definition: A homologous_region that is paralogous to another region.
annotations.comment: A term to be used in conjunction with the paralogous_to relationship.

annotations.label: orthologous_region
annotations.definition: A homologous_region that is orthologous to another region.
annotations.comment: This term should be used in conjunction with the similarity relationships defined in SO.

annotations.label: conserved
annotations.definition: A region that is similar or identical across more than one species.

annotations.label: homologous
annotations.definition: Similarity due to common ancestry.

annotations.label: orthologous
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a kind of homology where divergence occurred after a speciation event.

annotations.label: paralogous
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a kind of homology where divergence occurred after a duplication event.

annotations.label: syntenic
annotations.definition: Attribute describing sequence regions occurring in same order on chromosome of different species.
