annotations.label: capped_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript that is capped.

annotations.label: capped_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA that is capped.

annotations.label: mRNA_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an mRNA feature.

annotations.label: exemplar
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence is representative of a class of similar sequences.

annotations.label: frameshift
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that contains a mutation involving the deletion or insertion of one or more bases, where this number is not divisible by 3.

annotations.label: minus_1_frameshift
annotations.definition: A frameshift caused by deleting one base.

annotations.label: minus_2_frameshift
annotations.definition: A frameshift caused by deleting two bases.

annotations.label: plus_1_frameshift
annotations.definition: A frameshift caused by inserting one base.

annotations.label: plus_2_framshift
annotations.definition: A frameshift caused by inserting two bases.

annotations.label: trans_spliced
annotations.definition: An attribute describing transcript sequence that is created by splicing exons from diferent genes.

annotations.label: polyadenylated_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA that is polyadenylated.

annotations.label: trans_spliced_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA that is trans-spliced.

annotations.label: edited_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that is edited.

annotations.label: edited_transcript_by_A_to_I_substitution
annotations.definition: A transcript that has been edited by A to I substitution.

synonyms.text: edited transcript by A to I substitution

annotations.label: bound_by_protein
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that is bound by a protein.

annotations.label: bound_by_nucleic_acid
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that is bound by a nucleic acid.

annotations.label: alternatively_spliced
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a situation where a gene may encode for more than 1 transcript.

annotations.label: monocistronic
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that contains the code for one gene product.

annotations.label: dicistronic
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that contains the code for two gene products.

annotations.label: polycistronic
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that contains the code for more than one gene product.

annotations.label: recoded
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an mRNA sequence that has been reprogrammed at translation, causing localized alterations.

annotations.label: codon_redefined
annotations.definition: An attribute describing the alteration of codon meaning.

annotations.label: stop_codon_read_through
annotations.definition: A stop codon redefined to be a new amino acid.

annotations.label: stop_codon_redefined_as_pyrrolysine
annotations.definition: A stop codon redefined to be the new amino acid, pyrrolysine.

annotations.label: stop_codon_redefined_as_selenocysteine
annotations.definition: A stop codon redefined to be the new amino acid, selenocysteine.

annotations.label: recoded_by_translational_bypass
annotations.definition: Recoded mRNA where a block of nucleotides is not translated.

annotations.label: translationally_frameshifted
annotations.definition: Recoding by frameshifting a particular site.

annotations.label: maternally_imprinted_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is maternally_imprinted.

annotations.label: paternally_imprinted_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is paternally imprinted.

annotations.label: post_translationally_regulated_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is post translationally regulated.

annotations.label: negatively_autoregulated_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is negatively autoreguated.

annotations.label: positively_autoregulated_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is positively autoregulated.

annotations.label: silenced
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an epigenetic process where a gene is inactivated at transcriptional or translational level.

annotations.label: silenced_by_DNA_modification
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an epigenetic process where a gene is inactivated by DNA modifications, resulting in repression of transcription.

annotations.label: silenced_by_DNA_methylation
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an epigenetic process where a gene is inactivated by DNA methylation, resulting in repression of transcription.

annotations.label: translationally_regulated_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is translationally regulated.

annotations.label: allelically_excluded_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is allelically_excluded.

annotations.label: epigenetically_modified_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is epigenetically modified.

annotations.label: transgene
annotations.definition: A transgene is a gene that has been transferred naturally or by any of a number of genetic engineering techniques from one organism to another.

annotations.label: endogenous_retroviral_sequence
annotations.definition: Endogenous DNA sequence that are likely to have arisen from retroviruses.

annotations.label: rearranged_at_DNA_level
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe the sequence of a feature, where the DNA is rearranged.

annotations.label: status
annotations.definition: An attribute describing the status of a feature, based on the available evidence.
annotations.comment: This term is the hypernym of attributes and should not be annotated to.

annotations.label: independently_known
annotations.definition: Attribute to describe a feature that is independently known - not predicted.

annotations.label: supported_by_sequence_similarity
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that has been predicted using sequence similarity techniques.

annotations.label: supported_by_domain_match
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that has been predicted using sequence similarity of a known domain.

annotations.label: supported_by_EST_or_cDNA
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature that has been predicted using sequence similarity to EST or cDNA data.

annotations.label: orphan
annotations.definition: A gene whose predicted amino acid sequence is unsupported by any experimental evidence or by any match with any other known sequence.

annotations.label: predicted_by_ab_initio_computation
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a feature that is predicted by a computer program that did not rely on sequence similarity.

annotations.label: asx_turn
annotations.definition: A motif of three consecutive residues and one H-bond in which: residue(i) is Aspartate or Asparagine (Asx), the side-chain O of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+2).

annotations.label: cloned_cDNA_insert
annotations.definition: A clone insert made from cDNA.

annotations.label: cloned_genomic_insert
annotations.definition: A clone insert made from genomic DNA.

annotations.label: engineered_insert
annotations.definition: A clone insert that is engineered.

annotations.label: edited_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA that is edited.

annotations.label: guide_RNA_region
annotations.definition: A region of guide RNA.

annotations.label: anchor_region
annotations.definition: A region of a guide_RNA that base-pairs to a target mRNA.

annotations.label: pre_edited_mRNA
annotations.definition: A primary transcript that, at least in part, encodes one or more proteins that has not been edited.

annotations.label: intermediate
annotations.definition: An attribute to describe a feature between stages of processing.

annotations.label: miRNA_target_site
annotations.definition: A miRNA target site is a binding site where the molecule is a micro RNA.

annotations.label: edited_CDS
annotations.definition: A CDS that is edited.

annotations.label: vertebrate_immunoglobulin_T_cell_receptor_rearranged_segment
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in partially rearranged genomic DNA.

synonyms.text: vertebrate immunoglobulin T cell receptor rearranged segment

annotations.label: vertebrate_immunoglobulin_T_cell_receptor_rearranged_gene_cluster
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA of immunoglobulin/T-cell receptor gene in rearranged configuration.

synonyms.text: vertebrate immunoglobulin T cell receptor rearranged gene cluster

annotations.label: vertebrate_immune_system_gene_recombination_signal_feature
annotations.definition: Feature used for the recombination of genomic material for the purpose of generating diversity of the immune system.

synonyms.text: vertebrate immune system gene recombination signal feature

annotations.label: recombinationally_rearranged
annotations.definition: A gene that is recombinationally rearranged.

annotations.label: recombinationally_rearranged_vertebrate_immune_system_gene
annotations.definition: A recombinationally rearranged gene of the vertebrate immune system.

synonyms.text: recombinationally rearranged vertebrate immune system gene

annotations.label: attP_site
annotations.definition: An integration/excision site of a phage chromosome at which a recombinase acts to insert the phage DNA at a cognate integration/excision site on a bacterial chromosome.

annotations.label: attB_site
annotations.definition: An integration/excision site of a bacterial chromosome at which a recombinase acts to insert foreign DNA containing a cognate integration/excision site.

annotations.label: attL_site
annotations.definition: A region that results from recombination between attP_site and attB_site, composed of the 5' portion of attB_site and the 3' portion of attP_site.

annotations.label: attR_site
annotations.definition: A region that results from recombination between attP_site and attB_site, composed of the 5' portion of attP_site and the 3' portion of attB_site.

annotations.label: integration_excision_site
annotations.definition: A region specifically recognised by a recombinase, which inserts or removes another region marked by a distinct cognate integration/excision site.

annotations.label: resolution_site
annotations.definition: A region specifically recognized by a recombinase, which separates a physically contiguous circle of DNA into two physically separate circles.

annotations.label: inversion_site
annotations.definition: A region specifically recognised by a recombinase, which inverts the region flanked by a pair of sites.
annotations.comment: A target region for site-specific inversion of a DNA region and which carries binding sites for a site-specific recombinase and accessory proteins as well as the site for specific cleavage by the recombinase.

annotations.label: dif_site
annotations.definition: A site at which replicated bacterial circular chromosomes are decatenated by site specific resolvase.

annotations.label: attC_site
annotations.definition: An attC site is a sequence required for the integration of a DNA of an integron.

annotations.label: eukaryotic_terminator
annotations.definition: A signal for RNA polymerase to terminate transcription.

annotations.label: oriV
annotations.definition: An origin of vegetative replication in plasmids and phages.

annotations.label: oriC
annotations.definition: An origin of bacterial chromosome replication.

annotations.label: DNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, DNA molecule.

annotations.label: double_stranded_DNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, double-stranded DNA molecule.

annotations.label: single_stranded_DNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, single-stranded DNA molecule.

annotations.label: linear_double_stranded_DNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, double-stranded, linear DNA molecule.

annotations.label: circular_double_stranded_DNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, double-stranded, circular DNA molecule.

annotations.label: linear_single_stranded_DNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, single-stranded, linear DNA molecule.

annotations.label: circular_single_stranded_DNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, single-stranded, circular DNA molecule.

annotations.label: RNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, RNA molecule.

annotations.label: single_stranded_RNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, single-stranded RNA molecule.

annotations.label: linear_single_stranded_RNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, single-stranded, linear RNA molecule.

annotations.label: linear_double_stranded_RNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, double-stranded, linear RNA molecule.

annotations.label: double_stranded_RNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, double-stranded RNA molecule.

annotations.label: circular_single_stranded_RNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, single-stranded, circular DNA molecule.

annotations.label: circular_double_stranded_RNA_chromosome
annotations.definition: Structural unit composed of a self-replicating, double-stranded, circular RNA molecule.

annotations.label: insertion_sequence
annotations.definition: A terminal_inverted_repeat_element that is bacterial and only encodes the functions required for its transposition between these inverted repeats.

annotations.label: minicircle_gene
annotations.definition: A gene found within a minicircle.

annotations.label: cryptic
annotations.definition: A feature_attribute describing a feature that is not manifest under normal conditions.

annotations.label: anchor_binding_site
annotations.comment: Part of an edited transcript only.

annotations.label: template_region
annotations.definition: A region of a guide_RNA that specifies the insertions and deletions of bases in the editing of a target mRNA.

annotations.label: gRNA_encoding
annotations.definition: A non-protein_coding gene that encodes a guide_RNA.

annotations.label: minicircle
annotations.definition: A minicircle is a replicon, part of a kinetoplast, that encodes for guide RNAs.

annotations.label: rho_dependent_bacterial_terminator
annotations.definition: A transcription terminator that is dependent upon Rho.

annotations.label: rho_independent_bacterial_terminator
annotations.definition: A transcription terminator that is not dependent upon Rho. Rather, the mRNA contains a sequence that allows it to base-pair with itself and make a stem-loop structure.

annotations.label: strand_attribute
annotations.definition: The attribute of how many strands are present in a nucleotide polymer.
annotations.comment: Attributes added to describe the different kinds of replicon. SO workshop, September 2006.

annotations.label: single
annotations.definition: When a nucleotide polymer has only one strand.
annotations.comment: Attributes added to describe the different kinds of replicon. SO workshop, September 2006.

annotations.label: double
annotations.definition: When a nucleotide polymer has two strands that are reverse-complement to one another and pair together.
annotations.comment: Attributes added to describe the different kinds of replicon. SO workshop, September 2006.

annotations.label: topology_attribute
annotations.definition: The attribute of whether a nucleotide polymer is linear or circular.
annotations.comment: Attributes added to describe the different kinds of replicon. SO workshop, September 2006.

annotations.label: linear
annotations.definition: A quality of a nucleotide polymer that has a 3'-terminal residue and a 5'-terminal residue.
annotations.comment: Attributes added to describe the different kinds of replicon. SO workshop, September 2006.

annotations.label: circular
annotations.definition: A quality of a nucleotide polymer that has no terminal nucleotide residues.
annotations.comment: Attributes added to describe the different kinds of replicon. SO workshop, September 2006.

annotations.label: class_II_RNA
annotations.definition: Small non-coding RNA (59-60 nt long) containing 5' and 3' ends that are predicted to come together to form a stem structure. Identified in the social amoeba Dictyostelium discoideum and localized in the cytoplasm.

annotations.label: class_I_RNA
annotations.definition: Small non-coding RNA (55-65 nt long) containing highly conserved 5' and 3' ends (16 and 8 nt, respectively) that are predicted to come together to form a stem structure. Identified in the social amoeba Dictyostelium discoideum and localized in the cytoplasm.
annotations.comment: Requested by Karen Pilcher - Dictybase. song-Term Tracker-1574577.

annotations.label: genomic_DNA
annotations.definition: DNA located in the genome and able to be transmitted to the offspring.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: BAC_cloned_genomic_insert
annotations.definition: A region of DNA that has been inserted into the bacterial genome using a bacterial artificial chromosome.
annotations.comment: Requested by Andy Schroder - Flybase Harvard, Nov 2006.

annotations.label: consensus
annotations.definition: A sequence produced from an aligment algorithm that uses multiple sequences as input.
annotations.comment: Term added Dec 06 to comply with mapping to MGED terms. It should be used to generate consensus regions. The specific cross product terms they require are consensus_region and consensus_mRNA.

annotations.label: consensus_region
annotations.definition: A region that has a known consensus sequence.
annotations.comment: DO not obsolete without considering MGED mapping.

annotations.label: consensus_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA sequence produced from an aligment algorithm that uses multiple sequences as input.
annotations.comment: DO not obsolete without considering MGED mapping.

annotations.label: predicted_gene
annotations.definition: A region of the genome that has been predicted to be a gene but has not been confirmed by laboratory experiments.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: gene_fragment
annotations.definition: A portion of a gene that is not the complete gene.
annotations.comment: This term is mapped to MGED. Do not obsolete without consulting MGED ontology.

annotations.label: recursive_splice_site
annotations.definition: A recursive splice site is a splice site which subdivides a large intron. Recursive splicing is a mechanism that splices large introns by sub dividing the intron at non exonic elements and alternate exons.

annotations.label: BAC_end
annotations.definition: A region of sequence from the end of a BAC clone that may provide a highly specific marker.
annotations.comment: Requested by Keith Boroevich December, 2006.

annotations.label: cytosolic_16S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 16S rRNA is an RNA component of the small subunit of cytosolic ribosomes in prokaryotes.
annotations.comment: Renamed to cytosolic_16S_rRNA from rRNA_16S on 10 June 2021 as per restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Request from EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: cytosolic_23S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 23S rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of cytosolic ribosomes in prokaryotes.
annotations.comment: Renamed from rRNA_23S to cytosolic_23S_rRNA on 27 May 2021 with the restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Requested by EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: cytosolic_25S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 25S rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of cytosolic ribosomes most eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Renamed from rRNA_5S to cytosolic_5S_rRNA on 27 May 2021 with the restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Requested by EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: solo_LTR
annotations.definition: A recombination product between the 2 LTR of the same element.
annotations.comment: Requested by Hadi Quesneville January 2007.

annotations.label: low_complexity
annotations.definition: When a sequence does not contain an equal distribution of all four possible nucleotide bases or does not contain all nucleotide bases.

annotations.label: low_complexity_region
annotations.definition: A region where the DNA does not contain an equal distrubution of all four possible nucleotides or does not contain all four nucleotides.

annotations.label: prophage
annotations.definition: A phage genome after it has established in the host genome in a latent/immune state either as a plasmid or as an integrated "island".

annotations.label: cryptic_prophage
annotations.definition: A remnant of an integrated prophage in the host genome or an "island" in the host genome that includes phage like-genes.
annotations.comment: This is not cryptic in the same sense as a cryptic gene or cryptic splice site.

annotations.label: tetraloop
annotations.definition: A base-paired stem with loop of 4 non-hydrogen bonded nucleotides.

annotations.label: DNA_constraint_sequence
annotations.definition: A double-stranded DNA used to control macromolecular structure and function.

annotations.label: i_motif
annotations.definition: A cytosine rich domain whereby strands associate both inter- and intramolecularly at moderately acidic pH.

annotations.label: PNA_oligo
annotations.definition: Peptide nucleic acid, is a chemical not known to occur naturally but is artificially synthesized and used in some biological research and medical treatments. The PNA backbone is composed of repeating N-(2-aminoethyl)-glycine units linked by peptide bonds. The purine and pyrimidine bases are linked to the backbone by methylene carbonyl bonds.

annotations.label: DNAzyme
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence with catalytic activity.
annotations.comment: Added by request from Colin Batchelor.

annotations.label: MNP
annotations.definition: A multiple nucleotide polymorphism with alleles of common length > 1, for example AAA/TTT.

annotations.label: intron_domain
annotations.definition: An intronic region that has an attribute.
annotations.comment: Requested by Colin Batchelor, Feb 2007.

annotations.label: wobble_base_pair
annotations.definition: A type of non-canonical base pairing, most commonly between G and U, which is important for the secondary structure of RNAs. It has similar thermodynamic stability to the Watson-Crick pairing. Wobble base pairs only have two hydrogen bonds. Other wobble base pair possibilities are I-A, I-U and I-C.

annotations.label: internal_guide_sequence
annotations.definition: A purine-rich sequence in the group I introns which determines the locations of the splice sites in group I intron splicing and has catalytic activity.

annotations.label: silent_mutation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that does not affect protein function. Silent mutations may occur in genic ( CDS, UTR, intron etc) and intergenic regions. Silent mutations may have affects on processes such as splicing and regulation.
annotations.comment: Added in March 2007 in after meeting with PharmGKB. Although this term is in common usage, it is better to annotate with the most specific term possible, such as synonymous codon, intron variant etc.

annotations.label: epitope
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with antibodies, B cells or T cells.
annotations.comment: Requested by Trish Whetzel.

annotations.label: copy_number_variation
annotations.definition: A variation that increases or decreases the copy number of a given region.

annotations.label: chromosome_breakpoint
annotations.definition: A chromosomal region that may sustain a double-strand break, resulting in a recombination event.

annotations.label: inversion_breakpoint
annotations.definition: The point within a chromosome where an inversion begins or ends.

annotations.label: allele
annotations.definition: An allele is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a gene.

annotations.label: haplotype
annotations.definition: A haplotype is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a haplotype block.

annotations.label: polymorphic_sequence_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that is segregating in one or more natural populations of a species.

annotations.label: genome
annotations.definition: A genome is the sum of genetic material within a cell or virion.

annotations.label: genotype
annotations.definition: A genotype is a variant genome, complete or incomplete.

annotations.label: diplotype
annotations.definition: A diplotype is a pair of haplotypes from a given individual. It is a genotype where the phase is known.

annotations.label: direction_attribute
annotations.definition: The attribute of whether the sequence is the same direction as a feature (forward) or the opposite direction as a feature (reverse).

annotations.label: forward
annotations.definition: Forward is an attribute of the feature, where the feature is in the 5' to 3' direction.

annotations.label: reverse
annotations.definition: Reverse is an attribute of the feature, where the feature is in the 3' to 5' direction. Again could be applied to primer.

annotations.label: mitochondrial_DNA
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a mitochondria.
annotations.comment: This terms is used by MO.

annotations.label: chloroplast_DNA
annotations.definition: DNA belonging to the genome of a chloroplast, a photosynthetic plastid.
annotations.comment: This term is used by MO.

annotations.label: miRtron
annotations.definition: A de-branched intron which mimics the structure of pre-miRNA and enters the miRNA processing pathway without Drosha mediated cleavage.
annotations.comment: Ruby et al. Nature 448:83 describe a new class of miRNAs that are derived from de-branched introns.

annotations.label: piRNA
annotations.definition: A small non coding RNA, part of a silencing system that prevents the spreading of selfish genetic elements.

annotations.label: arginyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has an arginine anticodon, and a 3' arginine binding region.

annotations.label: mobile_genetic_element
annotations.definition: A nucleotide region with either intra-genome or intracellular mobility, of varying length, which often carry the information necessary for transfer and recombination with the host genome.

annotations.label: extrachromosomal_mobile_genetic_element
annotations.definition: An MGE that is not integrated into the host chromosome.

annotations.label: integrated_mobile_genetic_element
annotations.definition: An MGE that is integrated into the host chromosome.

annotations.label: integrated_plasmid
annotations.definition: A plasmid sequence that is integrated within the host chromosome.

annotations.label: viral_sequence
annotations.definition: The region of nucleotide sequence of a virus, a submicroscopic particle that replicates by infecting a host cell.
annotations.comment: The definitions of the children of this term were revised Decemeber 2007 after discussion on song-devel. The resulting definitions are slightly unweildy but hopefully more logically correct.

annotations.label: phage_sequence
annotations.definition: The nucleotide sequence of a virus that infects bacteria.

annotations.label: attCtn_site
annotations.definition: An attachment site located on a conjugative transposon and used for site-specific integration of a conjugative transposon.

annotations.label: nuclear_mt_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A nuclear pseudogene of either coding or non-coding mitochondria derived sequence.
annotations.comment: Definition change requested by Val, 3172757.

annotations.label: cointegrated_plasmid
annotations.definition: A MGE region consisting of two fused plasmids resulting from a replicative transposition event.

annotations.label: IRLinv_site
annotations.definition: Component of the inversion site located at the left of a region susceptible to site-specific inversion.

annotations.label: IRRinv_site
annotations.definition: Component of the inversion site located at the right of a region susceptible to site-specific inversion.

annotations.label: inversion_site_part
annotations.definition: A region located within an inversion site.
annotations.comment: A term created to allow the parts of an inversion site have an is_a path back to the root.

annotations.label: defective_conjugative_transposon
annotations.definition: An island that contains genes for integration/excision and the gene and site for the initiation of intercellular transfer by conjugation. It can be complemented for transfer by a conjugative transposon.

annotations.label: repeat_fragment
annotations.definition: A portion of a repeat, interrupted by the insertion of another element.
annotations.comment: Requested by Chris Smith, and others at Flybase to help annotate nested repeats.

annotations.label: transposon_fragment
annotations.definition: A portion of a transposon, interrupted by the insertion of another element.

annotations.label: transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A regulatory_region that modulates the transcription of a gene or genes.
annotations.comment: Previous parent term transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001067) has been merged with this term on 11 Feb 2021 as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: splicing_regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A regulatory_region that modulates splicing.
annotations.comment: Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: sequence_alteration
annotations.definition: A sequence_alteration is a sequence_feature whose extent is the deviation from another sequence.
annotations.comment: Merged with partially characterized change in nucleotide sequence.

synonyms.text: partially characterised change in DNA sequence

annotations.label: sequence_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence_variant is a non exact copy of a sequence_feature or genome exhibiting one or more sequence_alteration.

annotations.label: propeptide_cleavage_site
annotations.definition: The propeptide_cleavage_site is the arginine/lysine boundary on a propeptide where cleavage occurs.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: propeptide
annotations.definition: Part of a peptide chain which is cleaved off during the formation of the mature protein.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: immature_peptide_region
annotations.definition: An immature_peptide_region is the extent of the peptide after it has been translated and before any processing occurs.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: active_peptide
annotations.definition: Active peptides are proteins which are biologically active, released from a precursor molecule.
annotations.comment: Hormones, neuropeptides, antimicrobial peptides, are active peptides. They are typically short (<40 amino acids) in length.

annotations.label: compositionally_biased_region_of_peptide
annotations.definition: Polypeptide region that is rich in a particular amino acid or homopolymeric and greater than three residues in length.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: polypeptide_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence motif is a short (up to 20 amino acids) region of biological interest. Such motifs, although they are too short to constitute functional domains, share sequence similarities and are conserved in different proteins. They display a common function (protein-binding, subcellular location etc.).
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: polypeptide_repeat
annotations.definition: A polypeptide_repeat is a single copy of an internal sequence repetition.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: polypeptide_structural_region
annotations.definition: Region of polypeptide with a given structural property.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: membrane_structure
annotations.definition: Arrangement of the polypeptide with respect to the lipid bilayer.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: extramembrane_polypeptide_region
annotations.definition: Polypeptide region that is localized outside of a lipid bilayer.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: cytoplasmic_polypeptide_region
annotations.definition: Polypeptide region that is localized inside the cytoplasm.

annotations.label: non_cytoplasmic_polypeptide_region
annotations.definition: Polypeptide region that is localized outside of a lipid bilayer and outside of the cytoplasm.
annotations.comment: This could be inside an organelle within the cell.

annotations.label: intramembrane_polypeptide_region
annotations.definition: Polypeptide region present in the lipid bilayer.

annotations.label: membrane_peptide_loop
annotations.definition: Polypeptide region localized within the lipid bilayer where both ends traverse the same membrane.

annotations.label: transmembrane_polypeptide_region
annotations.definition: Polypeptide region traversing the lipid bilayer.

annotations.label: polypeptide_secondary_structure
annotations.definition: A region of peptide with secondary structure has hydrogen bonding along the peptide chain that causes a defined conformation of the chain.
annotations.comment: Biosapien term was secondary_structure.

annotations.label: polypeptide_structural_motif
annotations.definition: Motif is a three-dimensional structural element within the chain, which appears also in a variety of other molecules. Unlike a domain, a motif does not need to form a stable globular unit.

annotations.label: coiled_coil
annotations.definition: A coiled coil is a structural motif in proteins, in which alpha-helices are coiled together like the strands of a rope.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: helix_turn_helix
annotations.definition: A motif comprising two helices separated by a turn.

annotations.label: polypeptide_sequencing_information
annotations.definition: Incompatibility in the sequence due to some experimental problem.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: non_adjacent_residues
annotations.definition: Indicates that two consecutive residues in a fragment sequence are not consecutive in the full-length protein and that there are a number of unsequenced residues between them.

annotations.label: non_terminal_residue
annotations.definition: The residue at an extremity of the sequence is not the terminal residue.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: sequence_conflict
annotations.definition: Different sources report differing sequences.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: sequence_uncertainty
annotations.definition: Describes the positions in a sequence where the authors are unsure about the sequence assignment.

annotations.label: post_translationally_modified_region
annotations.definition: A region where a transformation occurs in a protein after it has been synthesized. This which may regulate, stabilize, crosslink or introduce new chemical functionalities in the protein.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: polypeptide_metal_contact
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with metal ions.
annotations.comment: Residue is part of a binding site for a metal ion.

annotations.label: protein_protein_contact
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the protein molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with polypeptide residues.

annotations.label: polypeptide_calcium_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with calcium ions.
annotations.comment: Residue involved in contact with calcium.

annotations.label: polypeptide_cobalt_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with cobalt ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_copper_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with copper ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_iron_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with iron ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_magnesium_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with magnesium ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_manganese_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with manganese ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_molybdenum_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with molybdenum ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_nickel_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with nickel ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_tungsten_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with tungsten ions.

annotations.label: polypeptide_zinc_ion_contact_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with zinc ions.

annotations.label: catalytic_residue
annotations.definition: Amino acid involved in the activity of an enzyme.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: polypeptide_ligand_contact
annotations.definition: Residues which interact with a ligand.

annotations.label: asx_motif
annotations.definition: A motif of five consecutive residues and two H-bonds in which: Residue(i) is Aspartate or Asparagine (Asx), side-chain O of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+2) or (i+3), main-chain CO of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+3) or (i+4).

annotations.label: beta_bulge
annotations.definition: A motif of three residues within a beta-sheet in which the main chains of two consecutive residues are H-bonded to that of the third, and in which the dihedral angles are as follows: Residue(i): -140 degrees < phi(l) -20 degrees , -90 degrees < psi(l) < 40 degrees. Residue (i+1): -180 degrees < phi < -25 degrees or +120 degrees < phi < +180 degrees, +40 degrees < psi < +180 degrees or -180 degrees < psi < -120 degrees.

annotations.label: beta_bulge_loop
annotations.definition: A motif of three residues within a beta-sheet consisting of two H-bonds. Beta bulge loops often occur at the loop ends of beta-hairpins.

annotations.label: beta_bulge_loop_five
annotations.definition: A motif of three residues within a beta-sheet consisting of two H-bonds in which: the main-chain NH of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain CO of residue(i+4), the main-chain CO of residue i is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+3), these loops have an RL nest at residues i+2 and i+3.

annotations.label: beta_bulge_loop_six
annotations.definition: A motif of three residues within a beta-sheet consisting of two H-bonds in which: the main-chain NH of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain CO of residue(i+5), the main-chain CO of residue i is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+4), these loops have an RL nest at residues i+3 and i+4.

annotations.label: beta_strand
annotations.definition: A beta strand describes a single length of polypeptide chain that forms part of a beta sheet. A single continuous stretch of amino acids adopting an extended conformation of hydrogen bonds between the N-O and the C=O of another part of the peptide. This forms a secondary protein structure in which two or more extended polypeptide regions are hydrogen-bonded to one another in a planar array.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: antiparallel_beta_strand
annotations.definition: A peptide region which hydrogen bonded to another region of peptide running in the oposite direction (one running N-terminal to C-terminal and one running C-terminal to N-terminal). Hydrogen bonding occurs between every other C=O from one strand to every other N-H on the adjacent strand. In this case, if two atoms C-alpha (i) and C-alpha (j) are adjacent in two hydrogen-bonded beta strands, then they form two mutual backbone hydrogen bonds to each other's flanking peptide groups; this is known as a close pair of hydrogen bonds. The peptide backbone dihedral angles (phi, psi) are about (-140 degrees, 135 degrees) in antiparallel sheets.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: parallel_beta_strand
annotations.definition: A peptide region which hydrogen bonded to another region of peptide running in the oposite direction (both running N-terminal to C-terminal). This orientation is slightly less stable because it introduces nonplanarity in the inter-strand hydrogen bonding pattern. Hydrogen bonding occurs between every other C=O from one strand to every other N-H on the adjacent strand. In this case, if two atoms C-alpha (i)and C-alpha (j) are adjacent in two hydrogen-bonded beta strands, then they do not hydrogen bond to each other; rather, one residue forms hydrogen bonds to the residues that flank the other (but not vice versa). For example, residue i may form hydrogen bonds to residues j - 1 and j + 1; this is known as a wide pair of hydrogen bonds. By contrast, residue j may hydrogen-bond to different residues altogether, or to none at all. The dihedral angles (phi, psi) are about (-120 degrees, 115 degrees) in parallel sheets.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: peptide_helix
annotations.definition: A helix is a secondary_structure conformation where the peptide backbone forms a coil.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: left_handed_peptide_helix
annotations.definition: A left handed helix is a region of peptide where the coiled conformation turns in an anticlockwise, left handed screw.

annotations.label: right_handed_peptide_helix
annotations.definition: A right handed helix is a region of peptide where the coiled conformation turns in a clockwise, right handed screw.

annotations.label: alpha_helix
annotations.definition: The helix has 3.6 residues per turn which corresponds to a translation of 1.5 angstroms (= 0.15 nm) along the helical axis. Every backbone N-H group donates a hydrogen bond to the backbone C=O group of the amino acid four residues earlier.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: pi_helix
annotations.definition: The pi helix has 4.1 residues per turn and a translation of 1.15 (=0.115 nm) along the helical axis. The N-H group of an amino acid forms a hydrogen bond with the C=O group of the amino acid five residues earlier.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: three_ten_helix
annotations.definition: The 3-10 helix has 3 residues per turn with a translation of 2.0 angstroms (=0.2 nm) along the helical axis. The N-H group of an amino acid forms a hydrogen bond with the C=O group of the amino acid three residues earlier.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: polypeptide_nest_motif
annotations.definition: A motif of two consecutive residues with dihedral angles. Nest should not have Proline as any residue. Nests frequently occur as parts of other motifs such as Schellman loops.

annotations.label: polypeptide_nest_left_right_motif
annotations.definition: A motif of two consecutive residues with dihedral angles: Residue(i): +20 degrees < phi < +140 degrees, -40 degrees < psi < +90 degrees. Residue(i+1): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi < +40 degrees.

annotations.label: polypeptide_nest_right_left_motif
annotations.definition: A motif of two consecutive residues with dihedral angles: Residue(i): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi < +40 degrees. Residue(i+1): +20 degrees < phi < +140 degrees, -40 degrees < psi < +90 degrees.

annotations.label: schellmann_loop
annotations.definition: A motif of six or seven consecutive residues that contains two H-bonds.

annotations.label: schellmann_loop_seven
annotations.definition: Wild type: A motif of seven consecutive residues that contains two H-bonds in which: the main-chain CO of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+6), the main-chain CO of residue(i+1) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+5).

annotations.label: schellmann_loop_six
annotations.definition: Common Type: A motif of six consecutive residues that contains two H-bonds in which: the main-chain CO of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+5) the main-chain CO of residue(i+1) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+4).

annotations.label: serine_threonine_motif
annotations.definition: A motif of five consecutive residues and two hydrogen bonds in which: residue(i) is Serine (S) or Threonine (T), the side-chain O of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+2) or (i+3) , the main-chain CO group of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+3) or (i+4).

annotations.label: serine_threonine_staple_motif
annotations.definition: A motif of four or five consecutive residues and one H-bond in which: residue(i) is Serine (S) or Threonine (T), the side-chain OH of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain CO of residue(i3) or (i4), Phi angles of residues(i1), (i2) and (i3) are negative.

annotations.label: polypeptide_turn_motif
annotations.definition: A reversal in the direction of the backbone of a protein that is stabilized by hydrogen bond between backbone NH and CO groups, involving no more than 4 amino acid residues.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: asx_turn_left_handed_type_one
annotations.definition: Left handed type I (dihedral angles):- Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi (1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi +120 degrees < +40 degrees. Residue(i+1): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi < +40 degrees.

synonyms.text: asx turn left handed type one

annotations.label: asx_turn_left_handed_type_two
annotations.definition: Left handed type II (dihedral angles):- Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi (1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, +80 degrees < psi +120 degrees < +180 degrees. Residue(i+1): +20 degrees < phi < +140 degrees, -40 degrees < psi < +90 degrees.

synonyms.text: asx turn left handed type two

annotations.label: asx_turn_right_handed_type_two
annotations.definition: Right handed type II (dihedral angles):- Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi (1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, +80 degrees < psi +120 degrees < +180 degrees. Residue(i+1): +20 degrees < phi < +140 degrees, -40 degrees < psi < +90 degrees.

synonyms.text: asx turn right handed type two

annotations.label: asx_turn_right_handed_type_one
annotations.definition: Right handed type I (dihedral angles):- Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi (1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi +120 degrees < +40 degrees. Residue(i+1): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi < +40 degrees.

synonyms.text: asx turn type right handed type one

annotations.label: beta_turn
annotations.definition: A motif of four consecutive residues that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth. It is characterized by the dihedral angles of the second and third residues, which are the basis for sub-categorization.

annotations.label: beta_turn_left_handed_type_one
annotations.definition: Left handed type I:A motif of four consecutive residues that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth. It is characterized by the dihedral angles:- Residue(i+1): -140 degrees > phi > -20 degrees, -90 degrees > psi > +40 degrees. Residue(i+2): -140 degrees > phi > -20 degrees, -90 degrees > psi > +40 degrees.

synonyms.text: beta turn left handed type one

annotations.label: beta_turn_left_handed_type_two
annotations.definition: Left handed type II: A motif of four consecutive residues that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth. It is characterized by the dihedral angles: Residue(i+1): -140 degrees > phi > -20 degrees, +80 degrees > psi > +180 degrees. Residue(i+2): +20 degrees > phi > +140 degrees, -40 degrees > psi > +90 degrees.

synonyms.text: beta turn left handed type two

annotations.label: beta_turn_right_handed_type_one
annotations.definition: Right handed type I:A motif of four consecutive residues that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth. It is characterized by the dihedral angles: Residue(i+1): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi < +40 degrees. Residue(i+2): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 degrees < psi < +40 degrees.

synonyms.text: beta turn right handed type one

annotations.label: beta_turn_right_handed_type_two
annotations.definition: Right handed type II:A motif of four consecutive residues that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth. It is characterized by the dihedral angles: Residue(i+1): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, +80 degrees < psi < +180 degrees. Residue(i+2): +20 degrees < phi < +140 degrees, -40 degrees < psi < +90 degrees.

synonyms.text: beta turn right handed type two

annotations.label: gamma_turn
annotations.definition: Gamma turns, defined for 3 residues i,( i+1),( i+2) if a hydrogen bond exists between residues i and i+2 and the phi and psi angles of residue i+1 fall within 40 degrees.

annotations.label: gamma_turn_classic
annotations.definition: Gamma turns, defined for 3 residues i, i+1, i+2 if a hydrogen bond exists between residues i and i+2 and the phi and psi angles of residue i+1 fall within 40 degrees: phi(i+1)=75.0 - psi(i+1)=-64.0.

annotations.label: gamma_turn_inverse
annotations.definition: Gamma turns, defined for 3 residues i, i+1, i+2 if a hydrogen bond exists between residues i and i+2 and the phi and psi angles of residue i+1 fall within 40 degrees: phi(i+1)=-79.0 - psi(i+1)=69.0.

annotations.label: serine_threonine_turn
annotations.definition: A motif of three consecutive residues and one H-bond in which: residue(i) is Serine (S) or Threonine (T), the side-chain O of residue(i) is H-bonded to the main-chain NH of residue(i+2).

annotations.label: st_turn_left_handed_type_one
annotations.definition: The peptide twists in an anticlockwise, left handed manner. The dihedral angles for this turn are: Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi(1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, -90 degrees psi +120 degrees < +40 degrees, residue(i+1): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 < psi < +40 degrees.

synonyms.text: st turn left handed type one

annotations.label: st_turn_left_handed_type_two
annotations.definition: The peptide twists in an anticlockwise, left handed manner. The dihedral angles for this turn are: Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi(1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, +80 degrees psi +120 degrees < +180 degrees, residue(i+1): +20 degrees < phi < +140 degrees, -40 < psi < +90 degrees.

synonyms.text: st turn left handed type two

annotations.label: st_turn_right_handed_type_one
annotations.definition: The peptide twists in an clockwise, right handed manner. The dihedral angles for this turn are: Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi(1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, -90 degrees psi +120 degrees < +40 degrees, residue(i+1): -140 degrees < phi < -20 degrees, -90 < psi < +40 degrees.

synonyms.text: st turn right handed type one

annotations.label: st_turn_right_handed_type_two
annotations.definition: The peptide twists in an clockwise, right handed manner. The dihedral angles for this turn are: Residue(i): -140 degrees < chi(1) -120 degrees < -20 degrees, +80 degrees psi +120 degrees < +180 degrees, residue(i+1): +20 degrees < phi < +140 degrees, -40 < psi < +90 degrees.

synonyms.text: st turn right handed type two

annotations.label: polypeptide_variation_site
annotations.definition: A site of sequence variation (alteration). Alternative sequence due to naturally occurring events such as polymorphisms and alternative splicing or experimental methods such as site directed mutagenesis.
annotations.comment: For example, was a substitution natural or mutated as part of an experiment? This term is added to merge the biosapiens term sequence_variations.

annotations.label: natural_variant_site
annotations.definition: Describes the natural sequence variants due to polymorphisms, disease-associated mutations, RNA editing and variations between strains, isolates or cultivars.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: mutated_variant_site
annotations.definition: Site which has been experimentally altered.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: alternate_sequence_site
annotations.definition: Description of sequence variants produced by alternative splicing, alternative promoter usage, alternative initiation and ribosomal frameshifting.
annotations.comment: Discrete.

annotations.label: beta_turn_type_six
annotations.definition: A motif of four consecutive peptide resides of type VIa or type VIb and where the i+2 residue is cis-proline.

annotations.label: beta_turn_type_six_a
annotations.definition: A motif of four consecutive peptide residues, of which the i+2 residue is proline, and that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth and is characterized by the dihedral angles: Residue(i+1): phi ~ -60 degrees, psi ~ 120 degrees. Residue(i+2): phi ~ -90 degrees, psi ~ 0 degrees.

annotations.label: beta_turn_type_six_a_one
annotations.definition: A type VIa beta turn with the following phi and psi sngles on amino acid residues 2 and 3: phi-2 = -60 degrees, psi-2 = 120 degrees, phi-3 = -90 degrees, psi-3 = 0 degrees.

synonyms.text: beta turn type six a one

annotations.label: beta_turn_type_six_a_two
annotations.definition: A type VIa beta turn with the following phi and psi sngles on amino acid residues 2 and 3: phi-2 = -120 degrees, psi-2 = 120 degrees, phi-3 = -60 degrees, psi-3 = 0 degrees.

synonyms.text: beta turn type six a two

annotations.label: beta_turn_type_six_b
annotations.definition: A motif of four consecutive peptide residues, of which the i+2 residue is proline, and that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth and is characterized by the dihedral angles: Residue(i+1): phi ~ -120 degrees, psi ~ 120 degrees. Residue(i+2): phi ~ -60 degrees, psi ~ 0 degrees.

annotations.label: beta_turn_type_eight
annotations.definition: A motif of four consecutive peptide residues that may contain one H-bond, which, if present, is between the main-chain CO of the first residue and the main-chain NH of the fourth and is characterized by the dihedral angles: Residue(i+1): phi ~ -60 degrees, psi ~ -30 degrees. Residue(i+2): phi ~ -120 degrees, psi ~ 120 degrees.

annotations.label: DRE_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, usually located between -10 and -60 relative to the TSS. Consensus sequence is WATCGATW.
annotations.comment: This consensus sequence was identified computationally using the MEME algorithm within core promoter sequences from -60 to +40, with an E value of 1.7e-183. Tends to co-occur with Motif 7. Tends to not occur with DPE motif (SO:0000015) or motif 10.

annotations.label: DMv4_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, located immediately upstream of some TATA box elements with respect to the TSS (+1). Consensus sequence is YGGTCACACTR. Marked spatial preference within core promoter; tend to occur near the TSS, although not as tightly as INR (SO:0000014).

annotations.label: E_box_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, usually located between -60 and +1 relative to the TSS. Consensus sequence is AWCAGCTGWT. Tends to co-occur with DMv2 (SO:0001161). Tends to not occur with DPE motif (SO:0000015).

annotations.label: DMv5_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, usually located between -50 and -10 relative to the TSS. Consensus sequence is KTYRGTATWTTT. Tends to co-occur with DMv4 (SO:0001157) . Tends to not occur with DPE motif (SO:0000015) or MTE (SO:0001162).

annotations.label: DMv3_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, usually located between -30 and +15 relative to the TSS. Consensus sequence is KNNCAKCNCTRNY. Tends to co-occur with DMv2 (SO:0001161). Tends to not occur with DPE motif (SO:0000015) or MTE (0001162).

annotations.label: DMv2_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, usually located between -60 and -45 relative to the TSS. Consensus sequence is MKSYGGCARCGSYSS. Tends to co-occur with DMv3 (SO:0001160). Tends to not occur with DPE motif (SO:0000015) or MTE (SO:0001162).

annotations.label: MTE
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters, usually located between +20 and +30 relative to the TSS. Consensus sequence is CSARCSSAACGS. Tends to co-occur with INR motif (SO:0000014). Tends to not occur with DPE motif (SO:0000015) or DMv5 (SO:0001159).

annotations.label: INR1_motif
annotations.definition: A promoter motif with consensus sequence TCATTCG.

annotations.label: DPE1_motif
annotations.definition: A promoter motif with consensus sequence CGGACGT.

annotations.label: DMv1_motif
annotations.definition: A promoter motif with consensus sequence CARCCCT.

annotations.label: GAGA_motif
annotations.definition: A non directional promoter motif with consensus sequence GAGAGCG.

annotations.label: NDM2_motif
annotations.definition: A non directional promoter motif with consensus CGMYGYCR.

annotations.label: NDM3_motif
annotations.definition: A non directional promoter motif with consensus sequence GAAAGCT.

annotations.label: ds_RNA_viral_sequence
annotations.definition: A ds_RNA_viral_sequence is a viral_sequence that is the sequence of a virus that exists as double stranded RNA.

annotations.label: polinton
annotations.definition: A kind of DNA transposon that populates the genomes of protists, fungi, and animals, characterized by a unique set of proteins necessary for their transposition, including a protein-primed DNA polymerase B, retroviral integrase, cysteine protease, and ATPase. Polintons are characterized by 6-bp target site duplications, terminal-inverted repeats that are several hundred nucleotides long, and 5'-AG and TC-3' termini. Polintons exist as autonomous and nonautonomous elements.

annotations.label: tRNA_region
annotations.definition: A region of a tRNA.

annotations.label: anticodon_loop
annotations.definition: A sequence of seven nucleotide bases in tRNA which contains the anticodon. It has the sequence 5'-pyrimidine-purine-anticodon-modified purine-any base-3.

annotations.label: anticodon
annotations.definition: A sequence of three nucleotide bases in tRNA which recognizes a codon in mRNA.

annotations.label: CCA_tail
annotations.definition: Base sequence at the 3' end of a tRNA. The 3'-hydroxyl group on the terminal adenosine is the attachment point for the amino acid.

annotations.label: DHU_loop
annotations.definition: Non-base-paired sequence of nucleotide bases in tRNA. It contains several dihydrouracil residues.

annotations.label: T_loop
annotations.definition: Non-base-paired sequence of three nucleotide bases in tRNA. It has sequence T-Psi-C.

annotations.label: pyrrolysine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding pyrrolysyl tRNA (SO:0000766).

annotations.label: U3_snoRNA
annotations.definition: U3 snoRNA is a member of the box C/D class of small nucleolar RNAs. The U3 snoRNA secondary structure is characterised by a small 5' domain (with boxes A and A'), and a larger 3' domain (with boxes B, C, C', and D), the two domains being linked by a single-stranded hinge. Boxes B and C form the B/C motif, which appears to be exclusive to U3 snoRNAs, and boxes C' and D form the C'/D motif. The latter is functionally similar to the C/D motifs found in other snoRNAs. The 5' domain and the hinge region act as a pre-rRNA-binding domain. The 3' domain has conserved protein-binding sites. Both the box B/C and box C'/D motifs are sufficient for nuclear retention of U3 snoRNA. The box C'/D motif is also necessary for nucleolar localization, stability and hypermethylation of U3 snoRNA. Both box B/C and C'/D motifs are involved in specific protein interactions and are necessary for the rRNA processing functions of U3 snoRNA.
annotations.comment: The definition is most of the old definition for snoRNA (SO:0000275).

annotations.label: AU_rich_element
annotations.definition: A cis-acting element found in the 3' UTR of some mRNA which is rich in AUUUA pentamers. Messenger RNAs bearing multiple AU-rich elements are often unstable.

annotations.label: Bruno_response_element
annotations.definition: A cis-acting element found in the 3' UTR of some mRNA which is bound by the Drosophila Bruno protein and its homologs.
annotations.comment: Not to be confused with BRE_motif (SO:0000016), which binds transcription factor II B.

annotations.label: iron_responsive_element
annotations.definition: A regulatory sequence found in the 5' and 3' UTRs of many mRNAs which encode iron-binding proteins. It has a hairpin structure and is recognized by trans-acting proteins known as iron-regulatory proteins.

annotations.label: morpholino_backbone
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence composed of nucleobases bound to a morpholino backbone. A morpholino backbone consists of morpholine (CHEBI:34856) rings connected by phosphorodiamidate linkages.
annotations.comment: Do not use this for feature annotation. Use morpholino_oligo (SO:0000034) instead.

annotations.label: PNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence composed of peptide nucleic acid (CHEBI:48021), a chemical consisting of nucleobases bound to a backbone composed of repeating N-(2-aminoethyl)-glycine units linked by peptide bonds. The purine and pyrimidine bases are linked to the backbone by methylene carbonyl bonds.
annotations.comment: Do not use this term for feature annotation. Use PNA_oligo (SO:0001011) instead.

annotations.label: enzymatic
annotations.definition: An attribute describing the sequence of a transcript that has catalytic activity with or without an associated ribonucleoprotein.
annotations.comment: Do not use this for feature annotation. Use enzymatic_RNA (SO:0000372) instead.

annotations.label: ribozymic
annotations.definition: An attribute describing the sequence of a transcript that has catalytic activity even without an associated ribonucleoprotein.
annotations.comment: Do not use this for feature annotation. Use ribozyme (SO:0000374) instead.

annotations.label: pseudouridylation_guide_snoRNA
annotations.definition: A snoRNA that specifies the site of pseudouridylation in an RNA molecule by base pairing with a short sequence around the target residue.
annotations.comment: Has RNA pseudouridylation guide activity (GO:0030558).

annotations.label: LNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence consisting of nucleobases attached to a repeating unit made of 'locked' deoxyribose rings connected to a phosphate backbone. The deoxyribose unit's conformation is 'locked' by a 2'-C,4'-C-oxymethylene link.
annotations.comment: Do not use this term for feature annotation. Use LNA_oligo (SO:0001189) instead.

annotations.label: LNA_oligo
annotations.definition: An oligo composed of LNA residues.

annotations.label: TNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence consisting of nucleobases attached to a repeating unit made of threose rings connected to a phosphate backbone.
annotations.comment: Do not use this term for feature annotation. Use TNA_oligo (SO:0001191) instead.

annotations.label: TNA_oligo
annotations.definition: An oligo composed of TNA residues.

annotations.label: GNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence consisting of nucleobases attached to a repeating unit made of an acyclic three-carbon propylene glycol connected to a phosphate backbone. It has two enantiomeric forms, (R)-GNA and (S)-GNA.
annotations.comment: Do not use this term for feature annotation. Use GNA_oligo (SO:0001192) instead.

annotations.label: GNA_oligo
annotations.definition: An oligo composed of GNA residues.

annotations.label: R_GNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a GNA sequence in the (R)-GNA enantiomer.
annotations.comment: Do not use this term for feature annotation. Use R_GNA_oligo (SO:0001195) instead.

annotations.label: R_GNA_oligo
annotations.definition: An oligo composed of (R)-GNA residues.

annotations.label: S_GNA
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a GNA sequence in the (S)-GNA enantiomer.
annotations.comment: Do not use this term for feature annotation. Use S_GNA_oligo (SO:0001197) instead.

annotations.label: S_GNA_oligo
annotations.definition: An oligo composed of (S)-GNA residues.

annotations.label: ds_DNA_viral_sequence
annotations.definition: A ds_DNA_viral_sequence is a viral_sequence that is the sequence of a virus that exists as double stranded DNA.

annotations.label: ss_RNA_viral_sequence
annotations.definition: A ss_RNA_viral_sequence is a viral_sequence that is the sequence of a virus that exists as single stranded RNA.

annotations.label: negative_sense_ssRNA_viral_sequence
annotations.definition: A negative_sense_RNA_viral_sequence is a ss_RNA_viral_sequence that is the sequence of a single stranded RNA virus that is complementary to mRNA and must be converted to positive sense RNA by RNA polymerase before translation.

synonyms.text: negative sense single stranded RNA virus

annotations.label: positive_sense_ssRNA_viral_sequence
annotations.definition: A positive_sense_RNA_viral_sequence is a ss_RNA_viral_sequence that is the sequence of a single stranded RNA virus that can be immediately translated by the host.

synonyms.text: positive sense single stranded RNA virus

annotations.label: ambisense_ssRNA_viral_sequence
annotations.definition: A ambisense_RNA_virus is a ss_RNA_viral_sequence that is the sequence of a single stranded RNA virus with both messenger and anti messenger polarity.

annotations.label: Phage_RNA_Polymerase_Promoter
annotations.definition: A region (DNA) to which Bacteriophage RNA polymerase binds, to begin transcription.
annotations.comment: former parent RNA_polymerase_promoter SO:0001203 was merged with promoter SO:0000167 in Aug 2020 as part of GREEKC.

annotations.label: SP6_RNA_Polymerase_Promoter
annotations.definition: A region (DNA) to which the SP6 RNA polymerase binds, to begin transcription.

annotations.label: T3_RNA_Polymerase_Promoter
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence to which the T3 RNA polymerase binds, to begin transcription.

annotations.label: T7_RNA_Polymerase_Promoter
annotations.definition: A region (DNA) to which the T7 RNA polymerase binds, to begin transcription.

annotations.label: five_prime_EST
annotations.definition: An EST read from the 5' end of a transcript that usually codes for a protein. These regions tend to be conserved across species and do not change much within a gene family.

annotations.label: three_prime_EST
annotations.definition: An EST read from the 3' end of a transcript. They are more likely to fall within non-coding, or untranslated regions(UTRs).

annotations.label: translational_frameshift
annotations.definition: The region of mRNA (not divisible by 3 bases) that is skipped or added during the process of translational frameshifting (GO:0006452), causing the reading frame to be different.
annotations.comment: Added synonym 'ribosomal_slippage' on Feb 1, 2021, a term in INSDC and GenBank. See GitHub Issue #522.

annotations.label: plus_1_translational_frameshift
annotations.definition: The region of mRNA 1 base long that is skipped during the process of translational frameshifting (GO:0006452), causing the reading frame to be different.

annotations.label: plus_2_translational_frameshift
annotations.definition: The region of mRNA 2 bases long that is skipped during the process of translational frameshifting (GO:0006452), causing the reading frame to be different.

annotations.label: group_III_intron
annotations.definition: Group III introns are introns found in the mRNA of the plastids of euglenoid protists. They are spliced by a two step transesterification with bulged adenosine as initiating nucleophile.

annotations.label: noncoding_region_of_exon
annotations.definition: The maximal intersection of exon and UTR.
annotations.comment: An exon either containing but not starting with a start codon or containing but not ending with a stop codon will be partially coding and partially non coding.

annotations.label: coding_region_of_exon
annotations.definition: The region of an exon that encodes for protein sequence.
annotations.comment: An exon containing either a start or stop codon will be partially coding and partially non coding.

annotations.label: endonuclease_spliced_intron
annotations.definition: An intron that spliced via endonucleolytic cleavage and ligation rather than transesterification.

annotations.label: protein_coding_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for an RNA that can be translated into a protein.

annotations.label: transgenic_insertion
annotations.definition: An insertion that derives from another organism, via the use of recombinant DNA technology.

annotations.label: retrogene
annotations.definition: A gene that has been produced as the product of a reverse transcriptase mediated event.

annotations.label: silenced_by_RNA_interference
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an epigenetic process where a gene is inactivated by RNA interference.
annotations.comment: RNA interference is GO:0016246.

annotations.label: silenced_by_histone_modification
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an epigenetic process where a gene is inactivated by histone modification.
annotations.comment: Histone modification is GO:0016570.

annotations.label: silenced_by_histone_methylation
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an epigenetic process where a gene is inactivated by histone methylation.
annotations.comment: Histone methylation is GO:0016571.

annotations.label: silenced_by_histone_deacetylation
annotations.definition: An attribute describing an epigenetic process where a gene is inactivated by histone deacetylation.
annotations.comment: Histone deacetylation is GO:0016573.

annotations.label: gene_silenced_by_RNA_interference
annotations.definition: A gene that is silenced by RNA interference.

annotations.label: gene_silenced_by_histone_modification
annotations.definition: A gene that is silenced by histone modification.

annotations.label: gene_silenced_by_histone_methylation
annotations.definition: A gene that is silenced by histone methylation.

annotations.label: gene_silenced_by_histone_deacetylation
annotations.definition: A gene that is silenced by histone deacetylation.

annotations.label: dihydrouridine
annotations.definition: A modified RNA base in which the 5,6-dihydrouracil is bound to the ribose ring.

annotations.label: pseudouridine
annotations.definition: A modified RNA base in which the 5- position of the uracil is bound to the ribose ring instead of the 4- position.
annotations.comment: The free molecule is CHEBI:17802.

annotations.label: inosine
annotations.definition: A modified RNA base in which hypoxanthine is bound to the ribose ring.
annotations.comment: The free molecule is CHEBI:17596.

annotations.label: seven_methylguanine
annotations.definition: A modified RNA base in which guanine is methylated at the 7- position.
annotations.comment: The free molecule is CHEBI:2274.

annotations.label: ribothymidine
annotations.definition: A modified RNA base in which thymine is bound to the ribose ring.
annotations.comment: The free molecule is CHEBI:30832.

annotations.label: methylinosine
annotations.definition: A modified RNA base in which methylhypoxanthine is bound to the ribose ring.

annotations.label: mobile
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a feature that has either intra-genome or intracellular mobility.

annotations.label: replicon
annotations.definition: A region containing at least one unique origin of replication and a unique termination site.

annotations.label: base
annotations.definition: A base is a sequence feature that corresponds to a single unit of a nucleotide polymer.

annotations.label: amino_acid
annotations.definition: A sequence feature that corresponds to a single amino acid residue in a polypeptide.
annotations.comment: Probably in the future this will cross reference to Chebi.

annotations.label: major_TSS
annotations.definition: The tanscription start site that is most frequently used for transcription of a gene.

annotations.label: minor_TSS
annotations.definition: A tanscription start site that is not the most frequently used for transcription of a gene.

annotations.label: encodes_alternate_transcription_start_sites
annotations.definition: A gene that has multiple possible transcription start sites.

annotations.label: miRNA_primary_transcript_region
annotations.definition: A part of an miRNA primary_transcript.

annotations.label: pre_miRNA
annotations.definition: The 60-70 nucleotide region remain after Drosha processing of the primary transcript, that folds back upon itself to form a hairpin structure.

annotations.label: miRNA_stem
annotations.definition: The stem of the hairpin loop formed by folding of the pre-miRNA.

annotations.label: miRNA_loop
annotations.definition: The loop of the hairpin loop formed by folding of the pre-miRNA.

annotations.label: synthetic_oligo
annotations.definition: An oligo composed of synthetic nucleotides.

annotations.label: assembly
annotations.definition: A region of the genome of known length that is composed by ordering and aligning two or more different regions.

annotations.label: fragment_assembly
annotations.definition: A fragment assembly is a genome assembly that orders overlapping fragments of the genome based on landmark sequences. The base pair distance between the landmarks is known allowing additivity of lengths.

annotations.label: fingerprint_map
annotations.definition: A fingerprint_map is a physical map composed of restriction fragments.

annotations.label: STS_map
annotations.definition: An STS map is a physical map organized by the unique STS landmarks.

annotations.label: RH_map
annotations.definition: A radiation hybrid map is a physical map.

annotations.label: sonicate_fragment
annotations.definition: A DNA fragment generated by sonication. Sonication is a technique used to sheer DNA into smaller fragments.

annotations.label: polyploid
annotations.definition: A kind of chromosome variation where the chromosome complement is an exact multiple of the haploid number and is greater than the diploid number.

annotations.label: autopolyploid
annotations.definition: A polyploid where the multiple chromosome set was derived from the same organism.

annotations.label: allopolyploid
annotations.definition: A polyploid where the multiple chromosome set was derived from a different organism.

annotations.label: homing_endonuclease_binding_site
annotations.definition: The binding site (recognition site) of a homing endonuclease. The binding site is typically large.

annotations.label: octamer_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence element characteristic of some RNA polymerase II promoters with sequence ATTGCAT that binds Pou-domain transcription factors.

annotations.label: apicoplast_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome originating in an apicoplast.

annotations.label: sequence_collection
annotations.definition: A collection of discontinuous sequences.

annotations.label: overlapping_feature_set
annotations.definition: A continuous region of sequence composed of the overlapping of multiple sequence_features, which ultimately provides evidence for another sequence_feature.
annotations.comment: This feature was requested by Nicole, tracker id 1911479. It is required to gather evidence together for annotation. An example would be overlapping ESTs that support an mRNA.

annotations.label: overlapping_EST_set
annotations.definition: A continous experimental result region extending the length of multiple overlapping EST's.

annotations.label: ncRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a non-coding RNA.

annotations.label: gRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A noncoding RNA that guides the insertion or deletion of uridine residues in mitochondrial mRNAs. This may also refer to synthetic RNAs used to guide DNA editing using the CRIPSR/Cas9 system.

annotations.label: miRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A small noncoding RNA of approximately 22 nucleotides in length which may be involved in regulation of gene expression.
annotations.comment: Moved from ncRNA_gene to sncRNA_gene 27 April 2021 to be more consistent with the organization of the ncRNA branch of SO. Requested by FlyBase, moved by Dave Sant. See GitHub Issue #514.

annotations.label: scRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene encoding a small noncoding RNA that is generally found only in the cytoplasm.

annotations.label: snoRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene encoding a small noncoding RNA that participates in the processing or chemical modifications of many RNAs, including ribosomal RNAs and spliceosomal RNAs.
annotations.comment: Moved from ncRNA_gene to sncRNA_gene 27 April 2021 to be more consistent with the organization of the ncRNA branch of SO. Requested by FlyBase, moved by Dave Sant. See GitHub Issue #514. Added additional children of snoRNA on 18 Nov 2021 at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519.

annotations.label: snRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a small nuclear RNA.
annotations.comment: Moved from ncRNA_gene to sncRNA_gene 27 April 2021 to be more consistent with the organization of the ncRNA branch of SO. Requested by FlyBase, moved by Dave Sant. See GitHub Issue #514.

annotations.label: SRP_RNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a signal recognition particle (SRP) RNA.

annotations.label: tmRNA_gene
