annotations.definition: A bacterial RNA with both tRNA and mRNA like properties.
annotations.comment: Moved from ncRNA_gene to sncRNA_gene 27 April 2021 to be more consistent with the organization of the ncRNA branch of SO. Requested by FlyBase, moved by Dave Sant. See GitHub Issue #514.

annotations.label: tRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A noncoding RNA that binds to a specific amino acid to allow that amino acid to be used by the ribosome during translation of RNA.
annotations.comment: Moved from ncRNA_gene to sncRNA_gene 27 April 2021 to be more consistent with the organization of the ncRNA branch of SO. Requested by FlyBase, moved by Dave Sant. See GitHub Issue #514.

annotations.label: peptidyl
annotations.definition: An attribute describing the nature of a proteinaceous polymer, where by the amino acid units are joined by peptide bonds.

annotations.label: cleaved_for_gpi_anchor_region
annotations.definition: The C-terminal residues of a polypeptide which are exchanged for a GPI-anchor.

annotations.label: biomaterial_region
annotations.definition: A region which is intended for use in an experiment.

annotations.label: experimental_feature
annotations.definition: A region which is the result of some arbitrary experimental procedure. The procedure may be carried out with biological material or inside a computer.

annotations.label: biological_region
annotations.definition: A region defined by its disposition to be involved in a biological process.

annotations.label: topologically_defined_region
annotations.definition: A DNA region within which self-interaction occurs more often than expected by chance because of DNA-looping.

annotations.label: translocation_breakpoint
annotations.definition: The point within a chromosome where a translocation begins or ends.

annotations.label: insertion_breakpoint
annotations.definition: The point within a chromosome where a insertion begins or ends.

annotations.label: deletion_breakpoint
annotations.definition: The point within a chromosome where a deletion begins or ends.

annotations.label: five_prime_flanking_region
annotations.definition: A flanking region located five prime of a specific region.

annotations.label: three_prime_flanking_region
annotations.definition: A flanking region located three prime of a specific region.

annotations.label: transcribed_fragment
annotations.definition: An experimental region, defined by a tiling array experiment to be transcribed at some level.
annotations.comment: Term requested by the MODencode group.

annotations.label: cis_splice_site
annotations.definition: Intronic 2 bp region bordering exon. A splice_site that adjacent_to exon and overlaps intron.

annotations.label: trans_splice_site
annotations.definition: Primary transcript region bordering trans-splice junction.

annotations.label: splice_junction
annotations.definition: The boundary between an intron and an exon.

annotations.label: conformational_switch
annotations.definition: A region of a polypeptide, involved in the transition from one conformational state to another.
annotations.comment: MM Young, K Kirshenbaum, KA Dill & S Highsmith. Predicting conformational switches in proteins. Protein Science, 1999, 8, 1752-64. K. Kirshenbaum, M.M. Young and S. Highsmith. Predicting Allosteric Switches in Myosins. Protein Science 8(9):1806-1815. 1999.

annotations.label: dye_terminator_read
annotations.definition: A read produced by the dye terminator method of sequencing.

annotations.label: pyrosequenced_read
annotations.definition: A read produced by pyrosequencing technology.
annotations.comment: An example is a read produced by Roche 454 technology.

annotations.label: ligation_based_read
annotations.definition: A read produced by ligation based sequencing technologies.
annotations.comment: An example of this kind of read is one produced by ABI SOLiD.

annotations.label: polymerase_synthesis_read
annotations.definition: A read produced by the polymerase based sequence by synthesis method.
annotations.comment: An example is a read produced by Illumina technology.

annotations.label: cis_regulatory_frameshift_element
annotations.definition: A structural region in an RNA molecule which promotes ribosomal frameshifting of cis coding sequence.
annotations.comment: Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: expressed_sequence_assembly
annotations.definition: A sequence assembly derived from expressed sequences.
annotations.comment: From tracker [ 2372385 ] expressed_sequence_assembly.

annotations.label: DNA_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with DNA.

annotations.label: cryptic_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that is not transcribed under normal conditions and is not critical to normal cellular functioning.

annotations.label: three_prime_RACE_clone
annotations.definition: A three prime RACE (Rapid Amplification of cDNA Ends) clone is a cDNA clone copied from the 3' end of an mRNA (using a poly-dT primer to capture the polyA tail and a gene-specific or randomly primed 5' primer), and spliced into a vector for propagation in a suitable host.

annotations.label: cassette_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A cassette pseudogene is a kind of gene in an inactive form which may recombine at a telomeric locus to form a functional copy.
annotations.comment: Requested by the Trypanosome community.

annotations.label: alanine
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codons GCN (GCT, GCC, GCA and GCG).
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: valine
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codons GTN (GTT, GTC, GTA and GTG).
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: leucine
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codons CTN (CTT, CTC, CTA and CTG), TTA and TTG.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: isoleucine
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codons ATH (ATT, ATC and ATA).
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: proline
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codons CCN (CCT, CCC, CCA and CCG).
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: tryptophan
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codon TGG.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: phenylalanine
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codons TTT and TTC.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: methionine
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophobic amino acid encoded by the codon ATG.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: glycine
annotations.definition: A non-polar, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons GGN (GGT, GGC, GGA and GGG).
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: serine
annotations.definition: A polar, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons TCN (TCT, TCC, TCA, TCG), AGT and AGC.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: threonine
annotations.definition: A polar, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons ACN (ACT, ACC, ACA and ACG).
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: tyrosine
annotations.definition: A polar, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons TAT and TAC.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: cysteine
annotations.definition: A polar amino acid encoded by the codons TGT and TGC.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: glutamine
annotations.definition: A polar, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons CAA and CAG.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: asparagine
annotations.definition: A polar, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons AAT and AAC.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: lysine
annotations.definition: A positively charged, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons AAA and AAG.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: arginine
annotations.definition: A positively charged, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons CGN (CGT, CGC, CGA and CGG), AGA and AGG.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: histidine
annotations.definition: A positively charged, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons CAT and CAC.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: aspartic_acid
annotations.definition: A negatively charged, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons GAT and GAC.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: glutamic_acid
annotations.definition: A negatively charged, hydorophilic amino acid encoded by the codons GAA and GAG.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: selenocysteine
annotations.definition: A relatively rare amino acid encoded by the codon UGA in some contexts, whereas UGA is a termination codon in other contexts.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: pyrrolysine
annotations.definition: A relatively rare amino acid encoded by the codon UAG in some contexts, whereas UAG is a termination codon in other contexts.
annotations.comment: A place holder for a cross product with chebi.

annotations.label: transcribed_cluster
annotations.definition: A region defined by a set of transcribed sequences from the same gene or expressed pseudogene.
annotations.comment: This term was requested by Jeff Bowes, using the tracker, ID = 2594157.

annotations.label: unigene_cluster
annotations.definition: A kind of transcribed_cluster defined by a set of transcribed sequences from the a unique gene.
annotations.comment: This term was requested by Jeff Bowes, using the tracker, ID = 2594157.

annotations.label: CRISPR
annotations.definition: Clustered Palindromic Repeats interspersed with bacteriophage derived spacer sequences.

annotations.label: insulator_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in an insulator region of a nucleotide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with polypeptide residues.
annotations.comment: See tracker ID 2060908.

annotations.label: enhancer_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the enhancer region of a nucleotide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with polypeptide residues.

annotations.label: contig_collection
annotations.definition: A collection of contigs.
annotations.comment: See tracker ID: 2138359.

annotations.label: lincRNA
annotations.definition: Long, intervening non-coding RNA. A transcript that does not overlap within the start or end genomic coordinates of a coding gene or pseudogene on either strand.

annotations.label: UST
annotations.definition: An EST spanning part or all of the untranslated regions of a protein-coding transcript.

annotations.label: three_prime_UST
annotations.definition: A UST located in the 3'UTR of a protein-coding transcript.

annotations.label: five_prime_UST
annotations.definition: An UST located in the 5'UTR of a protein-coding transcript.

annotations.label: RST
annotations.definition: A tag produced from a single sequencing read from a RACE product; typically a few hundred base pairs long.

annotations.label: three_prime_RST
annotations.definition: A tag produced from a single sequencing read from a 3'-RACE product; typically a few hundred base pairs long.

annotations.label: five_prime_RST
annotations.definition: A tag produced from a single sequencing read from a 5'-RACE product; typically a few hundred base pairs long.

annotations.label: UST_match
annotations.definition: A match against an UST sequence.

annotations.label: RST_match
annotations.definition: A match against an RST sequence.

annotations.label: primer_match
annotations.definition: A nucleotide match to a primer sequence.

annotations.label: miRNA_antiguide
annotations.definition: A region of the pri miRNA that base pairs with the guide to form the hairpin.

annotations.label: trans_splice_junction
annotations.definition: The boundary between the spliced leader and the first exon of the mRNA.

annotations.label: outron
annotations.definition: A region of a primary transcript, that is removed via trans splicing.

annotations.label: natural_plasmid
annotations.definition: A plasmid that occurs naturally.

annotations.label: gene_trap_construct
annotations.definition: A gene trap construct is a type of engineered plasmid which is designed to integrate into a genome and produce a fusion transcript between exons of the gene into which it inserts and a reporter element in the construct. Gene traps contain a splice acceptor, do not contain promoter elements for the reporter, and are mutagenic. Gene traps may be bicistronic with the second cassette containing a promoter driving an a selectable marker.

annotations.label: promoter_trap_construct
annotations.definition: A promoter trap construct is a type of engineered plasmid which is designed to integrate into a genome and express a reporter when inserted in close proximity to a promoter element. Promoter traps typically do not contain promoter elements and are mutagenic.

annotations.label: enhancer_trap_construct
annotations.definition: An enhancer trap construct is a type of engineered plasmid which is designed to integrate into a genome and express a reporter when the expression from a basic minimal promoter is enhanced by genomic enhancer elements. Enhancer traps contain promoter elements and are not usually mutagenic.

annotations.label: PAC_end
annotations.definition: A region of sequence from the end of a PAC clone that may provide a highly specific marker.

annotations.label: RAPD
annotations.definition: RAPD is a 'PCR product' where a sequence variant is identified through the use of PCR with random primers.

annotations.label: shadow_enhancer
annotations.definition: An enhancer that drives the pattern of transcription and binds to the same TF as the primary enhancer, but is located in the intron of or on the far side of a neighboring gene.

annotations.label: SNV
annotations.definition: SNVs are single nucleotide positions in genomic DNA at which different sequence alternatives exist.

annotations.label: X_element_combinatorial_repeat
annotations.definition: An X element combinatorial repeat is a repeat region located between the X element and the telomere or adjacent Y' element.
annotations.comment:
X element combinatorial repeats contain Tbf1p binding sites,
and possible functions include a role in telomerase-independent telomere
maintenance via recombination or as a barrier against transcriptional
silencing. These are usually present as a combination of one or more of
several types of smaller elements (designated A, B, C, or D). This term was requested 2009-10-16 by Michel Dumontier, tracker id 2880747.

annotations.label: Y_prime_element
annotations.definition: A Y' element is a repeat region (SO:0000657) located adjacent to telomeric repeats or X element combinatorial repeats, either as a single copy or tandem repeat of two to four copies.
annotations.comment: This term was requested 2009-10-16 by Michel Dumontier, tracker id 2880747.

annotations.label: standard_draft
annotations.definition: The status of a whole genome sequence, where the data is minimally filtered or un-filtered, from any number of sequencing platforms, and is assembled into contigs. Genome sequence of this quality may harbour regions of poor quality and can be relatively incomplete.

annotations.label: high_quality_draft
annotations.definition: The status of a whole genome sequence, where overall coverage represents at least 90 percent of the genome.

annotations.label: improved_high_quality_draft
annotations.definition: The status of a whole genome sequence, where additional work has been performed, using either manual or automated methods, such as gap resolution.

annotations.label: annotation_directed_improved_draft
annotations.definition: The status of a whole genome sequence,where annotation, and verification of coding regions has occurred.

annotations.label: noncontiguous_finished
annotations.definition: The status of a whole genome sequence, where the assembly is high quality, closure approaches have been successful for most gaps, misassemblies and low quality regions.

annotations.label: finished_genome
annotations.definition: The status of a whole genome sequence, with less than 1 error per 100,000 base pairs.

annotations.label: intronic_regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A regulatory region that is part of an intron.
annotations.comment: Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: centromere_DNA_Element_I
annotations.definition: A centromere DNA Element I (CDEI) is a conserved region, part of the centromere, consisting of a consensus region composed of 8-11bp which enables binding by the centromere binding factor 1(Cbf1p).
annotations.comment: This term was requested 2009-10-16 by Michel Dumontier, tracker id 2880699.

annotations.label: centromere_DNA_Element_II
annotations.definition: A centromere DNA Element II (CDEII) is part a conserved region of the centromere, consisting of a consensus region that is AT-rich and ~ 75-100 bp in length.
annotations.comment: This term was requested 2009-10-16 by Michel Dumontier, tracker id 2880699.

annotations.label: centromere_DNA_Element_III
annotations.definition: A centromere DNA Element I (CDEI) is a conserved region, part of the centromere, consisting of a consensus region that consists of a 25-bp which enables binding by the centromere DNA binding factor 3 (CBF3) complex.
annotations.comment: This term was requested 2009-10-16 by Michel Dumontier, tracker id 2880699.

annotations.label: telomeric_repeat
annotations.definition: The telomeric repeat is a repeat region, part of the chromosome, which in yeast, is a G-rich terminal sequence of the form (TG(1-3))n or more precisely ((TG)(1-6)TG(2-3))n.
annotations.comment: The repeats are maintained by telomerase and there is generally 300 (+/-) 75 bp of TG(1-3) at a given end. Telomeric repeats function in completing chromosome replication and protecting the ends from degradation and end-to-end fusions. This term was requested 2009-10-16 by Michel Dumontier, tracker id 2880739.

annotations.label: X_element
annotations.definition: The X element is a conserved region, of the telomere, of ~475 bp that contains an ARS sequence and in most cases an Abf1p binding site.
annotations.comment:
Possible functions include roles in chromosomal segregation,
maintenance of chromosome stability, recombinational sequestering, or as a
barrier to transcriptional silencing. This term was requested 2009-10-16 by Michel Dumontier, tracker id 2880747.

From Janos Demeter: The only region shared by all chromosome ends, the X element core sequence is a small conserved element (~475 bp) that contains an ARS sequence and in most cases an Abf1p binding site. Between these is a GC-rich region nearly identical to the meiosis-specific regulatory sequence URS1.

annotations.label: YAC_end
annotations.definition: A region of sequence from the end of a YAC clone that may provide a highly specific marker.

annotations.label: whole_genome_sequence_status
annotations.definition: The status of whole genome sequence.
annotations.comment: This terms and children were added to SO in response to tracker request by Patrick Chain. The paper Genome Project Standards in a New Era of Sequencing. Science October 9th 2009, addresses these terms.

annotations.label: heritable_phenotypic_marker
annotations.definition: A biological_region characterized as a single heritable trait in a phenotype screen. The heritable phenotype may be mapped to a chromosome but generally has not been characterized to a specific gene locus.

annotations.label: peptide_collection
annotations.definition: A collection of peptide sequences.
annotations.comment: Term requested via tracker ID: 2910829.

annotations.label: high_identity_region
annotations.definition: An experimental feature with high sequence identity to another sequence.
annotations.comment: Requested by tracker ID: 2902685.

annotations.label: processed_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript for which no open reading frame has been identified and for which no other function has been determined.
annotations.comment: Ensembl and Vega also use this term name. Requested by Howard Deen of MGI.

annotations.label: assortment_derived_variation
annotations.definition: A chromosome variation derived from an event during meiosis.

annotations.label: reference_genome
annotations.definition: A collection of sequences (often chromosomes) taken as the standard for a given organism and genome assembly.

annotations.label: variant_genome
annotations.definition: A collection of sequences (often chromosomes) of an individual.

annotations.label: variant_collection
annotations.definition: A collection of one or more sequences of an individual.

annotations.label: alteration_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute of alteration of one or more chromosomes.

annotations.label: chromosomal_variation_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute of a change in the structure or number of a chromosomes.

annotations.label: intrachromosomal
annotations.definition: A change in chromosomes that occurs between two separate chromosomes.

annotations.label: interchromosomal
annotations.definition: A change in chromosomes that occurs between two sections of the same chromosome or between homologous chromosomes.

annotations.label: insertion_attribute
annotations.definition: A quality of a chromosomal insertion,.

annotations.label: tandem
annotations.definition: An insertion of extension of a tandem repeat.

annotations.label: direct
annotations.definition: A quality of an insertion where the insert is not in a cytologically inverted orientation.

annotations.label: inverted
annotations.definition: A quality of an insertion where the insert is in a cytologically inverted orientation.

annotations.label: free
annotations.definition: The quality of a duplication where the new region exists independently of the original.

annotations.label: inversion_attribute
annotations.definition: When a region of a chromosome is changed to the reverse order without duplication or deletion.

annotations.label: pericentric
annotations.definition: An inversion event that includes the centromere.

annotations.label: paracentric
annotations.definition: An inversion event that does not include the centromere.

annotations.label: translocaton_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute of a translocation, which is then a region of nucleotide sequence that has translocated to a new position. The observed adjacency of two previously separated regions.

annotations.label: reciprocal
annotations.definition: When translocation occurs between nonhomologous chromosomes and involved an equal exchange of genetic materials.

annotations.label: insertional
annotations.definition: When a translocation is simply moving genetic material from one chromosome to another.

annotations.label: duplication_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute of a duplication, which is an insertion which derives from, or is identical in sequence to, nucleotides present at a known location in the genome.

annotations.label: chromosomally_aberrant_genome
annotations.definition: When a genome contains an abnormal amount of chromosomes.

annotations.label: assembly_error_correction
annotations.definition: A region of sequence where the final nucleotide assignment differs from the original assembly due to an improvement that replaces a mistake.

annotations.label: base_call_error_correction
annotations.definition: A region of sequence where the final nucleotide assignment is different from that given by the base caller due to an improvement that replaces a mistake.

annotations.label: peptide_localization_signal
annotations.definition: A region of peptide sequence used to target the polypeptide molecule to a specific organelle.

annotations.label: nuclear_localization_signal
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that targets a polypeptide to the nucleus.

annotations.label: endosomal_localization_signal
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that targets a polypeptide to the endosome.

annotations.label: lysosomal_localization_signal
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that targets a polypeptide to the lysosome.

annotations.label: nuclear_export_signal
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that targets a polypeptide to he cytoplasm.

annotations.label: recombination_signal_sequence
annotations.definition: A region recognized by a recombinase.

annotations.label: cryptic_splice_site
annotations.definition: A splice site that is in part of the transcript not normally spliced. They occur via mutation or transcriptional error.

annotations.label: nuclear_rim_localization_signal
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that targets a polypeptide to the nuclear rim.

annotations.label: P_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: A P-element is a DNA transposon responsible for hybrid dysgenesis. P elements in this terminal inverted repeat (TIR) transposon superfamily have 31 bp perfect TIR and upon insertion duplicate an 8 bp sequence. It contains transposase that may lack the DDE domain.
annotations.comment: Moved from under DNA_transposon (SO:0000182) by Dave Sant as per request from GitHub issue #488 on June 25, 2020

annotations.label: functional_effect_variant
annotations.definition: A variant whereby the effect is evaluated with respect to a reference.
annotations.comment: Updated after request from Lea Starita, lea.starita@gmail.com from the NCBI.

annotations.label: structural_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes one or more structural features.

annotations.label: transcript_function_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which alters the functioning of a transcript with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: translational_product_function_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that affects the functioning of a translational product with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: level_of_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which alters the level of a transcript.

annotations.label: decreased_transcript_level_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that decreases the level of mature, spliced and processed RNA with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: increased_transcript_level_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that increases the level of mature, spliced and processed RNA with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: transcript_processing_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that affects the post transcriptional processing of a transcript with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: editing_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript processing variant whereby the process of editing is disrupted with respect to the reference.

annotations.label: polyadenylation_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes polyadenylation with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: transcript_stability_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that changes the stability of a transcript with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: decreased_transcript_stability_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that decreases transcript stability with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: increased_transcript_stability_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that increases transcript stability with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: transcription_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that changes alters the transcription of a transcript with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: rate_of_transcription_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the rate of transcription with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: increased_transcription_rate_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that increases the rate of transcription with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: decreased_transcription_rate_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that decreases the rate of transcription with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: translational_product_level_variant
annotations.definition: A functional variant that changes the translational product level with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: polypeptide_function_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which changes polypeptide functioning with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: decreased_translational_product_level
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which decreases the translational product level with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: increased_translational_product_level
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which increases the translational product level with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: polypeptide_gain_of_function_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which causes gain of polypeptide function with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: polypeptide_localization_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which changes the localization of a polypeptide with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: polypeptide_loss_of_function_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the loss of a polypeptide function with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: inactive_ligand_binding_site
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the inactivation of a ligand binding site with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: polypeptide_partial_loss_of_function
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes some but not all loss of polypeptide function with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: polypeptide_post_translational_processing_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes a change in post translational processing of the peptide with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: copy_number_change
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where copies of a feature (CNV) are either increased or decreased.

annotations.label: gene_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where the structure of the gene is changed.

annotations.label: gene_fusion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby a two genes have become joined.

annotations.label: regulatory_region_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located within a regulatory region.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Regulatory region variations - In regulatory region annotated by Ensembl.

annotations.label: stop_retained_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where at least one base in the terminator codon is changed, but the terminator remains.

annotations.label: splicing_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the process of splicing.

annotations.label: cryptic_splice_site_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant causing a new (functional) splice site.
annotations.comment: Added "cryptic splice site" as a synonym of cryptic_splice_site_variant at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: cryptic_splice_acceptor
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby a new splice site is created due to the activation of a new acceptor.
annotations.comment: Added "activated cryptic splice site acceptor" as a synonym of cryptic_splice_acceptor at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: cryptic_splice_donor
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby a new splice site is created due to the activation of a new donor.
annotations.comment: Added "activated cryptic splice donor" as a synonym of cryptic_splice_donor at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: exon_loss_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby an exon is lost from the transcript.
annotations.comment: Added "skipped exon" as a synonym of exon_loss_variant at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: intron_gain_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby an intron is gained by the processed transcript; usually a result of an alteration of the donor or acceptor.
annotations.comment: Added "included intron" and "retained intron" as synonyms of intron_gain_variant at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: splice_acceptor_variant
annotations.definition: A splice variant that changes the 2 base region at the 3' end of an intron.

annotations.label: splice_donor_variant
annotations.definition: A splice variant that changes the 2 base pair region at the 5' end of an intron.

annotations.label: transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the structure of the transcript.

annotations.label: complex_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant with a complex INDEL- Insertion or deletion that spans an exon/intron border or a coding sequence/UTR border.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Complex InDel - Insertion or deletion that spans an exon/intron border or a coding sequence/UTR border.

annotations.label: stop_lost
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where at least one base of the terminator codon (stop) is changed, resulting in an elongated transcript.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Stop lost - In coding sequence, resulting in the loss of a stop codon.

annotations.label: coding_sequence_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the coding sequence.

annotations.label: initiator_codon_variant
annotations.definition: A codon variant that changes at least one base of the first codon of a transcript.
annotations.comment: This is being used to annotate changes to the first codon of a transcript, when the first annotated codon is not to methionine. A variant is predicted to change the first amino acid of a translation irrespective of the fact that the underlying codon is an AUG. As such for transcripts with an incomplete CDS (sequence does not start with an AUG), it is still called.

annotations.label: missense_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Non-synonymous SNPs. SNPs that are located in the coding sequence and result in an amino acid change in the encoded peptide sequence. A change that causes a non_synonymous_codon can be more than 3 bases - for example 4 base substitution.

annotations.label: conservative_missense_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon is changed resulting in a codon that encodes for a different but similar amino acid. These variants may or may not be deleterious.

annotations.label: non_conservative_missense_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon is changed resulting in a codon that encodes for an amino acid with different biochemical properties.

annotations.label: stop_gained
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon is changed, resulting in a premature stop codon, leading to a shortened polypeptide.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Stop gained - In coding sequence, resulting in the gain of a stop codon (i.e. leading to a shortened peptide sequence).

annotations.label: frameshift_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, because the number of nucleotides inserted or deleted is not a multiple of three.
annotations.comment: EBI term:Frameshift variations - In coding sequence, resulting in a frameshift.

annotations.label: terminator_codon_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one of the bases in the terminator codon is changed.
annotations.comment: The terminal codon may be the terminator, or in an incomplete transcript the last available codon.

annotations.label: frame_restoring_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that reverts the sequence of a previous frameshift mutation back to the initial frame.

annotations.label: minus_1_frameshift_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, by shifting one base ahead.

annotations.label: minus_2_frameshift_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, by shifting two bases forward.

annotations.label: plus_1_frameshift_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, by shifting one base backward.

annotations.label: plus_2_frameshift_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, by shifting two bases backward.

annotations.label: transcript_secondary_structure_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant within a transcript that changes the secondary structure of the RNA product.

annotations.label: compensatory_transcript_secondary_structure_variant
annotations.definition: A secondary structure variant that compensate for the change made by a previous variant.

annotations.label: translational_product_structure_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant within the transcript that changes the structure of the translational product.

annotations.label: 3D_polypeptide_structure_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the resulting polypeptide structure.

annotations.label: complex_3D_structural_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the resulting polypeptide structure.

annotations.label: conformational_change_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant in the CDS region that causes a conformational change in the resulting polypeptide sequence.

annotations.label: complex_change_of_translational_product_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that changes the translational product with respect to the reference.

synonyms.text: complex change of translational product variant

annotations.label: polypeptide_sequence_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant with in the CDS that causes a change in the resulting polypeptide sequence.

annotations.label: amino_acid_deletion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant within a CDS resulting in the loss of an amino acid from the resulting polypeptide.

annotations.label: amino_acid_insertion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant within a CDS resulting in the gain of an amino acid to the resulting polypeptide.

annotations.label: amino_acid_substitution
annotations.definition: A sequence variant of a codon resulting in the substitution of one amino acid for another in the resulting polypeptide.

annotations.label: conservative_amino_acid_substitution
annotations.definition: A sequence variant of a codon causing the substitution of a similar amino acid for another in the resulting polypeptide.

annotations.label: non_conservative_amino_acid_substitution
annotations.definition: A sequence variant of a codon causing the substitution of a non conservative amino acid for another in the resulting polypeptide.

annotations.label: elongated_polypeptide
annotations.definition: An elongation of a polypeptide sequence deriving from a sequence variant extending the CDS.

annotations.label: elongated_polypeptide_C_terminal
annotations.definition: An elongation of a polypeptide sequence at the C terminus deriving from a sequence variant extending the CDS.

annotations.label: elongated_polypeptide_N_terminal
annotations.definition: An elongation of a polypeptide sequence at the N terminus deriving from a sequence variant extending the CDS.

annotations.label: elongated_in_frame_polypeptide_C_terminal
annotations.definition: A sequence variant with in the CDS that causes in frame elongation of the resulting polypeptide sequence at the C terminus.

synonyms.text: elongated in frame polypeptide C terminal

annotations.label: elongated_out_of_frame_polypeptide_C_terminal
annotations.definition: A sequence variant with in the CDS that causes out of frame elongation of the resulting polypeptide sequence at the C terminus.

synonyms.text: elongated polypeptide out of frame C terminal

annotations.label: elongated_in_frame_polypeptide_N_terminal_elongation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant with in the CDS that causes in frame elongation of the resulting polypeptide sequence at the N terminus.

synonyms.text: elongated in frame polypeptide N terminal

annotations.label: elongated_out_of_frame_polypeptide_N_terminal
annotations.definition: A sequence variant with in the CDS that causes out of frame elongation of the resulting polypeptide sequence at the N terminus.

synonyms.text: elongated out of frame N terminal

annotations.label: polypeptide_fusion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes a fusion of two polypeptide sequences.

annotations.label: polypeptide_truncation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant of the CD that causes a truncation of the resulting polypeptide.
annotations.comment: Added "protein truncation" as a synonym of polypeptide_truncation at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: inactive_catalytic_site
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the inactivation of a catalytic site with respect to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: non_coding_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant of a non coding RNA gene.
annotations.comment: Within non-coding gene - Located within a gene that does not code for a protein.

annotations.label: mature_miRNA_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant located with the sequence of the mature miRNA.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Within mature miRNA - Located within a microRNA.

annotations.label: NMD_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A variant in a transcript that is the target of nonsense-mediated mRNA decay.

annotations.label: UTR_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant that is located within the UTR.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_variant
annotations.definition: A UTR variant of the 5' UTR.
annotations.comment: EBI term: 5prime UTR variations - In 5prime UTR (untranslated region).

annotations.label: 3_prime_UTR_variant
annotations.definition: A UTR variant of the 3' UTR.
annotations.comment: EBI term 3prime UTR variations - In 3prime UTR.

annotations.label: incomplete_terminal_codon_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where at least one base of the final codon of an incompletely annotated transcript is changed.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Partial codon - Located within the final, incomplete codon of a transcript with a shortened coding sequence where the end is unknown.

annotations.label: intron_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant occurring within an intron.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Intronic variations - In intron.

annotations.label: intergenic_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located in the intergenic region, between genes.
annotations.comment: EBI term Intergenic variations - More than 5 kb either upstream or downstream of a transcript.

annotations.label: splice_site_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the first two or last two bases of an intron, or the 5th base from the start of the intron in the orientation of the transcript.
annotations.comment: EBI term - essential splice site - In the first 2 or the last 2 base pairs of an intron. The 5th base is on the donor (5') side of the intron. Updated to b in line with Cancer Genome Project at the Sanger.

annotations.label: splice_region_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant in which a change has occurred within the region of the splice site, either within 1-3 bases of the exon or 3-8 bases of the intron.
annotations.comment: EBI term: splice site - 1-3 bps into an exon or 3-8 bps into an intron.

annotations.label: upstream_gene_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located 5' of a gene.
annotations.comment: Different groups annotate up and downstream to different lengths. The subtypes are specific and are backed up with cross references.

annotations.label: downstream_gene_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located 3' of a gene.
annotations.comment: Different groups annotate up and downstream to different lengths. The subtypes are specific and are backed up with cross references.

annotations.label: 5KB_downstream_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located within 5 KB of the end of a gene.
annotations.comment: EBI term Downstream variations - Within 5 kb downstream of the 3prime end of a transcript.

annotations.label: 500B_downstream_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located within a half KB of the end of a gene.

annotations.label: 5KB_upstream_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located within 5KB 5' of a gene.
annotations.comment: EBI term Upstream variations - Within 5 kb upstream of the 5prime end of a transcript.

annotations.label: 2KB_upstream_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located within 2KB 5' of a gene.

annotations.label: rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes for ribosomal RNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: piRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes for an piwi associated RNA.
annotations.comment: Moved from ncRNA_gene to sncRNA_gene 27 April 2021 to be more consistent with the organization of the ncRNA branch of SO. Requested by FlyBase, moved by Dave Sant. See GitHub Issue #514.

annotations.label: RNase_P_RNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes an RNase P RNA.
annotations.comment: Moved under enzymatic_RNA_gene on 18 Nov 2021. See GitHub Issue #533.

annotations.label: RNase_MRP_RNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a RNase_MRP_RNA.
annotations.comment: Moved under enzymatic_RNA_gene on 18 Nov 2021. See GitHub Issue #533.

annotations.label: lincRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a long, intervening non-coding RNA.

annotations.label: mathematically_defined_repeat
annotations.definition: A mathematically defined repeat (MDR) is a experimental feature that is determined by querying overlapping oligomers of length k against a database of shotgun sequence data and identifying regions in the query sequence that exceed a statistically determined threshold of repetitiveness.
annotations.comment: Mathematically defined repeat regions are determined without regard to the biological origin of the repetitive region. The repeat units of a MDR are the overlapping oligomers of size k that were used to for the query. Tools that can annotate mathematically defined repeats include Tallymer (Kurtz et al 2008, BMC Genomics: 517) and RePS (Wang et al, Genome Res 12(5): 824-831.).

annotations.label: telomerase_RNA_gene
annotations.definition: A telomerase RNA gene is a non coding RNA gene the RNA product of which is a component of telomerase.

annotations.label: targeting_vector
annotations.definition: An engineered vector that is able to take part in homologous recombination in a host with the intent of introducing site specific genomic modifications.

annotations.label: genetic_marker
annotations.definition: A measurable sequence feature that varies within a population.

annotations.label: DArT_marker
annotations.definition: A genetic marker, discovered using Diversity Arrays Technology (DArT) technology.

annotations.label: kozak_sequence
annotations.definition: A kind of ribosome entry site, specific to Eukaryotic organisms that overlaps part of both 5' UTR and CDS sequence.

annotations.label: nested_transposon
annotations.definition: A transposon that is disrupted by the insertion of another element.

annotations.label: nested_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat that is disrupted by the insertion of another element.

annotations.label: inframe_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant which does not cause a disruption of the translational reading frame.

annotations.label: retinoic_acid_responsive_element
annotations.definition: A transcription factor binding site of variable direct repeats of the sequence PuGGTCA spaced by five nucleotides (DR5) found in the promoters of retinoic acid-responsive genes, to which retinoic acid receptors bind.

annotations.label: nucleotide_to_protein_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the nucleotide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with polypeptide residues.

annotations.label: nucleotide_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with nucleotide residues.
annotations.comment: See GO:0000166 : nucleotide binding.

annotations.label: metal_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with metal ions.
annotations.comment: See GO:0046872 : metal ion binding.

annotations.label: ligand_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with a small molecule such as a drug, or hormone.

annotations.label: nested_tandem_repeat
annotations.definition: An NTR is a nested repeat of two distinct tandem motifs interspersed with each other.
annotations.comment: Tracker ID: 3052459.

annotations.label: promoter_element
annotations.definition: An element that can exist within the promoter region of a gene.
annotations.comment: Mmoved from is_a: SO:0001055 transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region as per request from GREEKC initiative in August 2020.

annotations.label: core_eukaryotic_promoter_element
annotations.definition: An element that only exists within the promoter region of a eukaryotic gene.

annotations.label: RNA_polymerase_II_TATA_box
annotations.definition: A TATA box core promoter of a gene transcribed by RNA polymerase II.

annotations.label: RNA_polymerase_III_TATA_box
annotations.definition: A TATA box core promoter of a gene transcribed by RNA polymerase III.

annotations.label: BREd_motif
annotations.definition: A core RNA polymerase II promoter element with consensus (G/A)T(T/G/A)(T/A)(G/T)(T/G)(T/G).

annotations.label: DCE
annotations.definition: A discontinuous core element of RNA polymerase II transcribed genes, situated downstream of the TSS. It is composed of three sub elements: SI, SII and SIII.

annotations.label: DCE_SI
annotations.definition: A sub element of the DCE core promoter element, with consensus sequence CTTC.

annotations.label: DCE_SII
annotations.definition: A sub element of the DCE core promoter element with consensus sequence CTGT.

annotations.label: DCE_SIII
annotations.definition: A sub element of the DCE core promoter element with consensus sequence AGC.

annotations.label: proximal_promoter_element
annotations.definition: DNA segment that ranges from about -250 to -40 relative to +1 of RNA transcription start site, where sequence specific DNA-binding transcription factors binds, such as Sp1, CTF (CCAAT-binding transcription factor), and CBF (CCAAT-box binding factor).

annotations.label: RNApol_II_core_promoter
annotations.definition: The minimal portion of the promoter required to properly initiate transcription in RNA polymerase II transcribed genes.

annotations.label: distal_promoter_element
annotations.definition: A regulatory promoter element that is distal from the TSS.

annotations.label: bacterial_RNApol_promoter_sigma_70_element
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence to which bacterial RNA polymerase sigma 70 binds, to begin transcription.

annotations.label: bacterial_RNApol_promoter_sigma54_element
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence to which bacterial RNA polymerase sigma 54 binds, to begin transcription.

annotations.label: minus_12_signal
annotations.definition: A conserved region about 12-bp upstream of the start point of bacterial transcription units, involved with sigma factor 54.

annotations.label: minus_24_signal
annotations.definition: A conserved region about 24-bp upstream of the start point of bacterial transcription units, involved with sigma factor 54.

annotations.label: A_box_type_1
annotations.definition: An A box within an RNA polymerase III type 1 promoter.
annotations.comment: The A box can be found in the promoters of type 1 and type 2 (pol III) so sub-typing here allows the part of relationship of the subtypes to remain true.

annotations.label: A_box_type_2
annotations.definition: An A box within an RNA polymerase III type 2 promoter.
annotations.comment: The A box can be found in the promoters of type 1 and type 2 (pol III) so sub-typing here allows the part of relationship of the subtypes to remain true.

annotations.label: intermediate_element
annotations.definition: A core promoter region of RNA polymerase III type 1 promoters.

annotations.label: regulatory_promoter_element
annotations.definition: A promoter element that is not part of the core promoter, but provides the promoter with a specific regulatory region.

annotations.label: translation_regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A regulatory region that is involved in the control of the process of translation.

annotations.label: recombination_regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A regulatory region that is involved in the control of the process of recombination.

annotations.label: replication_regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A regulatory region that is involved in the control of the process of nucleotide replication.

annotations.label: sequence_motif
annotations.definition: A sequence motif is a nucleotide or amino-acid sequence pattern that may have biological significance.

annotations.label: experimental_feature_attribute
annotations.definition: An attribute of an experimentally derived feature.

annotations.label: score
annotations.definition: The score of an experimentally derived feature such as a p-value.

annotations.label: quality_value
annotations.definition: An experimental feature attribute that defines the quality of the feature in a quantitative way, such as a phred quality score.

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_recognition_site
annotations.definition: The nucleotide region (usually a palindrome) that is recognized by a restriction enzyme. This may or may not be equal to the restriction enzyme binding site.

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_cleavage_junction
annotations.definition: The boundary at which a restriction enzyme breaks the nucleotide sequence.

annotations.label: five_prime_restriction_enzyme_junction
annotations.definition: The restriction enzyme cleavage junction on the 5' strand of the nucleotide sequence.

annotations.label: three_prime_restriction_enzyme_junction
annotations.definition: The restriction enzyme cleavage junction on the 3' strand of the nucleotide sequence.

annotations.label: blunt_end_restriction_enzyme_cleavage_site
annotations.definition: A restriction enzyme recognition site that, when cleaved, results in no overhangs.

synonyms.text: blunt end restriction enzyme cleavage site

annotations.label: sticky_end_restriction_enzyme_cleavage_site
annotations.definition: A site where restriction enzymes can cleave that will produce an overhang or 'sticky end'.

synonyms.text: sticky end restriction enzyme cleavage site

annotations.label: blunt_end_restriction_enzyme_cleavage_junction
annotations.definition: A restriction enzyme cleavage site where both strands are cut at the same position.

synonyms.text: blunt end restriction enzyme cleavage site

annotations.label: single_strand_restriction_enzyme_cleavage_site
annotations.definition: A restriction enzyme cleavage site whereby only one strand is cut.

synonyms.text: single strand restriction enzyme cleavage site

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_single_strand_overhang
annotations.definition: A terminal region of DNA sequence where the end of the region is not blunt ended.

annotations.label: experimentally_defined_binding_region
annotations.definition: A region that has been implicated in binding although the exact coordinates of binding may be unknown.

annotations.label: ChIP_seq_region
annotations.definition: A region of sequence identified by CHiP seq technology to contain a protein binding site.

annotations.label: ASPE_primer
annotations.definition: "A primer containing an SNV at the 3' end for accurate genotyping.

annotations.label: dCAPS_primer
annotations.definition: A primer with one or more mismatches to the DNA template corresponding to a position within a restriction enzyme recognition site.

annotations.label: histone_modification
annotations.definition: Histone modification is a post translationally modified region whereby residues of the histone protein are modified by methylation, acetylation, phosphorylation, ubiquitination, sumoylation, citrullination, or ADP-ribosylation.

annotations.label: histone_methylation_site
annotations.definition: A histone modification site where the modification is the methylation of the residue.

annotations.label: histone_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A histone modification where the modification is the acylation of the residue.

annotations.label: H3K9_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 9th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H3K14_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 14th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H3K4_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification, whereby the 4th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 protein is mono-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K4_trimethylation
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 4th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 protein is tri-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K9_trimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 9th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is tri-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K27_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 27th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is mono-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K27_trimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 27th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is tri-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K79_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 79th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is mono- methylated.

annotations.label: H3K79_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 79th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is di-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K79_trimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 79th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is tri-methylated.

annotations.label: H4K20_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 20th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H4histone protein is mono-methylated.

annotations.label: H2BK5_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 5th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2B protein is methylated.

annotations.label: ISRE
annotations.definition: An ISRE is a transcriptional cis regulatory region, containing the consensus region: YAGTTTC(A/T)YTTTYCC, responsible for increased transcription via interferon binding.
annotations.comment: Term requested via tracker (2981725) by Alan Ruttenberg, April 2010. It has been described as both an enhancer and a promoter, so the parent is the more general term. Moved from is_a SO:0001055 transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to SO:0000235 TF_binding_site after Colin Logie pointed out that this is a consensus sequence where transcription factors bind, GREEKC Jan 21, 2021.

annotations.label: histone_ubiqitination_site
annotations.definition: A histone modification site where ubiquitin may be added.

annotations.label: H2B_ubiquitination_site
annotations.definition: A histone modification site on H2B where ubiquitin may be added.

annotations.label: H3K18_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 18th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H3K23_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification, whereby the 23rd residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: epigenetically_modified_region
annotations.definition: A biological DNA region implicated in epigenomic changes caused by mechanisms other than changes in the underlying DNA sequence. This includes, nucleosomal histone post-translational modifications, nucleosome depletion to render DNA accessible and post-replicational base modifications such as cytosine modification.
annotations.comment: Moved from is_a biological_region (SO:0001411) to is_a regulatory_region (SO:0005836) on 11 Feb 2021. GREEKC members pointed out that this would be a more appropriate location. See GitHub Issue #530. 11 Feb 2021 updated definition along with addition of epigenomically_modified_region (SO:0002332). Epigenetically modified region is now not inherited while epigenomically modified region is not annotated as inherited. See GitHub Issue #532 and issue #534.

annotations.label: H3K36_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 36th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is mono-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K36_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 36th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is dimethylated.

annotations.label: H3K36_trimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 36th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is tri-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K4_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 4th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is di-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K27_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 27th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is di-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K9_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 9th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is mono-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K9_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 9th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein may be dimethylated.

annotations.label: H4K16_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 16th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H4 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H4K5_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 5th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H4 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H4K8_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 8th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H4 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H3K27_methylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 27th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is methylated.

annotations.label: H3K36_methylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 36th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is methylated.

annotations.label: H3K4_methylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification, whereby the 4th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 protein is methylated.

annotations.label: H3K79_methylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 79th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is methylated.

annotations.label: H3K9_methylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 9th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is methylated.

annotations.label: histone_acylation_region
annotations.definition: A histone modification, whereby the histone protein is acylated at multiple sites in a region.

annotations.label: H4K_acylation_region
annotations.definition: A region of the H4 histone whereby multiple lysines are acylated.

annotations.label: gene_with_non_canonical_start_codon
annotations.definition: A gene with a start codon other than AUG.
annotations.comment: Requested by flybase, Dec 2010.

synonyms.text: gene with non canonical start codon

annotations.label: gene_with_start_codon_CUG
annotations.definition: A gene with a translational start codon of CUG.
annotations.comment: Requested by flybase, Dec 2010.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_gene_segment
annotations.definition: A gene segment which when incorporated by somatic recombination in the final gene transcript results in a nonfunctional product.

annotations.label: copy_number_gain
annotations.definition: A sequence alteration whereby the copy number of a given regions is greater than the reference sequence.

annotations.label: copy_number_loss
annotations.definition: A sequence alteration whereby the copy number of a given region is less than the reference sequence.

annotations.label: UPD
annotations.definition: Uniparental disomy is a sequence_alteration where a diploid individual receives two copies for all or part of a chromosome from one parent and no copies of the same chromosome or region from the other parent.

annotations.label: maternal_uniparental_disomy
annotations.definition: Uniparental disomy is a sequence_alteration where a diploid individual receives two copies for all or part of a chromosome from the mother and no copies of the same chromosome or region from the father.

annotations.label: paternal_uniparental_disomy
annotations.definition: Uniparental disomy is a sequence_alteration where a diploid individual receives two copies for all or part of a chromosome from the father and no copies of the same chromosome or region from the mother.

annotations.label: open_chromatin_region
annotations.definition: A DNA sequence that in the normal state of the chromosome corresponds to an unfolded, un-complexed stretch of double-stranded DNA.
annotations.comment: Requested by John Calley 3125900.

annotations.label: SL3_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL3 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL3 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL4_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL4 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL4 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL5_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL5 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL5 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL6_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL6 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL6 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL7_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL7 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL7 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL8_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL8 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL8 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL9_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL9 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL9 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL10_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL10 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL10 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL11_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL11 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL11 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: SL12_acceptor_site
annotations.definition: A SL2_acceptor_site which appends the SL12 RNA leader sequence to the 5' end of an mRNA. SL12 acceptor sites occur in genes in internal segments of polycistronic transcripts.

annotations.label: duplicated_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene that arose via gene duplication. Generally duplicated pseudogenes have the same structure as the original gene, including intron-exon structure and some regulatory sequence.

annotations.label: unitary_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene, deactivated from original state by mutation, fixed in a population,where the ortholog in a reference species such as mouse remains functional.
annotations.comment: This is different from a non processed pseudogene because the gene was not duplicated. An example is the L-gulono-lactone oxidase pseudogene in primates.

annotations.label: non_processed_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene that arose from a means other than retrotransposition. A pseudogene created via genomic duplication of a functional protein-coding parent gene followed by accumulation of deleterious mutations.

annotations.label: variant_quality
annotations.definition: A dependent entity that inheres in a bearer, a sequence variant.

annotations.label: variant_origin
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a variant by virtue of its origin.

annotations.label: variant_frequency
annotations.definition: A physical quality which inheres to the variant by virtue of the number instances of the variant within a population.

annotations.label: unique_variant
annotations.definition: A physical quality which inheres to the variant by virtue of the number instances of the variant within a population.

annotations.label: rare_variant
annotations.definition: When a variant from the genomic sequence is rarely found in the general population. The threshold for 'rare' varies between studies.

annotations.label: polymorphic_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that affects one of several possible alleles at that location, such as the major histocompatibility complex (MHC) genes.

annotations.label: common_variant
annotations.definition: When a variant from the genomic sequence is commonly found in the general population.

annotations.label: fixed_variant
annotations.definition: When a variant has become fixed in the population so that it is now the only variant.

annotations.label: variant_phenotype
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a variant by virtue of its phenotype.

annotations.label: benign_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that does not affect the function of the gene or cause disease.

annotations.label: disease_associated_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that has been found to be associated with disease.

annotations.label: disease_causing_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that has been found to cause disease.

annotations.label: lethal_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where the mutated gene product does not allow for one or more basic functions necessary for survival.

annotations.label: quantitative_variant
annotations.definition: A variant within a gene that contributes to a quantitative trait such as height or weight.

annotations.label: maternal_variant
annotations.definition: A variant in the genetic material inherited from the mother.

annotations.label: paternal_variant
annotations.definition: A variant in the genetic material inherited from the father.

annotations.label: somatic_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that has arisen after splitting of the embryo, resulting in the variant being found in only some of the tissues or cells of the body.

annotations.label: germline_variant
annotations.definition: A variant present in the embryo that is carried by every cell in the body.

annotations.label: pedigree_specific_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that is found only by individuals that belong to the same pedigree.

annotations.label: population_specific_variant
annotations.definition: A variant found within only speficic populations.

annotations.label: de_novo_variant
annotations.definition: A variant arising in the offspring that is not found in either of the parents.

annotations.label: TF_binding_site_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located within a transcription factor binding site.

annotations.label: complex_structural_alteration
annotations.definition: A structural sequence alteration or rearrangement encompassing one or more genome fragments, with 4 or more breakpoints.

annotations.label: structural_alteration
annotations.definition: An alteration of the genome that leads to a change in the structure of one or more chromosomes.

annotations.label: loss_of_heterozygosity
annotations.definition: A functional variant whereby the sequence alteration causes a loss of function of one allele of a gene.

annotations.label: splice_donor_5th_base_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes a change at the 5th base pair after the start of the intron in the orientation of the transcript.

annotations.label: U_box
annotations.definition: An U-box is a conserved T-rich region upstream of a retroviral polypurine tract that is involved in PPT primer creation during reverse transcription.

annotations.label: mating_type_region
annotations.definition: A specialized region in the genomes of some yeast and fungi, the genes of which regulate mating type.

annotations.label: paired_end_fragment
annotations.definition: An assembly region that has been sequenced from both ends resulting in a read_pair (mate_pair).

annotations.label: exon_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes exon sequence.

annotations.label: non_coding_transcript_exon_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes non-coding exon sequence in a non-coding transcript.

annotations.label: clone_end
annotations.definition: A read from an end of the clone sequence.

annotations.label: point_centromere
annotations.definition: A point centromere is a relatively small centromere (about 125 bp DNA) in discrete sequence, found in some yeast including S. cerevisiae.

annotations.label: regional_centromere
annotations.definition: A regional centromere is a large modular centromere found in fission yeast and higher eukaryotes. It consist of a central core region flanked by inverted inner and outer repeat regions.

annotations.label: regional_centromere_central_core
annotations.definition: A conserved region within the central region of a modular centromere, where the kinetochore is formed.

annotations.label: centromeric_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat region found within the modular centromere.

annotations.label: regional_centromere_inner_repeat_region
annotations.definition: The inner inverted repeat region of a modular centromere and part of the central core surrounding a non-conserved central region. This region is adjacent to the central core, on each chromosome arm.

annotations.label: regional_centromere_outer_repeat_region
annotations.definition: The heterochromatic outer repeat region of a modular centromere. These repeats exist in tandem arrays on both chromosome arms.

annotations.label: tasiRNA
annotations.definition: The sequence of a 21 nucleotide double stranded, polyadenylated non coding RNA, transcribed from the TAS gene.

annotations.label: tasiRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a tasiRNA.

annotations.label: increased_polyadenylation_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript processing variant whereby polyadenylation of the encoded transcript is increased with respect to the reference.
annotations.comment: Term requested by M. Dumontier, June 1 2011.

annotations.label: decreased_polyadenylation_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript processing variant whereby polyadenylation of the encoded transcript is decreased with respect to the reference.
annotations.comment: Term requested by M. Dumontier, June 1 2011.

annotations.label: DDB_box
annotations.definition: A conserved polypeptide motif that mediates protein-protein interaction and defines adaptor proteins for DDB1/cullin 4 ubiquitin ligases.
annotations.comment: Note: PMID:18794354 describes the DDB box, and has lots of alignments, but doesn't actually come out with a consensus sequence.

annotations.label: destruction_box
