annotations.definition: A conserved polypeptide motif that can be recognized by both Fizzy/Cdc20- and FZR/Cdh1-activated anaphase-promoting complex/cyclosome (APC/C) and targets a protein for ubiquitination and subsequent degradation by the APC/C. The consensus sequence is RXXLXXXXN.

annotations.label: ER_retention_signal
annotations.definition: A C-terminal tetrapeptide motif that mediates retention of a protein in (or retrieval to) the endoplasmic reticulum. In mammals the sequence is KDEL, and in fungi HDEL or DDEL.

annotations.label: KEN_box
annotations.definition: A conserved polypeptide motif that can be recognized by FZR/Cdh1-activated anaphase-promoting complex/cyclosome (APC/C) and targets a protein for ubiquitination and subsequent degradation by the APC/C. The consensus sequence is KENXXXN.

annotations.label: mitochondrial_targeting_signal
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that targets a polypeptide to the mitochondrion.

annotations.label: signal_anchor
annotations.definition: A signal sequence that is not cleaved from the polypeptide. Anchors a Type II membrane protein to the membrane.

annotations.label: PIP_box
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that mediates binding to PCNA. The consensus sequence is QXX(hh)XX(aa), where (h) denotes residues with moderately hydrophobic side chains and (a) denotes residues with highly hydrophobic aromatic side chains.

annotations.label: phosphorylation_site
annotations.definition: A post-translationally modified region in which residues of the protein are modified by phosphorylation.

annotations.label: transmembrane_helix
annotations.definition: A region that traverses the lipid bilayer and adopts a helical secondary structure.

annotations.label: vacuolar_sorting_signal
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that targets a polypeptide to the vacuole.

annotations.label: coding_variant_quality
annotations.definition: An attribute of a coding genomic variant.

annotations.label: synonymous
annotations.definition: A variant that does not lead to any change in the amino acid sequence.

annotations.label: non_synonymous
annotations.definition: A variant that leads to the change of an amino acid within the protein.

annotations.label: inframe
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a sequence that contains a mutation involving the deletion or insertion of one or more bases, where this number is divisible by 3.

annotations.label: protein_altering_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence_variant which is predicted to change the protein encoded in the coding sequence.

annotations.label: synonymous_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where there is no resulting change to the encoded amino acid.
annotations.comment: EBI term: Synonymous SNPs - In coding sequence, not resulting in an amino acid change (i.e. silent mutation).\nThis term is sometimes used synonomously with the more general term 'silent mutation', although a silent mutation may occur in non coding sequence. The best practice is to annotate to the most specific term.

annotations.label: inframe_indel
annotations.definition: A coding sequence variant where the change does not alter the frame of the transcript.

annotations.label: inframe_insertion
annotations.definition: An inframe non synonymous variant that inserts bases into in the coding sequence.

annotations.label: inframe_deletion
annotations.definition: An inframe non synonymous variant that deletes bases from the coding sequence.

annotations.label: conservative_inframe_insertion
annotations.definition: An inframe increase in cds length that inserts one or more codons into the coding sequence between existing codons.

annotations.label: disruptive_inframe_insertion
annotations.definition: An inframe increase in cds length that inserts one or more codons into the coding sequence within an existing codon.

annotations.label: conservative_inframe_deletion
annotations.definition: An inframe decrease in cds length that deletes one or more entire codons from the coding sequence but does not change any remaining codons.

annotations.label: disruptive_inframe_deletion
annotations.definition: An inframe decrease in cds length that deletes bases from the coding sequence starting within an existing codon.

annotations.label: mRNA_read
annotations.definition: A sequencer read of an mRNA substrate.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience June, 2011.

annotations.label: genomic_DNA_read
annotations.definition: A sequencer read of a genomic DNA substrate.

annotations.label: mRNA_contig
annotations.definition: A contig composed of mRNA_reads.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience June, 2011.

annotations.label: AFLP_fragment
annotations.definition: A PCR product obtained by applying the AFLP technique, based on a restriction enzyme digestion of genomic DNA and an amplification of the resulting fragments.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience June, 2011.

annotations.label: protein_hmm_match
annotations.definition: A match to a protein HMM such as pfam.

annotations.label: immunoglobulin_region
annotations.definition: A region of immunoglobulin sequence, either constant or variable.

annotations.label: V_region
annotations.definition: The variable region of an immunoglobulin polypeptide sequence.

annotations.label: C_region
annotations.definition: The constant region of an immunoglobulin polypeptide sequence.

annotations.label: N_region
annotations.definition: Extra nucleotides inserted between rearranged immunoglobulin segments.

annotations.label: S_region
annotations.definition: The switch region of immunoglobulin heavy chains; it is involved in the rearrangement of heavy chain DNA leading to the expression of a different immunoglobulin classes from the same B-cell.

annotations.label: mobile_element_insertion
annotations.definition: A kind of insertion where the inserted sequence is a mobile element.
annotations.comment: Requested by the EBI.

annotations.label: novel_sequence_insertion
annotations.definition: An insertion the sequence of which cannot be mapped to the reference genome.
annotations.comment: Requested by the NCBI.

annotations.label: CSL_response_element
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence GTGRGAA, bound by CSL (CBF1/RBP-JK/Suppressor of Hairless/LAG-1) transcription factors.

annotations.label: GATA_box
annotations.definition: A GATA transcription factor element containing the consensus sequence WGATAR (in which W indicates A/T and R indicates A/G).
annotations.comment: Changed to is_a SO:0001055 transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region from core_eukaryotic_promoter_element SO:0001660 after Ruth Lovering from GREEKC initiative pointed out that GATA boxes are frequently in enhancer regions, Dave Sant Aug 2020. Moved from is_a SO:0001055 transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to SO:0000235 TF_binding_site after Colin Logie pointed out that this is a consensus sequence where transcription factors bind, GREEKC Jan 21, 2021.

annotations.label: polymorphic_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene in the reference genome, though known to be intact in the genomes of other individuals of the same species. The annotation process has confirmed that the pseudogenisation event is not a genomic sequencing error.
annotations.comment: This terms is used by Ensembl and Vega. Pseudogene owing to a SNP/DIP but in other individuals/haplotypes/strains the gene is translated.

annotations.label: AP_1_binding_site
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence TGACTCA, bound by AP-1 and related transcription factors.

annotations.label: CRE
annotations.definition:
MERGED DEFINITION:
TARGET DEFINITION: A promoter element with consensus sequence TGACGTCA; bound by the ATF/CREB family of transcription factors.
--------------------
SOURCE DEFINITION: A promoter element that contains a core sequence TGACGT, bound by a protein complex that regulates transcription of genes encoding PKA pathway components.
annotations.comment: New synonym Atf1/Pcr1 recognition motif added in response to Antonia Lock GitHub Issue Request #437, PMID:15716492

annotations.label: CuRE
annotations.definition: A promoter element bound by copper ion-sensing transcription factors such as S. cerevisiae Mac1p or S. pombe Cuf1; the consensus sequence is HTHNNGCTGD (more specifically TTTGCKCR in budding yeast).

annotations.label: DRE
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence CGWGGWNGMM, bound by transcription factors related to RecA and found in promoters of genes expressed following several types of DNA damage or inhibition of DNA synthesis.

annotations.label: FLEX_element
annotations.definition: A promoter element that has consensus sequence GTAAACAAACAAAM and contains a heptameric core GTAAACA, bound by transcription factors with a forkhead DNA-binding domain.

annotations.label: forkhead_motif
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence TTTRTTTACA, bound by transcription factors with a forkhead DNA-binding domain.

annotations.label: homol_D_box
annotations.definition: A core promoter element that has the consensus sequence CAGTCACA (or its inverted form TGTGACTG), and plays the role of a TATA box in promoters that do not contain a canonical TATA sequence.

annotations.label: homol_E_box
annotations.definition: A core promoter element that has the consensus sequence ACCCTACCCT (or its inverted form AGGGTAGGGT), and is found near the homol D box in some promoters that use a homol D box instead of a canonical TATA sequence.

annotations.label: HSE
annotations.definition: A promoter element that consists of at least three copies of the pentanucleotide NGAAN, bound by the heat shock transcription factor HSF.

annotations.label: iron_repressed_GATA_element
annotations.definition: A GATA promoter element with consensus sequence WGATAA, found in promoters of genes repressed in the presence of iron.
annotations.comment: The synonym IDP (GATA) is found in an annotation but un-traced as far as literature goes.

annotations.label: mating_type_M_box
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence ACAAT, found in promoters of mating type M-specific genes in fission yeast and bound by the transcription factor Mat1-Mc.
annotations.comment: Note that this should not be confused with the M-box that has consensus sequence CATGTG and is bound by bHLH transcription factors such as MITF.

annotations.label: androgen_response_element
annotations.definition: A non-palindromic sequence found in the promoters of genes whose expression is regulated in response to androgen.

annotations.label: smFISH_probe
annotations.definition: A smFISH is a probe that binds RNA in a single molecule in situ hybridization experiment.

annotations.label: MCB
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence ACGCGT, bound by the transcription factor complex MBF (MCB-binding factor) and found in promoters of genes expressed during the G1/S transition of the cell cycle.

annotations.label: CCAAT_motif
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence CCAAT, bound by a protein complex that represses transcription in response to low iron levels.

annotations.label: Ace2_UAS
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence CCAGCC, bound by the fungal transcription factor Ace2.

annotations.label: TR_box
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence TTCTTTGTTY, bound an HMG-box transcription factor such as S. pombe Ste11, and found in promoters of genes up-regulated early in meiosis.

annotations.label: STREP_motif
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence CCCCTC, bound by the PKA-responsive zinc finger transcription factor Rst2.

synonyms.text: stress-starvation response element of Schizosaccharomyces pombe

annotations.label: rDNA_intergenic_spacer_element
annotations.definition: A DNA motif that contains a core consensus sequence AGGTAAGGGTAATGCAC, is found in the intergenic regions of rDNA repeats, and is bound by an RNA polymerase I transcription termination factor (e.g. S. pombe Reb1). The S. pombe telomeric repeat consensus is TTAC(0-1)A(0-1)G(1-8).

annotations.label: sterol_regulatory_element
annotations.definition: A 10-bp promoter element bound by sterol regulatory element binding proteins (SREBPs), found in promoters of genes involved in sterol metabolism. Many variants of the sequence ATCACCCCAC function as SREs.

annotations.label: GT_dinucleotide_repeat
annotations.definition: A dinucleotide repeat region composed of GT repeating elements.
annotations.comment: paper:PMID:16043634.

annotations.label: GTT_trinucleotide_repeat
annotations.definition: A trinucleotide repeat region composed of GTT repeating elements.

annotations.label: Sap1_recognition_motif
annotations.definition: A DNA motif to which the S. pombe Sap1 protein binds. The consensus sequence is 5'-TARGCAGNTNYAACGMG-3'; it is found at the mating type locus, where it is important for mating type switching, and at replication fork barriers in rDNA repeats.

annotations.label: CDRE_motif
annotations.definition: An RNA polymerase II promoter element found in the promoters of genes regulated by calcineurin. The consensus sequence is GNGGCKCA.

annotations.label: BAC_read_contig
annotations.definition: A contig of BAC reads.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience December, 2011.

annotations.label: candidate_gene
annotations.definition: A gene suspected of being involved in the expression of a trait.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience December, 2011.

annotations.label: positional_candidate_gene
annotations.definition: A candidate gene whose association with a trait is based on the gene's location on a chromosome.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience December, 2011.

annotations.label: functional_candidate_gene
annotations.definition: A candidate gene whose function has something in common biologically with the trait under investigation.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience December, 2011.

annotations.label: enhancerRNA
annotations.definition: A short ncRNA that is transcribed from an enhancer. May have a regulatory function.

annotations.label: PCB
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence GNAACR, bound by the transcription factor complex PBF (PCB-binding factor) and found in promoters of genes expressed during the M/G1 transition of the cell cycle.

annotations.label: rearrangement_region
annotations.definition: A region of a chromosome, where the chromosome has undergone a large structural rearrangement that altered the genome organization. There is no longer synteny to the reference genome.
annotations.comment: NCBI definition: An orphan rearrangement between chromosomal location observed in isolation.

annotations.label: interchromosomal_breakpoint
annotations.definition: A rearrangement breakpoint between two different chromosomes.

annotations.label: intrachromosomal_breakpoint
annotations.definition: A rearrangement breakpoint within the same chromosome.

annotations.label: unassigned_supercontig
annotations.definition: A supercontig that is not been assigned to any ultracontig during a genome assembly project.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience January, 2012.

annotations.label: partial_genomic_sequence_assembly
annotations.definition: A partial DNA sequence assembly of a chromosome or full genome, which contains gaps that are filled with N's.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience January, 2012.

annotations.label: lncRNA
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA generally longer than 200 nucleotides that cannot be classified as any other ncRNA subtype. Similar to mRNAs, lncRNAs are mainly transcribed by RNA polymerase II, are often capped by 7-methyl guanosine at their 5' ends, polyadenylated at their 3' ends and may be spliced.
annotations.comment: Updated the definition of lncRNA (SO:0001877) from "A non-coding RNA over 200nucleotides in length." to "A non-coding RNA generally longer than 200 nucleotides that cannot be classified as any other ncRNA subtype. Similar to mRNAs, lncRNAs are mainly transcribed by RNA polymerase II, are often capped by 7-methyl guanosine at their 5' ends, polyadenylated at their 3' ends and may be spliced." See GitHub Issue #575

annotations.label: feature_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that falls entirely or partially within a genomic feature.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: feature_ablation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant, caused by an alteration of the genomic sequence, where the deletion, is greater than the extent of the underlying genomic features.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: feature_amplification
annotations.definition: A sequence variant, caused by an alteration of the genomic sequence, where the structural change, an amplification of sequence, is greater than the extent of the underlying genomic features.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: feature_translocation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant, caused by an alteration of the genomic sequence, where the structural change, a translocation, is greater than the extent of the underlying genomic features.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: feature_fusion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant, caused by an alteration of the genomic sequence, where a deletion fuses genomic features.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: transcript_translocation
annotations.definition: A feature translocation where the region contains a transcript.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: regulatory_region_translocation
annotations.definition: A feature translocation where the region contains a regulatory region.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: TFBS_translocation
annotations.definition: A feature translocation where the region contains a transcription factor binding site.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: transcript_fusion
annotations.definition: A feature fusion where the deletion brings together transcript regions.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: regulatory_region_fusion
annotations.definition: A feature fusion where the deletion brings together regulatory regions.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: TFBS_fusion
annotations.definition: A fusion where the deletion brings together transcription factor binding sites.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: transcript_amplification
annotations.definition: A feature amplification of a region containing a transcript.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: transcript_regulatory_region_fusion
annotations.definition: A feature fusion where the deletion brings together a regulatory region and a transcript region.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: regulatory_region_amplification
annotations.definition: A feature amplification of a region containing a regulatory region.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: TFBS_amplification
annotations.definition: A feature amplification of a region containing a transcription factor binding site.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: transcript_ablation
annotations.definition: A feature ablation whereby the deleted region includes a transcript feature.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: regulatory_region_ablation
annotations.definition: A feature ablation whereby the deleted region includes a regulatory region.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: TFBS_ablation
annotations.definition: A feature ablation whereby the deleted region includes a transcription factor binding site.
annotations.comment: Created in conjunction with the EBI.

annotations.label: transposable_element_CDS
annotations.definition: A CDS that is part of a transposable element.

annotations.label: transposable_element_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene contained within a transposable element.

annotations.label: dg_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat region which is part of the regional centromere outer repeat region.
annotations.comment: For the S. pombe project - requested by Val Wood.

annotations.label: dh_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat region which is part of the regional centromere outer repeat region.
annotations.comment: For the S. pombe project - requested by Val Wood.

annotations.label: AACCCT_box
annotations.definition: A conserved 17-bp sequence (5'-ATCA(C/A)AACCCTAACCCT-3') commonly present upstream of the start site of histone transcription units functioning as a transcription factor binding site.

annotations.label: splice_region
annotations.definition: A region surrounding a cis_splice site, either within 1-3 bases of the exon or 3-8 bases of the intron.

annotations.label: antisense_lncRNA
annotations.definition: Non-coding RNA transcribed from the opposite DNA strand compared with other transcripts and overlap in part with sense RNA.
annotations.comment: Relationship is_a SO:0000644 antisense_RNA added 23 April 2021. See GitHub Issue #443

annotations.label: regional_centromere_outer_repeat_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that is transcribed from the outer repeat region of a regional centromere.

synonyms.text: regional centromere outer repeat region transcript

annotations.label: feature_truncation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the reduction of a genomic feature, with regard to the reference sequence.

annotations.label: feature_elongation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the extension of a genomic feature, with regard to the reference sequence.

annotations.label: internal_feature_elongation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the extension of a genomic feature from within the feature rather than from the terminus of the feature, with regard to the reference sequence.

annotations.label: frameshift_elongation
annotations.definition: A frameshift variant that causes the translational reading frame to be extended relative to the reference feature.

annotations.label: frameshift_truncation
annotations.definition: A frameshift variant that causes the translational reading frame to be shortened relative to the reference feature.

annotations.label: copy_number_increase
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where copies of a feature are increased relative to the reference.

annotations.label: copy_number_decrease
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where copies of a feature are decreased relative to the reference.

annotations.label: bacterial_RNApol_promoter_sigma_ecf_element
annotations.definition: A bacterial promoter with sigma ecf factor binding dependency. This is a type of bacterial promoters that requires a sigma ECF factor to bind to identified -10 and -35 sequence regions in order to mediate binding of the RNA polymerase to the promoter region as part of transcription initiation.
annotations.comment: Requested by Kevin Clancy - invitrogen -May 2012.

annotations.label: rDNA_replication_fork_barrier
annotations.definition: A DNA motif that is found in eukaryotic rDNA repeats, and is a site of replication fork pausing.
annotations.comment: Requested by Midori - June 2012.

annotations.label: transcription_start_cluster
annotations.definition: A region defined by a cluster of experimentally determined transcription starting sites.

annotations.label: CAGE_tag
annotations.definition: A CAGE tag is a sequence tag hat corresponds to 5' ends of mRNA at cap sites, produced by cap analysis gene expression and used to identify transcriptional start sites.

annotations.label: CAGE_cluster
annotations.definition: A kind of transcription_initiation_cluster defined by the clustering of CAGE tags on a sequence region.

annotations.label: 5_methylcytosine
annotations.definition: A cytosine methylated at the 5 carbon.

annotations.label: 4_methylcytosine
annotations.definition: A cytosine methylated at the 4 nitrogen.

annotations.label: N6_methyladenine
annotations.definition: An adenine methylated at the 6 nitrogen.

annotations.label: mitochondrial_contig
annotations.definition: A contig of mitochondria derived sequences.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience, October, 2012.

annotations.label: mitochondrial_supercontig
annotations.definition: A scaffold composed of mitochondrial contigs.

annotations.label: TERRA
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA transcript, derived from the transcription of the telomere. These transcripts contain G rich telomeric RNA repeats and RNA tracts corresponding to adjacent subtelomeric sequences. They are 100-9000 bases long.
annotations.comment: Telomeric transcription has been documented in mammals, birds, fish, plants and yeast. Requested by Antonia Lock, October 2012.

annotations.label: ARRET
annotations.definition: A non coding RNA transcript, complementary to subtelomeric tract of TERRA transcript but devoid of the repeats.
annotations.comment: Telomeric transcription has been documented in mammals, birds, fish, plants and yeast. Requested by Antonia Lock, October 2012.

annotations.label: ARIA
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA transcript, derived from the transcription of the telomere. These transcripts consist of C rich repeats.
annotations.comment: Telomeric transcription has been documented in mammals, birds, fish, plants and yeast. Requested by Antonia Lock, October 2012.

annotations.label: anti_ARRET
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA transcript, derived from the transcription of the telomere. These transcripts are antisense of ARRET transcripts.
annotations.comment: Telomeric transcription has been documented in mammals, birds, fish, plants and yeast. Requested by Antonia Lock, October 2012.

annotations.label: telomeric_transcript
annotations.definition: A non-coding transcript derived from the transcript of the telomere.

annotations.label: distal_duplication
annotations.definition: A duplication of the distal region of a chromosome.
annotations.comment: This term is used by Complete Genomics in the structural variant analysis files.

annotations.label: mitochondrial_DNA_read
annotations.definition: A sequencer read of a mitochondrial DNA sample.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience, October, 2012.

annotations.label: chloroplast_DNA_read
annotations.definition: A sequencer read of a chloroplast DNA sample.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience, October, 2012.

annotations.label: consensus_gDNA
annotations.definition: Genomic DNA sequence produced from some base calling or alignment algorithm which uses aligned or assembled multiple gDNA sequences as input.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience November, 2012.

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_five_prime_single_strand_overhang
annotations.definition: A terminal region of DNA sequence where the end of the region is not blunt ended and the exposed single strand terminates at the 5' end.

synonyms.text: restriction enzyme five prime single strand overhang

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_three_prime_single_strand_overhang
annotations.definition: A terminal region of DNA sequence where the end of the region is not blunt ended and the exposed single strand terminates at the 3' end.

synonyms.text: restriction enzyme three prime single strand overhang

annotations.label: monomeric_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat_region containing repeat_units of 1 bp that is repeated multiple times in tandem.

annotations.label: H3K20_trimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 20th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 protein is tri-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K36_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 36th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2BK12_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 12th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2B protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2AK5_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 5th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2A histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H4K12_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 12th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H4 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2BK120_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 120th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2B histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H4K91_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 91st residue (a lysine), from the start of the H4 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2BK20_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 20th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2B histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H3K4_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 4th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2AK9_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 9th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2A histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H3K56_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 56th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2BK15_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 15th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2B histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H3R2_monomethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 2nd residue (an arginine), from the start of the H3 protein is mono-methylated.

annotations.label: H3R2_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 2nd residue (an arginine), from the start of the H3 protein is di-methylated.

annotations.label: H4R3_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 3nd residue (an arginine), from the start of the H4 protein is di-methylated.

annotations.label: H4K4_trimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 4th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H4 protein is tri-methylated.

annotations.label: H3K23_dimethylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 23rd residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 protein is di-methylated.

annotations.label: promoter_flanking_region
annotations.definition: A region immediately adjacent to a promoter which may or may not contain transcription factor binding sites.

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_assembly_scar
annotations.definition: A region of DNA sequence formed from the ligation of two sticky ends where the palindrome is broken and no longer comprises the recognition site and thus cannot be re-cut by the restriction enzymes used to create the sticky ends.

annotations.label: restriction_enzyme_region
annotations.definition: A region related to restriction enzyme function.
annotations.comment: Not a great term for annotation, but used to classify the various regions related to restriction enzymes.

annotations.label: protein_stability_element
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that proves structure in a protein that affects the stability of the protein.

annotations.label: protease_site
annotations.definition: A polypeptide_region that codes for a protease cleavage site.

annotations.label: lariat_intron
annotations.definition: A kind of intron whereby the excision is driven by lariat formation.
annotations.comment: Requested by PomBase 3604508.

annotations.label: TCT_motif
annotations.definition: A cis-regulatory element, conserved sequence YYC+1TTTYY, and spans -2 to +6 relative to +1 TSS. It is present in most ribosomal protein genes in Drosophila and mammals but not in the yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Resembles the initiator (TCAKTY in Drosophila) but functionally distinct from initiator.

annotations.label: 5_hydroxymethylcytosine
annotations.definition: A modified DNA cytosine base feature, modified by a hydroxymethyl group at the 5 carbon.

annotations.label: 5_formylcytosine
annotations.definition: A modified DNA cytosine base feature, modified by a formyl group at the 5 carbon.

annotations.label: modified_adenine
annotations.definition: A modified adenine DNA base feature.

annotations.label: modified_cytosine
annotations.definition: A modified cytosine DNA base feature.

annotations.label: modified_guanine
annotations.definition: A modified guanine DNA base feature.

annotations.label: 8_oxoguanine
annotations.definition: A modified DNA guanine base,at the 8 carbon, often the product of DNA damage.

annotations.label: 5_carboxylcytosine
annotations.definition: A modified DNA cytosine base feature, modified by a carboxy group at the 5 carbon.

annotations.label: 8_oxoadenine
annotations.definition: A modified DNA adenine base,at the 8 carbon, often the product of DNA damage.

annotations.label: coding_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant of a protein coding gene.

annotations.label: coding_transcript_intron_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant occurring within an intron of a coding transcript.

annotations.label: non_coding_transcript_intron_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant occurring within an intron of a non coding transcript.

annotations.label: zinc_finger_binding_site
annotations.definition: A binding site to which a polypeptide will bind with a zinc finger motif, which is characterized by requiring one or more Zinc 2+ ions for stabilized folding.

annotations.label: histone_4_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A histone 4 modification where the modification is the acetylation of the residue.

annotations.label: histone_3_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A histone 3 modification where the modification is the acetylation of the residue.

annotations.label: CTCF_binding_site
annotations.definition: A transcription factor binding site with consensus sequence CCGCGNGGNGGCAG, bound by CCCTF-binding factor.

annotations.label: five_prime_sticky_end_restriction_enzyme_cleavage_site
annotations.definition: A restriction enzyme recognition site that, when cleaved, results in 5 prime overhangs.
annotations.comment: Requested by Jackie Quinn. The sticky restriction sites are different from junctions because they include the sequence that is cut, inclusive of the five prime junction and the three prime junction.

synonyms.text: five prime sticky end restriction enzyme cleavage site

annotations.label: three_prime_sticky_end_restriction_enzyme_cleavage_site
annotations.definition: A restriction enzyme recognition site that, when cleaved, results in 3 prime overhangs.
annotations.comment: Requested by Jackie Quinn. The sticky restriction sites are different from junctions because they include the sequence that is cut, inclusive of the five prime junction and the three prime junction.

synonyms.text: three prime sticky end restriction enzyme cleavage site

annotations.label: ribonuclease_site
annotations.definition: A region of a transcript encoding the cleavage site for a ribonuclease enzyme.

annotations.label: signature
annotations.definition: A region of sequence where developer information is encoded.
annotations.comment: Requested by Jackie Quinn for use in synthetic biology.

annotations.label: RNA_stability_element
annotations.definition: A motif that affects the stability of RNA.

annotations.label: G_box
annotations.definition: A regulatory promoter element identified in mutation experiments, with consensus sequence: CACGTG. Present in promoters, intergenic regions, coding regions, and introns. They are involved in gene expression responses to light and interact with G-box binding factor and I-box binding factor 1a.
annotations.comment: A plant specific region.

annotations.label: L_box
annotations.definition: An orientation dependent regulatory promoter element, with consensus sequence of TTGCACAN4TTGCACA, found in plants.

annotations.label: I-box
annotations.definition: A plant regulatory promoter motif, composed of a highly conserved hexamer GATAAG (I-box core).

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_premature_start_codon_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant where a premature start codon is introduced, moved or lost.
annotations.comment: Requested by Andy Menzies at the Sanger. This isn't necessarily a protein coding change. A premature start codon can effect the production of a mature protein product by providing a competing translation start point. Some genes balance their expression this way, eg THPO requires the presence of a premature start to limit expression, its loss leads to Familial thrombocythemia.

annotations.label: silent_mating_type_cassette_array
annotations.definition: A gene cassette array that corresponds to a silenced version of a mating type region.

annotations.label: Okazaki_fragment
annotations.definition: Any of the DNA segments produced by discontinuous synthesis of the lagging strand during DNA replication.
annotations.comment: Requested by Midori Harris, 2013.

annotations.label: upstream_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A feature variant, where the alteration occurs upstream of the transcript TSS.
annotations.comment: Requested by Graham Ritchie, EBI/Sanger.

annotations.label: downstream_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A feature variant, where the alteration occurs downstream of the transcript termination site.
annotations.comment: Requested by Graham Ritchie, EBI/Sanger.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_premature_start_codon_gain_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant where a premature start codon is gained.

synonyms.text: 5 prime UTR premature start codon gain variant

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_premature_start_codon_loss_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant where a premature start codon is lost.

annotations.label: five_prime_UTR_premature_start_codon_location_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant where a premature start codon is moved.

annotations.label: consensus_AFLP_fragment
annotations.definition: A consensus AFLP fragment is an AFLP sequence produced from any alignment algorithm which uses assembled multiple AFLP sequences as input.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Cropscience September, 2013.

synonyms.text: consensus amplified fragment length polymorphism fragment

annotations.label: nonsynonymous_variant
annotations.definition: A non-synonymous variant is an inframe, protein altering variant, resulting in a codon change.

annotations.label: extended_cis_splice_site
annotations.definition: Intronic positions associated with cis-splicing. Contains the first and second positions immediately before the exon and the first, second and fifth positions immediately after.
annotations.comment: Added by Andy Menzies (Sanger).

annotations.label: intron_base_5
annotations.definition: Fifth intronic position after the intron exon boundary, close to the 5' edge of the intron.

annotations.label: extended_intronic_splice_region_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant occurring in the intron, within 10 bases of exon.
annotations.comment: Added by Andy Menzies (Sanger).

annotations.label: extended_intronic_splice_region
annotations.definition: Region of intronic sequence within 10 bases of an exon.

annotations.label: subtelomere
annotations.definition: A heterochromatic region of the chromosome, adjacent to the telomere (on the centromeric side) that contains repetitive DNA and sometimes genes and it is transcribed.

annotations.label: sgRNA
annotations.definition: A small RNA oligo, typically about 20 bases, that guides the cas nuclease to a target DNA sequence in the CRISPR/cas mutagenesis method.

annotations.label: mating_type_region_motif
annotations.definition: DNA motif that is a component of a mating type region.

annotations.label: Y_region
annotations.definition: A segment of non-homology between a and alpha mating alleles, found at all three mating loci (HML, MAT, and HMR), has two forms (Ya and Yalpha).
annotations.comment: Requested by Janos Demeter, SGD.

annotations.label: Z1_region
annotations.definition: A mating type region motif, one of two segments of homology found at all three mating loci (HML, MAT, and HMR).
annotations.comment: Requested by Janos Demeter, SGD.

annotations.label: Z2_region
annotations.definition: A mating type region motif, the rightmost segment of homology in the HML and MAT mating loci (not present in HMR).
annotations.comment: Requested by Janos Demeter, SGD.

annotations.label: ARS_consensus_sequence
annotations.definition: The ACS is an 11-bp sequence of the form 5'-WTTTAYRTTTW-3' which is at the core of every yeast ARS, and is necessary but not sufficient for recognition and binding by the origin recognition complex (ORC). Functional ARSs require an ACS, as well as other cis elements in the 5' (C domain) and 3' (B domain) flanking sequences of the ACS.

annotations.label: DSR_motif
annotations.definition: The determinant of selective removal (DSR) motif consists of repeats of U(U/C)AAAC. The motif targets meiotic transcripts for removal during mitosis via the exosome.
annotations.comment: Requested by Antonia Locke, (Pombe).

annotations.label: zinc_repressed_element
annotations.definition: A promoter element that has the consensus sequence GNMGATC, and is found in promoters of genes repressed in the presence of zinc.
annotations.comment: This element is bound by Loz1 in S. pombe. The paper does not name the element. This term was requested by Midoris Harris, for Pombe.

annotations.label: MNV
annotations.definition: An MNV is a multiple nucleotide variant (substitution) in which the inserted sequence is the same length as the replaced sequence.

annotations.label: rare_amino_acid_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon encoding a rare amino acid is changed, resulting in a different encoded amino acid.
annotations.comment: Request from Uma Devi Paila, UVA. Variants in the sites of rare amino acids e.g. Selenocysteine. These are important impact terms since a loss of such rare amino acids may lead to a loss of function.

annotations.label: selenocysteine_loss
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon encoding selenocysteine is changed, resulting in a different encoded amino acid.
annotations.comment: Request from Uma Devi Paila, UVA. Variants in the sites of rare amino acids e.g. Selenocysteine. These are important impact terms since a loss of such rare amino acids may lead to a loss of function.

annotations.label: pyrrolysine_loss
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon encoding pyrrolysine is changed, resulting in a different encoded amino acid.
annotations.comment: Request from Uma Devi Paila, UVA. Variants in the sites of rare amino acids e.g. Selenocysteine. These are important impact terms since a loss of such rare amino acids may lead to a loss of function.

annotations.label: intragenic_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that occurs within a gene but falls outside of all transcript features. This occurs when alternate transcripts of a gene do not share overlapping sequence.
annotations.comment: Requested by Pablo Cingolani, for use in SnpEff.

annotations.label: start_lost
annotations.definition: A codon variant that changes at least one base of the canonical start codon.
annotations.comment: Request from Uma Devi Paila, UVA. This term should not be applied to incomplete transcripts.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_truncation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the reduction of a the 5'UTR with regard to the reference sequence.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_elongation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the extension of 5' UTR, with regard to the reference sequence.

annotations.label: 3_prime_UTR_truncation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the reduction of a the 3' UTR with regard to the reference sequence.

annotations.label: 3_prime_UTR_elongation
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that causes the extension of 3' UTR, with regard to the reference sequence.

annotations.label: conserved_intergenic_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located in a conserved intergenic region, between genes.
annotations.comment: Requested by Uma Paila (UVA) for snpEff.

annotations.label: conserved_intron_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant occurring within a conserved region of an intron.
annotations.comment: Requested by Uma Paila (UVA) for snpEff.

annotations.label: start_retained_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where at least one base in the start codon is changed, but the start remains.
annotations.comment: Requested by Uma Paila as this term is annotated by snpEff. This would be used for non_AUG start codon annotation.

annotations.label: boundary_element
annotations.definition: Boundary elements are DNA motifs that prevent heterochromatin from spreading into neighboring euchromatic regions.
annotations.comment: Requested by Antonia Lock. Insulator is included as a related synonym since this is used to refer to insulator in the literature (NCBI:cf).

annotations.label: mating_type_region_replication_fork_barrier
annotations.definition: A DNA motif that is found in eukaryotic rDNA repeats, and is a site of replication fork pausing.
annotations.comment: Requested by Midori Harris.

synonyms.text: mating type region replication fork barrier

annotations.label: priRNA
annotations.definition: A small RNA molecule, 22-23 nt in size, that is the product of a longer RNA. The production of priRNAs is independent of dicer and involves binding of RNA by argonaute and trimming by triman. In fission yeast, priRNAs trigger the establishment of heterochromatin. PriRNAs are primarily generated from centromeric transcripts (dg and dh repeats), but may also be produced from degradation products of primary transcripts.

annotations.label: multiplexing_sequence_identifier
annotations.definition: A nucleic tag which is used in a ligation step of library preparation process to allow pooling of samples while maintaining ability to identify individual source material and creation of a multiplexed library.

annotations.label: W_region
annotations.definition: The leftmost segment of homology in the HML and MAT mating loci, but not present in HMR.
annotations.comment:
MERGED COMMENT:
TARGET COMMENT: Requested by Janos Demeter, SGD.
--------------------
SOURCE COMMENT: Requested by Janos Demeter, SGD.

annotations.label: cis_acting_homologous_chromosome_pairing_region
annotations.definition: A genome region where chromosome pairing occurs preferentially during homologous chromosome pairing during early meiotic prophase of Meiosis I.
annotations.comment: Comment: An example of this is the Sme2 locus in fission yeast S. pombe, where is coincident with an ribonuclear complex termed the "Mei2 dot". This term was Requested by Val Wood, PomBase.

annotations.label: intein_encoding_region
annotations.definition: The nucleotide sequence which encodes the intein portion of the precursor gene.
annotations.comment: Requested by Janos Demeter 2014.

annotations.label: uORF
annotations.definition: A short open reading frame that is found in the 5' untranslated region of an mRNA and plays a role in translational regulation.

annotations.label: sORF
annotations.definition: An open reading frame that encodes a peptide of less than 100 amino acids.

annotations.label: tnaORF
annotations.definition: A translated ORF encoded entirely within the antisense strand of a known protein coding gene.

annotations.label: X_region
annotations.definition: One of two segments of homology found at all three mating loci (HML, MAT and HMR).

annotations.label: shRNA
annotations.definition: A short hairpin RNA (shRNA) is an RNA transcript that makes a tight hairpin turn that can be used to silence target gene expression via RNA interference.

annotations.label: moR
annotations.definition: A non-coding transcript encoded by sequences adjacent to the ends of the 5' and 3' miR-encoding sequences that abut the loop in precursor miRNA.
annotations.comment: MoRs are generated from miR hairpins that are longer and can produce two functional miR per strand. They are called moRs because they are not located next to the loop and thus their biogenesis process is a little different, but functionally, they are supposed to act like miRs. It is the same for loRs that are the loop fragments, they are generated differently than miRs or moRs but if loaded into the risc they are supposed to act the same way miRs do.\nRequested by Thomas Desvignes, Jan 2015.

annotations.label: loR
annotations.definition: A short, non coding transcript of loop-derived sequences encoded in precursor miRNA.
annotations.comment: MoRs are generated from miR hairpins that are longer and can produce two functional miR per strand. They are called moRs because they are not located next to the loop and thus their biogenesis process is a little different, but functionally, they are supposed to act like miRs. It is the same for loRs that are the loop fragments, they are generated differently than miRs or moRs but if loaded into the risc they are supposed to act the same way miRs do.\nRequested by Thomas Desvignes, Jan 2015.

annotations.label: miR_encoding_snoRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A snoRNA primary transcript that also encodes pre-miR sequence that is processed to form functionally active miRNA.

annotations.label: lncRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a lncRNA.

annotations.label: miR_encoding_lncRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A lncRNA primary transcript that also encodes pre-miR sequence that is processed to form functionally active miRNA.

annotations.label: miR_encoding_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A tRNA primary transcript that also encodes pre-miR sequence that is processed to form functionally active miRNA.

annotations.label: shRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding an shRNA.

annotations.label: miR_encoding_shRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A shRNA primary transcript that also encodes pre-miR sequence that is processed to form functionally active miRNA.

annotations.label: vaultRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a vaultRNA.

annotations.label: miR_encoding_vaultRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A vaultRNA primary transcript that also encodes pre-miR sequence that is processed to form functionally active miRNA.

annotations.label: Y_RNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding a Y-RNA.

annotations.label: miR_encoding_Y_RNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A Y-RNA primary transcript that also encodes pre-miR sequence that is processed to form functionally active miRNA.

annotations.label: TCS_element
annotations.definition: A TCS element is a (yeast) transcription factor binding site, bound by the TEA DNA binding domain (DBD) of transcription factors. The consensus site is CATTCC or CATTCT.
annotations.comment: Requested by Rama - SGD.

annotations.label: pheromone_response_element
annotations.definition: A PRE is a (yeast) TFBS with consensus site [TGAAAC(A/G)].
annotations.comment: Requested by Rama, SGD.

annotations.label: FRE
annotations.definition: A FRE is an enhancer element necessary and sufficient to confer filamentation associated expression in S. cerevisiae.
annotations.comment: Requested by Rama, SGD.

annotations.label: transcription_pause_site
annotations.definition: Transcription pause sites are regions of a gene where RNA polymerase may pause during transcription. The functional role of pausing may be to facilitate factor recruitment, RNA folding, and synchronization with translation. Consensus transcription pause site have been observed in E. coli.
annotations.comment: Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: disabled_reading_frame
annotations.definition: A reading frame that could encode a full-length protein but which contains obvious mid-sequence disablements (frameshifts or premature stop codons).

annotations.label: H3K27_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 27th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H3 histone protein is acetylated.
annotations.comment: Requested by: Sagar Jain, Richard Scheuermann.

annotations.label: constitutive_promoter
annotations.definition: A promoter that allows for continual transcription of gene.

annotations.label: inducible_promoter
annotations.definition: A promoter whereby activity is induced by the presence or absence of biotic or abiotic factors.

annotations.label: dominant_negative_variant
annotations.definition: A variant where the mutated gene product adversely affects the other (wild type) gene product.
annotations.comment: Requested by Deanna Church.

annotations.label: gain_of_function_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby new or enhanced function is conferred on the gene product.

annotations.label: loss_of_function_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby the gene product has diminished or abolished function.

annotations.label: null_mutation
annotations.definition: A variant whereby the gene product is not functional or the gene product is not produced.

annotations.label: intronic_splicing_silencer
annotations.definition: An intronic splicing regulatory element that functions to recruit trans acting splicing factors suppress the transcription of the gene or genes they control.
annotations.comment: Requested by Javier Diez Perez.

annotations.label: exonic_splicing_silencer
annotations.definition: An exonic splicing regulatory element that functions to recruit trans acting splicing factors suppress the transcription of the gene or genes they control.
annotations.comment: Requested by Javier Diez Perez.

annotations.label: recombination_enhancer
annotations.definition: A regulatory_region that promotes or induces the process of recombination.

annotations.label: interchromosomal_translocation
annotations.definition: A translocation where the regions involved are from different chromosomes.

annotations.label: intrachromosomal_translocation
annotations.definition: A translocation where the regions involved are from the same chromosome.

annotations.label: complex_chromosomal_rearrangement
annotations.definition: A contiguous cluster of translocations, usually the result of a single catastrophic event such as chromothripsis or chromoanasynthesis.

annotations.label: Alu_insertion
annotations.definition: An insertion of sequence from the Alu family of mobile elements.

annotations.label: LINE1_insertion
annotations.definition: An insertion from the Line1 family of mobile elements.

annotations.label: SVA_insertion
annotations.definition: An insertion of sequence from the SVA family of mobile elements.

annotations.label: mobile_element_deletion
annotations.definition: A deletion of a mobile element when comparing a reference sequence (has mobile element) to a individual sequence (does not have mobile element).

annotations.label: HERV_deletion
annotations.definition: A deletion of the HERV mobile element with respect to a reference.

annotations.label: SVA_deletion
annotations.definition: A deletion of an SVA mobile element.

annotations.label: LINE1_deletion
annotations.definition: A deletion of a LINE1 mobile element with respect to a reference.

annotations.label: Alu_deletion
annotations.definition: A deletion of an Alu mobile element with respect to a reference.

annotations.label: CDS_supported_by_peptide_spectrum_match
annotations.definition: A CDS that is supported by proteomics data.

annotations.label: sequence_comparison
annotations.definition: A position or feature where two sequences have been compared.

annotations.label: no_sequence_alteration
annotations.definition: A position or feature within a sequence that is identical to the comparable position or feature of a specified reference sequence.
annotations.comment: This term is requested by the ClinVar data model group for use in the allele registry and such. A sequence at a defined location that is defined to match the reference assembly.

annotations.label: intergenic_1kb_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that falls in an intergenic region that is 1 kb or less between 2 genes.
annotations.comment: This term is added to map to the Annovar annotation 'upstream,downstream' .

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects an incompletely annotated transcript.
annotations.comment: This term is to map to the ANNOVAR term 'ncRNA' http://annovar.openbioinformatics.org/en/latest/user-guide/gene/ . The description in the documentation (11/23/15) 'variant overlaps a transcript without coding annotation in the gene definition'. and this is further clarified in the document: ncRNA above refers to RNA without coding annotation. It does not mean that this is a RNA that will never be translated; it merely means that the user-selected gene annotation system was not able to give a coding sequence annotation. It could still code protein products and may have such annotations in future versions of gene annotation or in another gene annotation system. For example, BC039000 is regarded as ncRNA by ANNOVAR when using UCSC Known Gene annotation, but it is regarded as a protein-coding gene by ANNOVAR when using ENSEMBL annotation.\n\nIt is further clarified in the comments section as: ncRNA does NOT mean conventional non-coding RNA. It means a RNA without complete coding sequence, and it can be a coding RNA that is annotated incorrectly by RefSeq or other gene definition systems.

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_3UTR_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects the 3' UTR of an incompletely annotated transcript.

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_5UTR_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects the 5' UTR of an incompletely annotated transcript.

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_intronic_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects the intron of an incompletely annotated transcript.

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_splice_region_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects the splice region of an incompletely annotated transcript.

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_exonic_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects the exon of an incompletely annotated transcript.

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_CDS
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects the coding regions of an incompletely annotated transcript.

annotations.label: incomplete_transcript_coding_splice_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that intersects the coding sequence near a splice region of an incompletely annotated transcript.

annotations.label: 2KB_downstream_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant located within 2KB 3' of a gene.

annotations.label: exonic_splice_region_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant in which a change has occurred within the exonic region of the splice site, 1-2 bases from boundary.

annotations.label: unidirectional_gene_fusion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby two genes, on the same strand have become joined.
annotations.comment: Requested by SNPEFF team. Feb 2016.

annotations.label: bidirectional_gene_fusion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby two genes, on alternate strands have become joined.
annotations.comment: Requested by SNPEFF team. Feb 2016.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_CDS
annotations.definition: A non functional descendant of the coding portion of a coding transcript, part of a pseudogene.

annotations.label: non_coding_transcript_splice_region_variant
annotations.definition: A transcript variant occurring within the splice region (1-3 bases of the exon or 3-8 bases of the intron) of a non coding transcript.

annotations.label: 3_prime_UTR_exon_variant
annotations.definition: A UTR variant of exonic sequence of the 3' UTR.
annotations.comment: Requested by visze github tracker ID 346.

annotations.label: 3_prime_UTR_intron_variant
annotations.definition: A UTR variant of intronic sequence of the 3' UTR.
annotations.comment: Requested by visze github tracker ID 346.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_intron_variant
annotations.definition: A UTR variant of intronic sequence of the 5' UTR.
annotations.comment: Requested by visze github tracker ID 346.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_exon_variant
annotations.definition: A UTR variant of exonic sequence of the 5' UTR.
annotations.comment: Requested by visze github tracker ID 346.

annotations.label: structural_interaction_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that impacts the internal interactions of the resulting polypeptide structure.
annotations.comment: Requested by Pablo Cingolani. The way I calculate this is simply by looking at the PDB entry of one protein and then marking those AA that are within 3 Angstrom of each other (and far away in the AA sequence, e.g. over 20 AA distance). The assumption is that, since they are very close in distance, they must be "interacting" and thus important for protein structure.

annotations.label: non_allelic_homologous_recombination_region
annotations.definition: A genomic region at a non-allelic position where exchange of genetic material happens as a result of homologous recombination.

annotations.label: scaRNA
annotations.definition: A ncRNA, specific to the Cajal body, that has been demonstrated to function as a guide RNA in the site-specific synthesis of 2'-O-ribose-methylated nucleotides and pseudouridines in the RNA polymerase II-transcribed U1, U2, U4 and U5 spliceosomal small nuclear RNAs (snRNAs).
annotations.comment: Moved from is_a ncRNA (SO:0000655) to is_a snoRNA (SO:0000275) as per request from FlyBase by Dave Sant 24 April 2021. See GitHub Issue #509.

annotations.label: short_tandem_repeat_variation
annotations.definition: A variation that expands or contracts a tandem repeat with regard to a reference.

annotations.label: vertebrate_immune_system_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene derived from a vertebrate immune system gene.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: immunoglobulin_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene derived from an immunoglobulin gene.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: T_cell_receptor_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogene derived from a T-cell receptor gene.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: IG_C_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogenic constant region of an immunoglobulin gene which closely resembles a known functional Imunoglobulin constant gene but in which the coding region has stop codons, frameshift mutations or a mutation that effects the initiation codon.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: IG_J_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogenic joining region which closely resembles a known functional imunoglobulin joining gene but in which the coding region has stop codons, frameshift mutations or a mutation that effects the initiation codon that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the joining region of the variable domain of an immunoglobulin chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: IG_V_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogenic variable region which closely resembles a known functional imunoglobulin variable gene but in which the coding region has stop codons, frameshift mutations or a mutation that effects the initiation codon that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the variable region of an immunoglobulin chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: TR_V_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogenic variable region which closely resembles a known functional T receptor variable gene but in which the coding region has stop codons, frameshift mutations or a mutation that effects the initiation codon that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the variable region of an immunoglobulin chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: TR_J_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A pseudogenic joining region which closely resembles a known functional T receptor (TR) joining gene but in which the coding region has stop codons, frameshift mutations or a mutation that effects the initiation codon that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the joining region of the variable domain of an immunoglobulin chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: translated_processed_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A processed pseudogene where there is evidence, (mass spec data) suggesting that it is also translated.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: translated_unprocessed_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A non-processed pseudogene where there is evidence, (mass spec data) suggesting that it is also translated.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: transcribed_unprocessed_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A unprocessed pseudogene supported by locus-specific evidence of transcription.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: transcribed_unitary_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A species specific unprocessed pseudogene without a parent gene, as it has an active orthologue in another species.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: transcribed_processed_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A processed_pseudogene overlapped by locus-specific evidence of transcription.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: polymorphic_pseudogene_with_retained_intron
annotations.definition: A polymorphic pseudogene in the reference genome, containing a retained intron, known to be intact in the genomes of other individuals of the same species. The annotation process has confirmed that the pseudogenisation event is not a genomic sequencing error.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: pseudogene_processed_transcript
annotations.definition: A processed_transcript supported by EST and/or mRNA evidence that aligns unambiguously to a pseudogene locus (i.e. alignment to the pseudogene locus clearly better than alignment to parent locus).
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: coding_transcript_with_retained_intron
annotations.definition: A protein coding transcript containing a retained intron.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: lncRNA_with_retained_intron
annotations.definition: A lncRNA transcript containing a retained intron.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: NMD_transcript
annotations.definition: A protein coding transcript that contains a CDS but has one or more splice junctions >50bp downstream of stop codon, making it susceptible to nonsense mediated decay.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: pseudogenic_transcript_with_retained_intron
annotations.definition: A transcript supported by EST and/or mRNA evidence that aligns unambiguously to the pseudogene locus; has retained intronic sequence compared to a reference transcript sequence.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes.

annotations.label: polymorphic_pseudogene_processed_transcript
annotations.definition: A processed transcript that does not contain a CDS that fullfills annotation criteria and not necessarily functionally non-coding.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: NMD_polymorphic_pseudogene_transcript
annotations.definition: A polymorphic pseudogene transcript that contains a CDS but has one or more splice junctions >50bp downstream of stop codon. Premature stop codon is not introduced, directly or indirectly, as a result of the variation i.e. must be present in both protein_coding and pseudogenic alleles.
annotations.comment: Term added as part of collaboration with Gencode, adding biotypes used in annotation.

annotations.label: allelic_frequency
annotations.definition: A physical quality which inheres to the allele by virtue of the number instances of the allele within a population. This is the relative frequency of the allele at a given locus in a population.
annotations.comment: Requested by HL7 clinical genomics group.

annotations.label: three_prime_overlapping_ncrna
annotations.definition: Transcript where ditag (digital gene expression profiling)and/or published experimental data strongly supports the existence of short non-coding transcripts transcribed from the 3'UTR.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes.

annotations.label: vertebrate_immune_system_gene
annotations.definition: The configuration of the IG and TR variable (V), diversity (D) and joining (J) germline genes before DNA rearrangements (with or without constant (C) genes in undefined configuration. (germline, non rearranged regions of the IG DNA loci).
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: immunoglobulin_gene
annotations.definition: A germline immunoglobulin gene.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: IG_C_gene
annotations.definition: A constant (C) gene, a gene that codes the constant region of an immunoglobulin chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: IG_D_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the diversity region of the variable domain of an immunoglobuin (IG) gene.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: IG_J_gene
annotations.definition: A joining gene that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the joining region of the variable domain of an immunoglobulin chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: IG_V_gene
annotations.definition: A variable gene that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the variable region of the variable domain of an Immunoglobulin chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: lncRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a long non-coding RNA.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes.

annotations.label: mt_rRNA
annotations.definition: Mitochondrial rRNA is an RNA component of the small or large subunits of mitochondrial ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions on 10 June 2021. Requested by EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: mt_tRNA
annotations.definition: Mitochondrial transfer RNA.

annotations.label: NSD_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that contains a CDS but has no stop codon before the polyA site is reached.

annotations.label: sense_intronic_lncRNA
annotations.definition: A long non-coding transcript found within an intron of a coding or non-coding gene, with no overlap of exonic sequence.
annotations.comment: Updating the names of sense_intronic_ncRNA (SO:0002131), sense_overlap_ncRNA (SO:0002132), sense_overlap_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002183), and sense_intronic_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002184) to _lncRNA. See GitHub Issue #579.

annotations.label: sense_overlap_lncRNA
annotations.definition: A long non-coding transcript that contains a protein coding gene within its intronic sequence on the same strand, with no overlap of exonic sequence.
annotations.comment: Updating the names of sense_intronic_ncRNA (SO:0002131), sense_overlap_ncRNA (SO:0002132), sense_overlap_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002183), and sense_intronic_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002184) to _lncRNA. See GitHub Issue #579.

annotations.label: T_cell_receptor_gene
annotations.definition: A T-cell receptor germline gene.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: TR_C_Gene
annotations.definition: A constant (C) gene, a gene that codes the constant region of a T-cell receptor chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: TR_D_Gene
annotations.definition: A gene that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the diversity region of the variable domain of aT-cell receptor gene.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: TR_J_Gene
annotations.definition: A joining gene that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the joining region of the variable domain of aT-cell receptor chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: TR_V_Gene
annotations.definition: A variable gene that rearranges at the DNA level and codes the variable region of the variable domain of aT-cell receptor chain.
annotations.comment: These terms have been requested by Adam Frankish to support Gencode and Vega biotypes. The terms are defined according to IGMT.

annotations.label: predicted_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript feature that has been predicted but is not yet validated.

annotations.label: unconfirmed_transcript
annotations.definition: This is used for non-spliced EST clusters that have polyA features. This category has been specifically created for the ENCODE project to highlight regions that could indicate the presence of protein coding genes that require experimental validation, either by 5' RACE or RT-PCR to extend the transcripts, or by confirming expression of the putatively-encoded peptide with specific antibodies.

annotations.label: early_origin_of_replication
annotations.definition: An origin of replication that initiates early in S phase.

annotations.label: late_origin_of_replication
annotations.definition: An origin of replication that initiates late in S phase.

annotations.label: histone_2A_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A histone 2A modification where the modification is the acetylation of the residue.

annotations.label: histone_2B_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A histone 2B modification where the modification is the acetylation of the residue.

annotations.label: histone_2AZ_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A histone 2AZ modification where the modification is the acetylation of the residue.

annotations.label: H2AZK4_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 4th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2AZ histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2AZK7_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 7th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2AZ histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2AZK11_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 11th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2AZ histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2AZK13_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 13th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2AZ histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: H2AZK15_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 15th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2AZ histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: AUG_initiated_uORF
annotations.definition: A uORF beginning with the canonical start codon AUG.

annotations.label: non_AUG_initiated_uORF
annotations.definition: A uORF beginning with a codon other than AUG.

annotations.label: genic_downstream_transcript_variant
