annotations.definition: A variant that falls downstream of a transcript, but within the genic region of the gene due to alternately transcribed isoforms.

annotations.label: genic_upstream_transcript_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that falls upstream of a transcript, but within the genic region of the gene due to alternately transcribed isoforms.

annotations.label: mitotic_recombination_region
annotations.definition: A genomic region where there is an exchange of genetic material with another genomic region, occurring in somatic cells.

annotations.label: meiotic_recombination_region
annotations.definition: A genomic region in which there is an exchange of genetic material as a result of the repair of meiosis-specific double strand breaks that occur during meiotic prophase.

annotations.label: CArG_box
annotations.definition: A promoter element bound by the MADS family of transcription factors with consensus 5'-(C/T)TA(T/A)4TA(G/A)-3'.
annotations.comment: Requested by Antonia Lock

annotations.label: Mat2P
annotations.definition: A gene cassette array containing H+ mating type specific information.

annotations.label: Mat3M
annotations.definition: A gene cassette array containing H- mating type specific information.

annotations.label: SHP_box
annotations.definition: A conserved Cdc48/p97 interaction motif with strict consensus sequence F[PI]GKG[TK][RK]LG[GT] and relaxed consensus sequence FXGKGX[RK]LG.

annotations.label: sequence_length_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that changes the length of one or more sequence features.

annotations.label: short_tandem_repeat_change
annotations.definition: A sequence variant where the copies of a short tandem repeat (STR) feature are either contracted or expanded.

annotations.label: short_tandem_repeat_expansion
annotations.definition: A short tandem repeat variant containing more repeat units than the reference sequence.

annotations.label: short_tandem_repeat_contraction
annotations.definition: A short tandem repeat variant containing fewer repeat units than the reference sequence.

annotations.label: H2BK5_acetylation_site
annotations.definition: A kind of histone modification site, whereby the 5th residue (a lysine), from the start of the H2B histone protein is acetylated.

annotations.label: trinucleotide_repeat_expansion
annotations.definition: A short tandem repeat expansion with an increase in a sequence of three nucleotide units repeated in tandem compared to a reference sequence.

annotations.label: ref_miRNA
annotations.definition: A ref_miRNA (RefSeq-miRNA) sequence is assigned at the creation of a new mature miRNA entry in a database. The ref_miRNA sequence designation remains unchanged even if a different isomiR is later shown to be expressed at a higher level. A ref_miRNA can be produced by one or multiple pre-miRNA.

annotations.label: isomiR
annotations.definition: IsomiRs are all the bona fide variants of a mature product. IsomiRs should be connected to the ref_miRNA it is most likely to be the variant of. Some isomiRs can be variations of one or multiple ref_miRNA.

annotations.label: RNA_thermometer
annotations.definition: An RNA_thermometer is a cis element in the 5' end of an mRNA that can change its secondary structure in response to temperature and coordinate temperature-dependent gene expression.

annotations.label: splice_polypyrimidine_tract_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that falls in the polypyrimidine tract at 3' end of intron between 17 and 3 bases from the end (acceptor -3 to acceptor -17).

annotations.label: splice_donor_region_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that falls in the region between the 3rd and 6th base after splice junction (5' end of intron).

annotations.label: telomeric_D_loop
annotations.definition: A telomeric D-loop is a three-stranded DNA displacement loop that forms at the site where the telomeric 3' single-stranded DNA overhang (formed of the repeat sequence TTAGGG in mammals) is tucked back inside the double-stranded component of telomeric DNA molecule, thus forming a t-loop or telomeric-loop and protecting the chromosome terminus.
annotations.comment: This definition is from GO:0061820 telomeric D-loop disassembly.

annotations.label: sequence_alteration_artifact
annotations.definition: A sequence_alteration where the source of the alteration is due to an artifact in the base-calling or assembly process.

annotations.label: indel_artifact
annotations.definition: An indel that is the result of base-calling or assembly error.

annotations.label: deletion_artifact
annotations.definition: A deletion that is the result of base-calling or assembly error.

annotations.label: insertion_artifact
annotations.definition: An insertion that is the result of base-calling or assembly error.

annotations.label: substitution_artifact
annotations.definition: A substitution that is the result of base-calling or assembly error.

annotations.label: duplication_artifact
annotations.definition: A duplication that is the result of base-calling or assembly error.

annotations.label: SNV_artifact
annotations.definition: An SNV that is the result of base-calling or assembly error.

annotations.label: MNV_artifact
annotations.definition: An MNV that is the result of base-calling or assembly error.

annotations.label: enzymatic_RNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes an enzymatic RNA.

annotations.label: ribozyme_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a ribozyme.

annotations.label: antisense_lncRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes an antisense long, non-coding RNA.

annotations.label: sense_overlap_lncRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a sense overlap long non-coding RNA.
annotations.comment: Updating the names of sense_intronic_ncRNA (SO:0002131), sense_overlap_ncRNA (SO:0002132), sense_overlap_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002183), and sense_intronic_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002184) to _lncRNA. See GitHub Issue #579.

annotations.label: sense_intronic_lncRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a sense intronic long non-coding RNA.
annotations.comment: Updating the names of sense_intronic_ncRNA (SO:0002131), sense_overlap_ncRNA (SO:0002132), sense_overlap_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002183), and sense_intronic_ncRNA_gene (SO:0002184) to _lncRNA. See GitHub Issue #579.

annotations.label: bidirectional_promoter_lncRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A non-coding locus that originates from within the promoter region of a protein-coding gene, with transcription proceeding in the opposite direction on the other strand.
annotations.comment: This is a gencode term. See GitHub Issue #408. Synonyms "bidirectional promoter lncRNA gene" and "bidirectional_promoter_lncRNA_gene" added 23 April 2021 by David Sant. See GitHub Issue #506.

annotations.label: mutational_hotspot
annotations.definition: A region of genomic sequence known to undergo mutational events with greater frequency than expected by chance.

annotations.label: HERV_insertion
annotations.definition: An insertion of sequence from the HERV family of mobile elements with respect to a reference.

annotations.label: functional_gene_region
annotations.definition: A gene_member_region that encodes sequence that directly contributes to the molecular function of its gene or gene product.
annotations.comment: A functional_gene_region is a sequence feature that resides within a gene. But it is typically the corresponding region of translated/transcribed sequence in a gene product, that performs the molecular function qualifying it as a functional_gene_region. Here, a functional_gene_region must contribute directly to the molecular function of the gene product - regions that code for purely structural elements in a gene product that connect such directly functional elements together are not considered functional_gene_regions. Examples of regions considered 'functional' include those encoding enzymatic activity, binding activity, regions required for localization or membrane association, channel-forming regions, and signal peptides or other elements critical for processing of a gene product. In addition, regions that function at the genomic/DNA level are also included - e.g. regions of sequence known to be critical for binding transcription or splicing factors.

annotations.label: allelic_pseudogene
annotations.definition: A (unitary) pseudogene that is stable in the population but importantly it has a functional alternative allele also in the population. i.e., one strain may have the gene, another strain may have the pseudogene. MHC haplotypes have allelic pseudogenes.

annotations.label: enhancer_blocking_element
annotations.definition: A transcriptional cis regulatory region that when located between an enhancer and a gene's promoter prevents the enhancer from modulating the expression of the gene. Sometimes referred to as an insulator but may not include the barrier function of an insulator.
annotations.comment: Insulator is included as a related synonym since this is used to refer to insulator in the literature (NCBI:cf).

annotations.label: imprinting_control_region
annotations.definition: A regulatory region that controls epigenetic imprinting and affects the expression of target genes in an allele- or parent-of-origin-specific manner. Associated regulatory elements may include differentially methylated regions and non-coding RNAs.
annotations.comment: Moved from is_a regulatory_region (SO:0005836) to is_a epigenetically_modified_region (SO:0001720) on 11 Feb 2021. GREEKC members pointed out that this would be a more appropriate location. See GitHub Issue #530.

annotations.label: flanking_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat lying outside the sequence for which it has functional significance (eg. transposon insertion target sites).

annotations.label: processed_pseudogenic_rRNA
annotations.definition: The pseudogene has arisen by reverse transcription of a mRNA into cDNA, followed by reintegration into the genome. Therefore, it has lost any intron/exon structure, and it might have a pseudo-polyA-tail.

annotations.label: unprocessed_pseudogenic_rRNA
annotations.definition: The pseudogene has arisen from a copy of the parent gene by duplication followed by accumulation of random mutation. The changes, compared to their functional homolog, include insertions, deletions, premature stop codons, frameshifts and a higher proportion of non-synonymous versus synonymous substitutions.

annotations.label: unitary_pseudogenic_rRNA
annotations.definition: The pseudogene has no parent. It is the original gene, which is functional in some species but disrupted in some way (indels, mutation, recombination) in another species or strain.

annotations.label: allelic_pseudogenic_rRNA
annotations.definition: A (unitary) pseudogene that is stable in the population but importantly it has a functional alternative allele also in the population. i.e., one strain may have the gene, another strain may have the pseudogene. MHC haplotypes have allelic pseudogenes.

annotations.label: processed_pseudogenic_tRNA
annotations.definition: The pseudogene has arisen by reverse transcription of a mRNA into cDNA, followed by reintegration into the genome. Therefore, it has lost any intron/exon structure, and it might have a pseudo-polyA-tail.

annotations.label: unprocessed_pseudogenic_tRNA
annotations.definition: The pseudogene has arisen from a copy of the parent gene by duplication followed by accumulation of random mutation. The changes, compared to their functional homolog, include insertions, deletions, premature stop codons, frameshifts and a higher proportion of non-synonymous versus synonymous substitutions.

annotations.label: unitary_pseudogenic_tRNA
annotations.definition: The pseudogene has no parent. It is the original gene, which is functional in some species but disrupted in some way (indels, mutation, recombination) in another species or strain.

annotations.label: allelic_pseudogenic_tRNA
annotations.definition: A (unitary) pseudogene that is stable in the population but importantly it has a functional alternative allele also in the population. i.e., one strain may have the gene, another strain may have the pseudogene. MHC haplotypes have allelic pseudogenes.

annotations.label: terminal_repeat
annotations.definition: A repeat at the ends of and within the sequence for which it has functional significance other than long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: repeat_instability_region
annotations.definition: A repeat region that is prone to expansions and/or contractions.

annotations.label: replication_start_site
annotations.definition: A nucleotide site from which replication initiates.

annotations.label: nucleotide_cleavage_site
annotations.definition: A point in nucleic acid where a cleavage event occurs.

annotations.label: response_element
annotations.definition: A regulatory element that acts in response to a stimulus, usually via transcription factor binding.
annotations.comment: Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: sequence_source
annotations.definition: Identifies the biological source of the specified span of the sequence
annotations.comment: Terms such as genomic_DNA or mRNA can be used to describe a sequence source.

annotations.label: UNAAAC_motif
annotations.definition: A hexameric RNA motif consisting of nucleotides UNAAAC (where N can be any nucleotide) that targets the RNA for degradation.

annotations.label: long_terminal_repeat_transcript
annotations.definition: An RNA that is transcribed from a long terminal repeat.

annotations.label: genomic_DNA_contig
annotations.definition: A contig composed of genomic DNA derived sequences.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Crop Science, March 2018

annotations.label: presence_absence_variation
annotations.definition: A variation qualifying the presence of a sequence in a genome which is entirely missing in another genome.
annotations.comment: Requested by Bayer Crop Science, March 2018

annotations.label: circular_plasmid
annotations.definition: A self replicating circular nucleic acid molecule that is distinct from a chromosome in the organism.

annotations.label: linear_plasmid
annotations.definition: A self replicating linear nucleic acid molecule that is distinct from a chromosome in the organism. They are capped by terminal proteins covalently bound to the 5' ends of the DNA.

annotations.label: transcription_termination_signal
annotations.definition: Termination signal preferentially observed downstream of polyadenylation signal

synonyms.text: polyA site associated transcription termination signal

annotations.label: redundant_inserted_stop_gained
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon is changed, resulting in a stop codon inserted next to an existing stop codon. This leads to a polypeptide of the same length.

annotations.label: Zas1_recognition_motif
annotations.definition: A DNA motif to which the S. pombe Zas1 protein binds. The consensus sequence is 5'-(Y)CCCCAY-3'.

annotations.label: Pho7_binding_site
annotations.definition: A promoter element with consensus sequence [5'-TCG(G/C)(A/T)xxTTxAA], bound by the transcription factor Pho7.

annotations.label: unspecified_indel
annotations.definition: A sequence alteration which includes an insertion or a deletion. This describes a sequence length change when the direction of the change is unspecified or when such changes are pooled into one category.
annotations.comment: This term is used when there is a change that is either an insertion or a deletion but it is unknown which event occurred.

annotations.label: functionally_abnormal
annotations.definition: A sequence variant in which the function of a gene product is altered with respect to a reference.
annotations.comment: Added after request from Lea Starita, lea.starita@gmail.com from the NCBI Feb 2019.

annotations.label: functionally_normal
annotations.definition: A sequence variant in which the function of a gene product is retained with respect to a reference.
annotations.comment: Added after request from Lea Starita, lea.starita@gmail.com from the NCBI Feb 2019.

annotations.label: function_uncertain_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant in which the function of a gene product is unknown with respect to a reference.
annotations.comment: Added after request from Lea Starita, lea.starita@gmail.com from the NCBI Feb 2019.

annotations.label: eukaryotic_promoter
annotations.definition: A regulatory_region including the Transcription Start Site (TSS) of a gene and serving as a platform for Pre-Initiation Complex (PIC) assembly, enabling transcription of a gene under certain conditions.

annotations.label: prokaryotic_promoter
annotations.definition: A regulatory_region essential for the specific initiation of transcription at a defined location in a DNA molecule, although this location might not be one single base. It is recognized by a specific RNA polymerase(RNAP)-holoenzyme, and this recognition is not necessarily autonomous.

annotations.label: inert_DNA_spacer
annotations.definition: Sequences that decrease interactions between biological regions, such as between a promoter, its 5' context and/or the translational unit(s) it regulates. Spacers can affect regulation of translation, transcription, and other biological processes.
annotations.comment: Updated by Evan Christensen on May 27, 2021 per github request https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/494

annotations.label: 2A_self_cleaving_peptide_region
annotations.definition: A region that codes for a 2A self-cleaving polypeptide region, which is a region that can result in a break in the peptide sequence at its terminal G-P junction.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave Sant on October 21, 2019 per github request https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/475

annotations.label: LOZ1_response_element
annotations.definition: A conserved sequence (5'-CGNMGATCNTY-3') transcription repressor binding site required for gene repression in the presence of high zinc.
annotations.comment: Added on October 30, 2019 as per request of Val Wood request on GitHub Issue# 476 https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/476

annotations.label: group_IIC_intron
annotations.definition: A group II intron that recognizes IBS1/EBS1 for the 5-prime exon and IBS3/EBS3 for the 3-prime exon and may also recognize a stem-loop in the RNA.

annotations.label: CDS_extension
annotations.definition: A sequence variant extending the CDS, that causes elongation of the resulting polypeptide sequence.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Edward Wallace GitHub issue #480 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/480)

annotations.label: CDS_five_prime_extension
annotations.definition: A sequence variant extending the CDS at the 5' end, that causes elongation of the resulting polypeptide sequence at the N terminus.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Edward Wallace GitHub issue #480 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/480)

annotations.label: CDS_three_prime_extension
annotations.definition: A sequence variant extending the CDS at the 3' end, that causes elongation of the resulting polypeptide sequence at the C terminus.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Edward Wallace GitHub issue #480 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/480)

annotations.label: CAAX_box
annotations.definition: A C-terminus protein motif (CAAX) serving as a post-translational prenylation site modified by the attachment of either a farnesyl or a geranyl-geranyl group to a cysteine residue. Farnesyltransferase recognizes CaaX boxes where X = M, S, Q, A, or C, whereas Geranylgeranyltransferase I recognizes CaaX boxes with X = L or E.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Val Wood GitHub issue #479 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/479)

annotations.label: self_cleaving_ribozyme
annotations.definition: An RNA that catalyzes its own cleavage.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by John T. Sexton GitHub issue #470 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/470)

annotations.label: selection_marker
annotations.definition: A genetic feature that encodes a trait used for artificial selection of a subpopulation.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Bryan Bartley GitHub issue #468 and #402 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/468)

annotations.label: homologous_chromosome_recognition_and_pairing_locus
annotations.definition: A chromosomal locus where complementary lncRNA and associated proteins accumulate at the corresponding lncRNA gene loci to tether homologous chromosome during chromosome pairing at meiosis I.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Val Wood GitHub issue #483 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/483)

synonyms.text: homologous chromosome recognition and pairing locus

annotations.label: pumilio_response_element
annotations.definition: A cis-acting element involved in RNA stability found in the 3' UTR of some RNA (consensus UGUAAAUA).
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Val Wood GitHub issue #455 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/455)

annotations.label: SUMO_interaction_motif
annotations.definition: A polypeptide region that mediates binding to SUMO. The motif contains a hydrophobic core sequence consisting of three or four Ile, Leu, or Val residues plus one acidic or polar residue at position 2 or 3.
annotations.comment: Added as per request GitHub issue #434 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/434)

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_18S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene which codes for 18S_rRNA, which functions as the small subunit of the ribosome in eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Antonia Lock GitHub issue #472 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/472). Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_16S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene which codes for 16S_rRNA, which functions as the small subunit of the ribosome in prokaryotes.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Antonia Lock GitHub issue #472 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/472). Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_5S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene which codes for 5S_rRNA, which is a portion of the large subunit of the ribosome in both eukaryotes and prokaryotes.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Antonia Lock GitHub issue #472 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/472). Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_28S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene which codes for 28S_rRNA, which functions as a component of the large subunit of the ribosome in eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Antonia Lock GitHub issue #472 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/472). Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_5_8S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene which codes for 5_8S_rRNA (5.8S rRNA), which functions as a component of the large subunit of the ribosome in eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Antonia Lock GitHub issue #472 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/472). Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_25S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene which codes for 25S_rRNA, which functions as a component of the large subunit of the ribosome in some eukaryotes.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Antonia Lock GitHub issue #472 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/472). Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_23S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene which codes for 23S_rRNA, which functions as a component of the large subunit of the ribosome in prokaryotes.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Antonia Lock GitHub issue #472 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/472). Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: partially_duplicated_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript which is partially duplicated due to duplication of DNA, leading to a new transcript that is only partial and likely nonfunctional.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from the Illumina group

annotations.label: five_prime_duplicated_transcript
annotations.definition: A partially_duplicated_transcript where the 5' end of the transcript is duplicated.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from the Illumina group

annotations.label: three_prime_duplicated_transcript
annotations.definition: A partially_duplicated_transcript where the 3' end of the transcript is duplicated.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from the Illumina group

annotations.label: sncRNA
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA less than 200 nucleotides in length.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #485 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/485)

annotations.label: spurious_protein
annotations.definition: A region of DNA that is predicted to be translated and transcribed into a protein by a protein detection algorithm that does not get transcribed in nature.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #478 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/478)

annotations.label: mature_protein_region_of_CDS
annotations.definition: A CDS region corresponding to a mature protein region of a polypeptide.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #484 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/484)

annotations.label: propeptide_region_of_CDS
annotations.definition: A CDS region corresponding to a propeptide of a polypeptide.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #484 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/484)

annotations.label: signal_peptide_region_of_CDS
annotations.definition: A CDS region corresponding to a signal peptide of a polypeptide.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #484 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/484)

annotations.label: transit_peptide_region_of_CDS
annotations.definition: CDS region corresponding to a transit peptide region of a polypeptide.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #484 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/484)

annotations.label: stem_loop_region
annotations.definition: A portion of a stem loop secondary structure in RNA.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #451 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/451)

annotations.label: loop
annotations.definition: The loop portion of a stem loop, which is not folded back upon itself.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #451 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/451)

annotations.label: stem
annotations.definition: The portion of a stem loop where the RNA is folded back upon itself.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #451 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/451)

annotations.label: non_complimentary_stem
annotations.definition: A region of a stem in a stem loop structure where the sequences are non-complimentary.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #451 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/451)

annotations.label: knob
annotations.definition: Cytologically observable heterochromatic regions of chromosomes away from centromeres that contain predominatly large tandem repeats and retrotransposons.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from GitHub Issue #487 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/487)

annotations.label: teb1_recognition_motif
annotations.definition: A binding motif with the consensus sequence TTAGGG to which Teb1 binds.
annotations.comment: Requested by Antonia Locke, (Pombe) as per GitHub Issue Request #439 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/439)

annotations.label: polyA_site_cluster
annotations.definition: A region defined by a cluster of experimentally determined polyadenylation sites, typically less than 25 bp in length and associated with a single polyadenylation signal.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #450 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/450)

annotations.label: LARD
annotations.definition: Large Retrotransposon Derivative elements are long-terminal repeats that contain reverse transcriptase priming sites and are conserved in sequence but contain no open reading frames encoding typical retrotransposon proteins . The LARDs identified in barley and other Triticeae have LTRs ~5.5 kb and an interal domain of ~3.5 kb. LARDs lack coding domains and thus do not encode proteins.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #429 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/429)

annotations.label: TRIM
annotations.definition: TRIM elements have terminal direct repeat sequences of 100-250 bp in length that flank an internal domain of 100–300 bp. TRIMs lack coding domains and thus do not encode proteins.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #429 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/429)

annotations.label: Watson_strand
annotations.definition: An absolute reference to the strand. When a chromosome has p and q arms, the Watson strand is the strand whose 5'-end is on the short arm of the chromosome. Of note, the term 'plus strand' is typically based on a reference sequence where it's preferred for the plus strand to be the Watson strand, but might not be and 'plus strand' is therefore not an exact synonym.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #419 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/419)

annotations.label: Crick_strand
annotations.definition: An absolute reference to the strand. When a chromosome has p and q arms, the Crick strand is the strand whose 5'-end is on the long arm of the chromosome. Of note, the term 'minus strand' is typically based on a reference sequence where it's preferred for the minus strand to be the Crick strand, but might not be and 'minus strand' is therefore not an exact synonym.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #419 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/419)

annotations.label: Copia_LTR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: LTR retrotransposons in the Copia superfamily contain elements coding for specific proteins in this order: GAG, AP, INT, RT, RH. GAG is a structural protein for virus-like particles. AP is aspartic proteinase. INT is a DDE integrase. RT is a reverse transcriptase. RH is RNAse H.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Gypsy_LTR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: LTR retrotransposons in the Gypsy superfamily contain elements coding for specific proteins in this order: GAG, AP, RT, RH, INT. GAG is a structural protein for virus-like particles. AP is aspartic proteinase. INT is a DDE integrase. RT is a reverse transcriptase. RH is RNAse H.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Bel_Pao_LTR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: LTR retrotransposons in the Bel-Pao superfamily are similar to LTRs in the Gypsy and Retrovirus superfamilies. Mainly described in metazoan genomes, this superfamily contain elements coding for specific proteins in this order: GAG, AP, RT, RH and INT. GAG is a structural protein for virus-like particles. AP is aspartic proteinase. INT is a DDE integrase. RT is a reverse transcriptase. RH is RNAse H.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Retrovirus_LTR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: LTR retrotransposons in the retrovirus superfamily are similar to LTR retrotransposons in the Gypsy and Bel-Pao superfamilies. Mainly described in vertebrate animals, this superfamily contain elements coding for specific proteins in this order: GAG, AP, RT, RH, INT, and ENV. GAG is a structural protein for virus-like particles. AP is aspartic proteinase. INT is a DDE integrase. RT is a reverse transcriptase. RH is RNAse H. ENV is envelop protein.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Endogenous_Retrovirus_LTR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Endogenous retrovirus (ERV) retrotransposons are abundant in the genomes of jawed vertebrates. Human ERVs (HERVs) are classified based on their homologies to animal retroviruses. Class I families are similar in sequence to mammalian Gammaretroviruses (type C) and Epsilonretroviruses (Type E). Class II families show homology to mammalian Betaretroviruses (Type B) and Deltaretroviruses (Type D). F-Class III families are similar to foamy viruses.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: R2_LINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: R2 retrotransposons are LINE elements (SO:0000194) that insert site-specifically into the host organism's 28S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) genes.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: RTE_LINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: RTE retrotransposons are LINE elements (SO:0000194) that contain a domain with homology to the apurinic-apyrimidic (AP) endonucleases in addition to the previously identified reverse transcriptase domain.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Jockey_LINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Jockey retrotransposons are LINE elements (SO:0000194) found only in arthropods. The full-length element is ~ 5 kb and contains two open reading frames (SO:0000236), ORF1 (568 aa) and ORF2 (916 aa), the second of which encodes an apurinic endonuclease (APE) and a reverse transcriptase (RT).
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: L1_LINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Long interspersed element-1 (LINE-1) elements are found in the human genome, which contains ORF1 (open reading frame1, including CC, coiled coil; RRM, RNA recognition motif; CTD, carboxyl-terminal domain) and ORF2 (including EN, endonuclease; RT, reverse transcriptase; C, cysteine-rich domain). The L1-encoded proteins (ORF1p and ORF2p) can mobilize nonautonomous retrotransposons, other noncoding RNAs, and messenger RNAs.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: I_LINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Elements of the LINE I superfamily are similar to the Jockey and L1 superfamily. They contains two ORFs, the.second of which includes  Apurinic endonuclease (APE) and  reverse transcriptase (RT). The I superfamily encodes an RH (RNase H) domain downstream of the RT domain.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: tRNA_SINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Short interspersed elements that originated from tRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: 7SL_SINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Short interspersed elements that originated from 7SL RNAs.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: 5S_SINE_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Short interspersed elements that originated from 5S rRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Crypton_YR_transposon
annotations.definition: Crypton is a superfamily of DNA transposons that use tyrosine recombinase (YR) to cut and rejoin the recombining DNA molecules.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Tc1_Mariner_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: Elements of the Tc1-Mariner terminal inverted repeat transposon superfamily (also called mariner transposons) are named after the Transponon of C. elegans number 1 transposasse. Their activity creates a 2-bp (TA) target-site duplication (TSD). Stowaway is the non-autonomous element in this superfamily usually shorter than 600 bp.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: hAT_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: The hAT terminal inverted repeat transposon superfamily elements were first found in maize (the Ac/Ds elements). Members of the hAT superfamily have TSDs of 8 bp, relatively short TIRs of 5–27 bp and overall lengths of less than 4 kb.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Mutator_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: Members of the Mutator family of terminal inverted repeat (TIR) transposon are usually long but are also highly divergent, either sharing only terminal G…C nucleotides, or with the G…C nucleotides absent. The length of the TSD (7-11 bp, usually 9 bp) remains probably the most useful criterion for identification.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Merlin_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: Terminal inverted repeat transposon superfamily Merlin elements create 8-9 bp target-site duplications (TSD).
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Transib_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: Terminal inverted repeat (TIR) transposons of the superfamily Transib contain the DDE motif, which is related to the RAG1 protein involved in V(D)J recombination.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: piggyBac_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: Primarily found in animals, the terminal inverted repeat (TIR) transposon superfamily piggyBac elements favour insertion adjacent to TTAA.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: PIF_Harbinger_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: Terminal inverted repeat transposons in the PIF/Harbinger/tourist superfamily create 3-bp target site duplication that are mainly 'TAA' or 'TTA'. The autonomous PIF-Harbinger elements are relatively small in size, usually a few kb in length. Non-autonomous elements in this superfamily usually shorter than 600 bp are referrred to as Tourist elements. The terminal sequences for PIF/Harbinger/Tourist elements are 'GGG/CCC…GGC/GCC' or 'GA/GGCA…TGCC/TC'.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: CACTA_TIR_transposon
annotations.definition: This terminal inverted repeat of the CACTA family generate 3-bp target site duplication (TSD) upon insertion. CACTA elements do not have a significant preference for genic region insertions. This terminal inverted repeat (TIR) transposon superfamily is named CACTA because their terminal sequences are 'CACTA/G…C/TAGTG'.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: YR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Tyrosine Kinase (YR) retrotransposons are a subclass of non-LTR retrotransposons. These YR-encoding elements consist of central gag, pol and tyrosine recombinase (YR) open reading frames (ORFs) flanked with terminal repeat. The pol ORF includes a reverse transcriptase (RT), a RNase H (RH) and, in case of DIRS, a domain similar to bacterial and phage DNA N-6-adenine-methyltransferase (MT). Compared to the retroviral pol (LTR retrotransposons, non-LTR retrotransposons and Penelope elements), both aspartic protease and DDE integrase are absent from YR retrotransposons. YR retrotransposons have inverted terminal repeats (ITRs).
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: DIRS_YR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Dictyostelium intermediate repeat sequence (DIRS) retrotransposons are members of the YR_retrotransposon (SO:0002286) superfamily with the following protein domains: RT, RH, YR, and MT. RT is a reverse transcriptase. RH is RNAse H. YR is tyrosine recombinase. MT is DNA N-6-adenine-methyltransferase.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Ngaro_YR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Ngaro retrotransposons are members of the YR_retrotransposon (SO:0002286) superfamily with the following protein domains: RT, RH, YR. RT is a reverse transcriptase. RH is RNAse H. YR is Tyrosine recombinase. Inverted terminal repeats (ITRs) in Ngaro are arranged in A-pol-B-A-B order where A and B represent ITRs.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Viper_YR_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: VIPER retrotransposons are members of the YR_retrotransposon (SO:0002286 superfamily with protein domains: RT, RH, YR. RT is a reverse transcriptase. RH is RNAse H. YR is Tyrosine recombinase. Inverted terminal repeats (ITRs) in VIPER are arranged in A-pol-B-A-B order where A and B represent ITRs. VIPER is only found in kinetoplastida genomes.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: Penelope_retrotransposon
annotations.definition: Penelope is a subclass of non_LTR_retrotransposons (SO:0000189). Penelope retrotransposons contains structural features of TR, RT, EN, TR, terminal repeats which can be in tandem or inverse orientation in different Penelope copies. RT is reverse transcriptase. EN is endonuclease.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #488 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/488)

annotations.label: circular_ncRNA
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA that is generated by backsplicing of exons or introns, resulting in a covalently closed loop without a 5’ cap or 3’ polyA tail.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #490 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/490) and GitHub Issue Request #391 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/391)

annotations.label: circular_mRNA
annotations.definition: An mRNA that is generated by backsplicing of exons or introns, resulting in a covalently closed loop without a 5’ cap or 3’ polyA tail.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #490 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/490) and GitHub Issue Request #391 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/391)

annotations.label: mitochondrial_control_region
annotations.definition: The non-coding region of the mitochondrial genome that controls RNA and DNA synthesis.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub Issue Request #417 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/417) from INSDC

annotations.label: mitochondrial_D_loop
annotations.definition: Mitochondrial displacement loop; a region within mitochondrial DNA in which a short stretch of RNA is paired with one strand of DNA, displacing the original partner DNA strand in this region.
annotations.comment: Added as per request by Terence Murphy (INSDC) for GitHub Issue #417 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/417)

annotations.label: transcription_factor_regulatory_site
annotations.definition: A TF_binding_site that is involved in regulation of expression.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585

annotations.label: TFRS_module
annotations.definition: The possible discontinuous stretch of DNA that is the combination of one or several TFRSs whose bound TFs work jointly in the regulation of a promoter.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585. Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: TFRS_collection
annotations.definition: The possible discontinous stretch of DNA that encompass all the TFRSs that regulate a promoter.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585. Moved from transcription_regulatory_region (SO:0001679) to transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region (SO:0001055) by Dave Sant on Feb 11, 2021 when transcription_regulatory_region was merged into transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region to be consistent with GO and reduce redundancy as part of the GREEKC consortium. See GitHub Issue #527.

annotations.label: simple_operon
annotations.definition: An operon whose transcription is coordinated on a single transcription unit.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585

annotations.label: complex_operon
annotations.definition: An operon whose transcription is coordinated on several mutually overlapping transcription units transcribed in the same direction and sharing at least one gene.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585

annotations.label: unit_of_gene_expression
annotations.definition: Transcription units or transcribed coding sequences.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585

annotations.label: transcription_unit
annotations.definition: DNA regions delimited by different nonspurious TSS-TTS pairs.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585

annotations.label: simple_regulon
annotations.definition: A regulon defined by considering one regulatory gene product.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585

annotations.label: complex_regulon
annotations.definition: A regulon defined by considering the units of expression regulated by a specified set of regulatory gene products.
annotations.comment: Added as per Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585

annotations.label: topologically_associated_domain
annotations.definition: An instance of a self-interacting DNA region flanked by left and right TAD boundaries.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave to be consistent with other ontologies updated with GREEKC initiative.

annotations.label: topologically_associated_domain_boundary
annotations.definition: A DNA region enriched in DNA loop anchors and across which DNA loops occur less often than expected by chance.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave to be consistent with other ontologies updated with GREEKC initiative.

annotations.label: chromatin_regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A region of a chromosome where regulatory events occur, including epigenetic modifications. These epigenetic modifications can include nucleosome modifications and post-replicational DNA modifications.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave to be consistent with other ontologies updated with GREEKC initiative.

annotations.label: DNA_loop
annotations.definition: A region of DNA between two loop anchor positions that are held in close physical proximity.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave to be consistent with other ontologies updated with GREEKC initiative. DS updated defintion Feb 16, 2021. See GitHub Issue #534.

annotations.label: DNA_loop_anchor
annotations.definition: The ends of a DNA loop where the two strands of DNA are held in close physical proximity. During interphase the anchors of DNA loops are convergently oriented CTCF binding sites.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave to be consistent with other ontologies updated with GREEKC initiative. DS updated defintion Feb 16, 2021. See GitHub Issue #534.

annotations.label: core_promoter_element
annotations.definition: An element that always exists within the promoter region of a gene. When multiple transcripts exist for a gene, the separate transcripts may have separate core_promoter_elements.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave to be consistent with other ontologies updated with GREEKC initiative.

annotations.label: cryptic_promoter
annotations.definition: The promoter of a cryptic gene.
annotations.comment: Added by Dave to be consistent with other ontologies updated with GREEKC initiative.

annotations.label: viral_promoter
annotations.definition: A regulatory_region including the Transcription Start Site (TSS) of a gene found in genes of viruses.

annotations.label: core_prokaryotic_promoter_element
annotations.definition: An element that always exists within the promoter region of a prokaryotic gene.

annotations.label: core_viral_promoter_element
annotations.definition: An element that always exists within the promoter region of a viral gene.

annotations.label: altered_gene_product_level
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that alters the level or amount of gene product produced. This high level term can be applied where the direction of level change (increased vs decreased gene product level) is unknown or not confirmed.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020. See Issue Request #501 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/501). Updated definition 17 Feb 2023 along with updates from GenCC. See Issue Request #612.

annotations.label: increased_gene_product_level
annotations.definition: A variant that increases the level or amount of gene product produced.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020. See Issue Request #501 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/501). Updated definition 17 Feb 2023 along with updates from GenCC. See Issue Request #612.

annotations.label: decreased_gene_product_level
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that decreases the level or amount of gene product produced.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020. See Issue Request #501 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/501). Updated definition 17 Feb 2023 along with updates from GenCC. See Issue Request #612.

annotations.label: absent_gene_product
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that results in no gene product.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020. See Issue Request #501 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/501). Updated definition 17 Feb 2023 along with updates from GenCC. See Issue Request #612.

annotations.label: altered_gene_product_sequence
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that alters the sequence of a gene product.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020. See Issue Request #501 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/501)

annotations.label: NMD_triggering_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that leads to a change in the location of a termination codon in a transcript that leads to nonsense-mediated decay (NMD). The change in location of a termination codon can be caused by several different types of sequence variants, including stop_gained (SO:0001587), frameshift_variant (SO:0001589), splice_donor_variant (SO:0001575), and splice_acceptor_variant (SO:0001574) types of variants.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: NMD_escaping_variant
annotations.definition: A sequence variant that leads to a change in the location of a termination codon in a transcript but allows the transcript to escape nonsense-mediated decay (NMD). The change in location of a termination codon can be caused by several different types of sequence variants, including stop_gained (SO:0001587), frameshift_variant (SO:0001589), splice_donor_variant (SO:0001575), and splice_acceptor_variant (SO:0001574) types of variants.
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: stop_gained_NMD_triggering
annotations.definition: A stop_gained (SO:0001587) variant that is degraded by nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: stop_gained_NMD_escaping
annotations.definition: A stop_gained (SO:0001587) variant that allows the transcript to escape nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: frameshift_variant_NMD_triggering
annotations.definition: A frameshift_variant (SO:0001589) that is degraded by nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: frameshift_variant_NMD_escaping
annotations.definition: A frameshift_variant (SO:0001589) that allows the transcript to escape nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: splice_donor_variant_NMD_triggering
annotations.definition: A splice_donor_variant (SO:0001575) that is degraded by nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: splice_donor_variant_NMD_escaping
annotations.definition: A splice_donor_variant (SO:0001575) that allows the transcript to escape nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: splice_acceptor_variant_NMD_triggering
annotations.definition: A splice_acceptor_variant (SO:0001574) that is degraded by nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: splice_acceptor_variant_NMD_escaping
annotations.definition: A splice_acceptor_variant (SO:0001574) that allows the transcript to escape nonsense-mediated decay (NMD).
annotations.comment: Added as per request from Ang Roberts as part of GenCC November 2020.

annotations.label: minus_1_translational_frameshift
annotations.definition: The region of mRNA 1 base long that is included as part of two separate codons during the process of translational frameshifting (GO:0006452), causing the reading frame to be different.
annotations.comment: Added along with the update to the definition of transaltional_frameshift SO:0001210 Feb 2021, brought to our attention by Terrence Murphy of INSDC. See GitHub Issue #522.

annotations.label: minus_2_translational_frameshift
annotations.definition: The region of mRNA 2 bases long that is included as part of two separate codons during the process of translational frameshifting (GO:0006452), causing the reading frame to be different.
annotations.comment: Added along with the update to the definition of transaltional_frameshift SO:0001210 Feb 2021, brought to our attention by Terrence Murphy of INSDC. See GitHub Issue #522.

annotations.label: accessible_DNA_region
annotations.definition: A region of DNA that is depleted of nucleosomes and accessible to DNA-binding proteins including transcription factors and nucleases.
annotations.comment: Added as part of GREEKC terms. See GitHub Issues #531 & #534.

annotations.label: epigenomically_modified_region
annotations.definition: A biological region implicated in inherited changes caused by mechanisms other than changes in the underlying DNA sequence.
annotations.comment: Added as part of GREEKC terms to differentiate between inherited and not inherited epigenetic changes. See GitHub Issue #532.

annotations.label: amber_stop_codon
annotations.definition: A stop codon with the DNA sequence TAG.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub request #537.

annotations.label: ochre_stop_codon
annotations.definition: A stop codon with the DNA sequence TAA.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub request #537.

annotations.label: opal_stop_codon
annotations.definition: A stop codon with the DNA sequence TGA.
annotations.comment: Added as per GitHub request #537.

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_2S_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes for 2S ribosomal RNA, which functions as a component of the large subunit of the ribosome in Drosophila and at least some other Diptera.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from FlyBase. See GitHub Issue #507. Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_2S_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic 2S rRNA is a 30 nucleotide RNA component of the large subunit of cytosolic ribosomes in Drosophila and at least some other Diptera. It is homologous to the 3' part of other 5.8S rRNA molecules. The 3' end of the 5.8S molecule is able to base-pair with the 5' end of the 2S rRNA to generate a helical region equivalent in position to the 'GC-rich hairpin' found in all previously sequenced 5.8S molecules.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from FlyBase. See GitHub Issue #507. Renamed from rRNA_2S to cytosolic_2S_rRNA on 27 May 2021 with the restructuring of rRNA child terms. Updated definition to be consistent with format of other rRNA definitions. Requested by EBI. See GitHub Issue #493.

annotations.label: U7_snRNA
annotations.definition: A 57 to 71 nucleotide RNA that is a component of the U7 small nuclear ribonucleoprotein complex (U7 snRNP). The U7 snRNP is required for histone pre-mRNA processing.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from FlyBase. See GitHub Issue #508

annotations.label: scaRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes for a scaRNA (small Cajal body-specific RNA).
annotations.comment: Added as a request from FlyBase. See GitHub Issue #510

annotations.label: RNA_7SK
annotations.definition: An abundant small nuclear RNA that, together with associated cellular proteins, regulates the activity of the positive transcription elongation factor b (P-TEFb). It is often described in literature as similar to a snRNA, except of longer length.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from FlyBase. See GitHub Issue #512

annotations.label: RNA_7SK_gene
annotations.definition: A gene encoding a 7SK RNA (SO:0002340).
annotations.comment: Added as a request from FlyBase. See GitHub Issue #512

annotations.label: sncRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A ncRNA_gene that encodes an ncRNA less than 200 nucleotides in length.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from FlyBase to make the ncRNA_gene branch in SO mirror the ncRNA branch. See GitHub Issue #514

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA
annotations.definition: Cytosolic rRNA is an RNA component of the small or large subunits of cytosolic ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from EBI. See GitHub Issue #493

annotations.label: mt_SSU_rRNA
annotations.definition: Mitochondrial SSU rRNA is an RNA component of the small subunit of mitochondrial ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from EMBL. See GitHub Issue #493

annotations.label: mt_LSU_rRNA
annotations.definition: Mitochondrial LSU rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of mitochondrial ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from EMBL. See GitHub Issue #493

annotations.label: plastid_rRNA
annotations.definition: Plastid rRNA is an RNA component of the small or large subunits of plastid (such as chloroplast) ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from EMBL. See GitHub Issue #493

annotations.label: plastid_SSU_rRNA
annotations.definition: Plastid SSU rRNA is an RNA component of the small subunit of plastid (such as chloroplast) ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from EMBL. See GitHub Issue #493

annotations.label: plastid_LSU_rRNA
annotations.definition: Plastid LSU rRNA is an RNA component of the large subunit of plastid (such as chloroplast) ribosomes.
annotations.comment: Added as a request from EMBL. See GitHub Issue #493

annotations.label: fragile_site
annotations.definition: A heritable locus on a chromosome that is prone to DNA breakage.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #301.

annotations.label: common_fragile_site
annotations.definition: A fragile site considered part of the normal chromosomal structure.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #301.

annotations.label: rare_fragile_site
annotations.definition: A fragile site found in the chromosomes of less than five percent of the human population.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #301.

annotations.label: sisRNA
annotations.definition: A non-coding RNA typically derived from intronic sequence of the sense strand of a cognate host gene, that is not rapidly degraded. It may contain exonic sequences, 5′ caps, and/or polyA tails.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #515.

annotations.label: sbRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene encoding a stem-bulge RNA.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #516.

annotations.label: sbRNA
annotations.definition: A small non-coding stem-loop RNA present in nematodes and insects, functionally and structurally related to vertebrate Y RNA.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #516.

annotations.label: hpRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene encoding a hpRNA.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #518.

annotations.label: hpRNA
annotations.definition: An RNA comprising an extended inverted repeat, the stem of which is typically much longer than that of miRNA precursors and can be up to 400 base pairs in length. hpRNAs are processed by Dicer-2 to generate endogenous short interfering RNAs (siRNAs).
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #518.

annotations.label: biosynthetic_gene_cluster
annotations.definition: A physically clustered group of two or more genes in a particular genome that together encode a biosynthetic pathway for the production of a specialized metabolite (including its chemical variants).
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #558.

annotations.label: vault_RNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a vault RNA.
annotations.comment: As of 11 November 2021 the HNGC lists 4 genes as RNA, vault. These are HGNC IDs: 12654, 12655, 12656, 37054.

annotations.label: Y_RNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that encodes a Y RNA.
annotations.comment: There are four genes from HGNC that are annotated this way. HGNC IDs: 10242, 10243, 10244, and 10248.

annotations.label: cytosolic_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for cytosolic rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_LSU_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for cytosolic LSU rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: cytosolic_SSU_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for cytosolic SSU rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: mt_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for mitochondrial rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: mt_LSU_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for mitochondrial LSU rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513). Obsoleted term rRNA_21S_gene (SO:0002241) merged into this term on 12 Sept 2022, see GitHub Issue #513.

annotations.label: mt_SSU_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for mitochondrial SSU rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: plastid_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for plastid rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: plastid_LSU_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for plastid LSU rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: plastid_SSU_rRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for plastid SSU rRNA.
annotations.comment: Adjusted heirarchy and names of rRNA_gene terms at the request of Steven Marygold GitHub Issue #513 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/513).

annotations.label: C_D_box_scaRNA
annotations.definition: A scaRNA possessing a box C/D sequence motif, guiding the methylation of snRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: H_ACA_box_scaRNA
annotations.definition: A scaRNA possessing a box H/ACA sequence motif, guiding the pseudouridylation of snRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: C-D_H_ACA_box_scaRNA
annotations.definition: A scaRNA possessing both box C/D and box H/ACA sequence motifs, guiding both the methylation and pseudouridylation of snRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: C_D_box_scaRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for scaRNA possessing a box C/D sequence motif, guiding the methylation of snRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: H_ACA_box_scaRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for scaRNA possessing a box H/ACA sequence motif, guiding the pseudouridylation of snRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: C-D_H_ACA_box_scaRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for scaRNA possessing both box C/D and box H/ACA sequence motifs, guiding both the methylation and pseudouridylation of snRNAs.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: C_D_box_snoRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes a C_D_box_snoRNA. Most box C/D snoRNAs also contain long (>10 nt) sequences complementary to rRNA. Boxes C and D, as well as boxes C' and D', are usually located in close proximity, and form a structure known as the box C/D motif. This motif is important for snoRNA stability, processing, nucleolar targeting and function. A small number of box C/D snoRNAs are involved in rRNA processing; most, however, are known or predicted to serve as guide RNAs in ribose methylation of rRNA. Targeting involves direct base pairing of the snoRNA at the rRNA site to be modified and selection of a rRNA nucleotide a fixed distance from box D or D'.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519). Added citations. See GitHub Issue #565.

synonyms.text: box C/D snoRNA gene, C D box snoRNA gene, C/D box snoRNA gene

annotations.label: H_ACA_box_snoRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for H_ACA_box_snoRNA. Members of the box H/ACA family contain an ACA triplet, exactly 3 nt upstream from the 3' end and an H-box in a hinge region that links two structurally similar functional domains of the molecule. Both boxes are important for snoRNA biosynthesis and function. A few box H/ACA snoRNAs are involved in rRNA processing; most others are known or predicted to participate in selection of uridine nucleosides in rRNA to be converted to pseudouridines. Site selection is mediated by direct base pairing of the snoRNA with rRNA through one or both targeting domains.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519). Added citations. See GitHub Issue #565.

synonyms.text: box H/ACA snoRNA gene, H ACA box snoRNA gene, H/ACA box snoRNA gene

annotations.label: U14_snoRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for U14_snoRNA. U14 small nucleolar RNA (U14 snoRNA) is required for early cleavages of eukaryotic precursor rRNAs. In yeasts, this molecule possess a stem-loop region (known as the Y-domain) which is essential for function. A similar structure, but with a different consensus sequence, is found in plants, but is absent in vertebrates.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

synonyms.text: small nucleolar RNA U14 gene, snoRNA U14 gene, U14 small nucleolar RNA gene, U14 snoRNA gene

annotations.label: U3_snoRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for U3_snoRNA. U3 snoRNA is a member of the box C/D class of small nucleolar RNAs. The U3 snoRNA secondary structure is characterised by a small 5' domain (with boxes A and A'), and a larger 3' domain (with boxes B, C, C', and D), the two domains being linked by a single-stranded hinge. Boxes B and C form the B/C motif, which appears to be exclusive to U3 snoRNAs, and boxes C' and D form the C'/D motif. The latter is functionally similar to the C/D motifs found in other snoRNAs. The 5' domain and the hinge region act as a pre-rRNA-binding domain. The 3' domain has conserved protein-binding sites. Both the box B/C and box C'/D motifs are sufficient for nuclear retention of U3 snoRNA. The box C'/D motif is also necessary for nucleolar localization, stability and hypermethylation of U3 snoRNA. Both box B/C and C'/D motifs are involved in specific protein interactions and are necessary for the rRNA processing functions of U3 snoRNA.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

synonyms.text: small nucleolar RNA U3 gene, snoRNA U3 gene, U3 small nucleolar RNA gene, U3 snoRNA gene

annotations.label: methylation_guide_snoRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for methylation_guide_snoRNA. A snoRNA that specifies the site of 2'-O-ribose methylation in an RNA molecule by base pairing with a short sequence around the target residue.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: pseudouridylation_guide_snoRNA_gene
annotations.definition: A gene that codes for pseudouridylation_guide_snoRNA. A snoRNA that specifies the site of pseudouridylation in an RNA molecule by base pairing with a short sequence around the target residue.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Steven Marygold. See GitHub Issue #519 (https://github.com/The-Sequence-Ontology/SO-Ontologies/issues/519).

annotations.label: bidirectional_promoter_lncRNA
annotations.definition: A long non-coding RNA which is produced using the promoter of a protein-coding gene but with transcription occurring in the opposite direction.
annotations.comment: Created new term "bidirectional_promoter_lncRNA" (SO:0002381). See GitHub Issue #579.

annotations.label: range_extender_element
annotations.definition: A conserved cis-acting element that confers extreme-distance regulatory activity to an enhancer.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Chris Mungall (Berkeley Lab) on 6 June 2024. See GitHub Issue #649.

annotations.label: oncogenic_variant
annotations.definition: A variant that has been found to be pathogenic in the context of a neoplastic disease.
annotations.comment: See GitHub Issue #643.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_uORF_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant within an upstream open reading frame.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Sarah Hunt (EBI). See GitHub Issue #647.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_uORF_stop_codon_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant where a stop codon in an upstream open reading frame is introduced, moved or lost.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Sarah Hunt (EBI). See GitHub Issue #622.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_uORF_frameshift_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant which disrupts the translation of an upstream open reading frame because the number of nucleotides inserted or deleted is not a multiple of three.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Sarah Hunt (EBI). See GitHub Issue #621.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_uORF_stop_codon_gain_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant where a premature stop codon is gained in an upstream open reading frame.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Sarah Hunt (EBI). See GitHub Issue #624.

annotations.label: 5_prime_UTR_uORF_stop_codon_loss_variant
annotations.definition: A 5' UTR variant where the stop codon of an upstream open reading frame is lost.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Sarah Hunt (EBI). See GitHub Issue #623.

annotations.label: pseudoexon_variant
annotations.definition: Inclusion of a non-conserved, unannotated exon in the spliced transcript, as a consequence of a variant in the gene.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: abolished_transcription_level_variant
annotations.definition: Complete lack of transcription of a gene, as a consequence of a variant in the gene or a regulatory region.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: increased_enzyme_activity_variant
annotations.definition: An increase in the activity of an enzyme, as a result of a variant in the gene that encodes the enzyme or a regulatory region.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: precursor_RNA
annotations.definition: Any RNA species that is not yet the mature RNA product; may include ncRNA, rRNA, tRNA, 5' untranslated region (5'UTR), coding sequences (CDS, exon), intervening sequences (intron) and 3' untranslated region (3'UTR).
annotations.comment: This term should be used for RNA which may be the result of post-transcriptional processing; if the RNA in question is known not to have been processed, use primary_transcript (SO:0000185). At the time this SO term was created, the INSDC Feature Table has a specific term (i.e. precursor_RNA) for transcripts that may be in various stages of processing between primary_transcript (SO:0000185) and mature_transcript (SO:0000233). See the INSDC Feature Table Definition for the "precursor_RNA" feature key https://www.insdc.org/submitting-standards/feature-table/. Added 9 Sep 2025 at the request of Terence Murphy of NCBI to support transformation between the INSDC Feature Table and GFF3 formats. See GitHub Issue #655.

annotations.label: decreased_enzyme_activity_variant
annotations.definition: A decrease in the activity of an enzyme, as a result of a variant in the gene that encodes the enzyme or a regulatory region.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: lost_polypeptide
annotations.definition: A complete loss of polypeptide (i.e. protein) expression, as a consequence of a variant in the gene.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 1 Aug 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: decreased_polypeptide_level
annotations.definition: A decrease in polypeptide (i.e. protein) expression, as a consequence of a variant in the gene.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 31 July 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: cellular_mislocalization_variant
