annotations.definition: Trafficking of a polypeptide (i.e. protein) sequence to the wrong cellular location, as a consequence of a variant in the gene.
annotations.comment: Added at the request of Melissa Landrum of the NCBI on 1 Aug 2025 (see GitHub issue #657).

annotations.label: selenocysteine_gain
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby at least one base of a codon is changed, resulting in a selenocysteine.
annotations.comment: Added on 9 Sep 2025 at the request of Till Hartmann of the Berlin Institute of Health (BIH). See GitHub Issue #653.

annotations.label: inframe_unidirectional_gene_fusion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby two genes, on the same strand have become joined without a disruption of the translational reading frame, because the number of nucleotides between the reading frames of the joined genes is a multiple of three.
annotations.comment: Added 10 Sep 2025. See GitHub Issue #664.

annotations.label: frameshift_unidirectional_gene_fusion
annotations.definition: A sequence variant whereby two genes, on the same strand have become joined with a disruption of the translational reading frame, because the number of nucleotides between the reading frames of the joined genes is not a multiple of three.
annotations.comment: Added 10 Sep 2025. See GitHub Issue #664.

annotations.label: regulatory_region
annotations.definition: A region of sequence that is involved in the control of a biological process.

annotations.label: U14_snoRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: The primary transcript of an evolutionarily conserved eukaryotic low molecular weight RNA capable of intermolecular hybridization with both homologous and heterologous 18S rRNA.

annotations.label: methylation_guide_snoRNA
annotations.definition: A snoRNA that specifies the site of 2'-O-ribose methylation in an RNA molecule by base pairing with a short sequence around the target residue.
annotations.comment: Has RNA 2'-O-ribose methylation guide activity (GO:0030561).

annotations.label: rRNA_cleavage_RNA
annotations.definition: An ncRNA that is part of a ribonucleoprotein that cleaves the primary pre-rRNA transcript in the process of producing mature rRNA molecules.

annotations.label: exon_of_single_exon_gene
annotations.definition: An exon that is the only exon in a gene.

annotations.label: cassette_array_member
annotations.definition: A gene that is a member of a gene cassette, which is a mobile genetic element.

annotations.label: gene_cassette_member
annotations.definition: A gene that is a member of a gene cassette, which is a mobile genetic element.

annotations.label: gene_subarray_member
annotations.definition: A gene that is a member of a group of genes that are either regulated or transcribed together within a larger group of genes that are regulated or transcribed together.

annotations.label: primer_binding_site
annotations.definition: Non-covalent primer binding site for initiation of replication, transcription, or reverse transcription.

annotations.label: gene_array
annotations.definition: An array includes two or more genes, or two or more gene subarrays, contiguously arranged where the individual genes, or subarrays, are either identical in sequence, or essentially so.
annotations.comment: This would include, for example, a cluster of genes each encoding the major ribosomal RNAs and a cluster of histone gene subarrays.

annotations.label: gene_subarray
annotations.definition: A subarray is, by defintition, a member of a gene array (SO:0005851); the members of a subarray may differ substantially in sequence, but are closely related in function.
annotations.comment: This would include, for example, a cluster of genes encoding different histones.

annotations.label: gene_cassette
annotations.definition: A gene that can be substituted for a related gene at a different site in the genome.
annotations.comment: This would include, for example, the mating type gene cassettes of S. cerevisiae. Gene cassettes usually exist as linear sequences as part of a larger DNA molecule, such as a chromosome or plasmid.

annotations.label: gene_cassette_array
annotations.definition: An array of non-functional genes whose members, when captured by recombination form functional genes.
annotations.comment: This would include, for example, the arrays of non-functional VSG genes of Trypanosomes.

annotations.label: gene_group
annotations.definition: A collection of related genes.

annotations.label: selenocysteine_tRNA_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript encoding seryl tRNA (SO:000269).

annotations.label: selenocysteinyl_tRNA
annotations.definition: A tRNA sequence that has a selenocysteine anticodon, and a 3' selenocysteine binding region.

annotations.label: syntenic_region
annotations.definition: A region in which two or more pairs of homologous markers occur on the same chromosome in two or more species.

annotations.label: biochemical_region_of_peptide
annotations.definition: A region of a peptide that is involved in a biochemical function.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: molecular_contact_region
annotations.definition: A region that is involved a contact with another molecule.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: intrinsically_unstructured_polypeptide_region
annotations.definition: A region of polypeptide chain with high conformational flexibility.

annotations.label: catmat_left_handed_three
annotations.definition: A motif of 3 consecutive residues with dihedral angles as follows: res i: phi -90 bounds -120 to -60, res i: psi -10 bounds -50 to 30, res i+1: phi -75 bounds -100 to -50, res i+1: psi 140 bounds 110 to 170. An extra restriction of the length of the O to O distance would be useful, that it be less than 5 Angstrom. More precisely these two oxygens are the main chain carbonyl oxygen atoms of residues i-1 and i+1.

annotations.label: catmat_left_handed_four
annotations.definition: A motif of 4 consecutive residues with dihedral angles as follows: res i: phi -90 bounds -120 to -60, res i psi -10 bounds -50 to 30, res i+1: phi -90 bounds -120 to -60, res i+1: psi -10 bounds -50 to 30, res i+2: phi -75 bounds -100 to -50, res i+2: psi 140 bounds 110 to 170. The extra restriction of the length of the O to O distance is similar, that it be less than 5 Angstrom. In this case these two Oxygen atoms are the main chain carbonyl oxygen atoms of residues i-1 and i+2.

annotations.label: catmat_right_handed_three
annotations.definition: A motif of 3 consecutive residues with dihedral angles as follows: res i: phi -90 bounds -120 to -60, res i: psi -10 bounds -50 to 30, res i+1: phi -75 bounds -100 to -50, res i+1: psi 140 bounds 110 to 170. An extra restriction of the length of the O to O distance would be useful, that it be less than 5 Angstrom. More precisely these two oxygens are the main chain carbonyl oxygen atoms of residues i-1 and i+1.

annotations.label: catmat_right_handed_four
annotations.definition: A motif of 4 consecutive residues with dihedral angles as follows: res i: phi -90 bounds -120 to -60, res i: psi -10 bounds -50 to 30, res i+1: phi -90 bounds -120 to -60, res i+1: psi -10 bounds -50 to 30, res i+2: phi -75 bounds -100 to -50, res i+2: psi 140 bounds 110 to 170. The extra restriction of the length of the O to O distance is similar, that it be less than 5 Angstrom. In this case these two Oxygen atoms are the main chain carbonyl oxygen atoms of residues i-1 and i+2.

annotations.label: alpha_beta_motif
annotations.definition: A motif of five consecutive residues and two H-bonds in which: H-bond between CO of residue(i) and NH of residue(i+4), H-bond between CO of residue(i) and NH of residue(i+3),Phi angles of residues(i+1), (i+2) and (i+3) are negative.

annotations.label: lipoprotein_signal_peptide
annotations.definition: A peptide that acts as a signal for both membrane translocation and lipid attachment in prokaryotes.

synonyms.text: prokaryotic membrane lipoprotein lipid attachment site

annotations.label: no_output
annotations.definition: An experimental region wherean analysis has been run and not produced any annotation.

annotations.label: cleaved_peptide_region
annotations.definition: The cleaved_peptide_region is the region of a peptide sequence that is cleaved during maturation.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: peptide_coil
annotations.definition: Irregular, unstructured regions of a protein's backbone, as distinct from the regular region (namely alpha helix and beta strand - characterised by specific patterns of main-chain hydrogen bonds).

annotations.label: hydrophobic_region_of_peptide
annotations.definition: Hydrophobic regions are regions with a low affinity for water.
annotations.comment: Range.

annotations.label: n_terminal_region
annotations.definition: The amino-terminal positively-charged region of a signal peptide (approx 1-5 aa).

annotations.label: c_terminal_region
annotations.definition: The more polar, carboxy-terminal region of the signal peptide (approx 3-7 aa).

annotations.label: central_hydrophobic_region_of_signal_peptide
annotations.definition: The central, hydrophobic region of the signal peptide (approx 7-15 aa).

synonyms.text: central hydrophobic region of signal peptide

annotations.label: polypeptide_conserved_motif
annotations.definition: A conserved motif is a short (up to 20 amino acids) region of biological interest that is conserved in different proteins. They may or may not have functional or structural significance within the proteins in which they are found.

annotations.label: polypeptide_binding_motif
annotations.definition: A polypeptide binding motif is a short (up to 20 amino acids) polypeptide region of biological interest that contains one or more amino acids experimentally shown to bind to a ligand.

annotations.label: polypeptide_catalytic_motif
annotations.definition: A polypeptide catalytic motif is a short (up to 20 amino acids) polypeptide region that contains one or more active site residues.

annotations.label: polypeptide_DNA_contact
annotations.definition: A binding site that, in the polypeptide molecule, interacts selectively and non-covalently with DNA.

annotations.label: polypeptide_conserved_region
annotations.definition: A subsection of sequence with biological interest that is conserved in different proteins. They may or may not have functional or structural significance within the proteins in which they are found.

annotations.label: substitution
annotations.definition: A sequence alteration where the length of the change in the variant is the same as that of the reference.

annotations.label: complex_substitution
annotations.definition: When no simple or well defined DNA mutation event describes the observed DNA change, the keyword "complex" should be used. Usually there are multiple equally plausible explanations for the change.

annotations.label: point_mutation
annotations.definition: A single nucleotide change which has occurred at the same position of a corresponding nucleotide in a reference sequence.

annotations.label: transition
annotations.definition: Change of a pyrimidine nucleotide, C or T, into an other pyrimidine nucleotide, or change of a purine nucleotide, A or G, into an other purine nucleotide.

annotations.label: pyrimidine_transition
annotations.definition: A substitution of a pyrimidine, C or T, for another pyrimidine.

annotations.label: C_to_T_transition
annotations.definition: A transition of a cytidine to a thymine.

annotations.label: C_to_T_transition_at_pCpG_site
annotations.definition: The transition of cytidine to thymine occurring at a pCpG site as a consequence of the spontaneous deamination of 5'-methylcytidine.

synonyms.text: C to T transition at pCpG site

annotations.label: T_to_C_transition
annotations.definition: A transition of a thymine to a cytidine.

annotations.label: purine_transition
annotations.definition: A substitution of a purine, A or G, for another purine.

annotations.label: A_to_G_transition
annotations.definition: A transition of an adenine to a guanine.

annotations.label: G_to_A_transition
annotations.definition: A transition of a guanine to an adenine.

annotations.label: transversion
annotations.definition: Change of a pyrimidine nucleotide, C or T, into a purine nucleotide, A or G, or vice versa.

annotations.label: pyrimidine_to_purine_transversion
annotations.definition: Change of a pyrimidine nucleotide, C or T, into a purine nucleotide, A or G.

annotations.label: C_to_A_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion from cytidine to adenine.

annotations.label: C_to_G_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion of a cytidine to a guanine.

annotations.label: T_to_A_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion from T to A.

annotations.label: T_to_G_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion from T to G.

annotations.label: purine_to_pyrimidine_transversion
annotations.definition: Change of a purine nucleotide, A or G , into a pyrimidine nucleotide C or T.

annotations.label: A_to_C_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion from adenine to cytidine.

annotations.label: A_to_T_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion from adenine to thymine.

annotations.label: G_to_C_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion from guanine to cytidine.

annotations.label: G_to_T_transversion
annotations.definition: A transversion from guanine to thymine.

annotations.label: intrachromosomal_mutation
annotations.definition: A chromosomal structure variation within a single chromosome.

annotations.label: chromosomal_deletion
annotations.definition: An incomplete chromosome.

annotations.label: chromosomal_inversion
annotations.definition: An interchromosomal mutation where a region of the chromosome is inverted with respect to wild type.

annotations.label: interchromosomal_mutation
annotations.definition: A chromosomal structure variation whereby more than one chromosome is involved.

annotations.label: delins
annotations.definition: A sequence alteration which included an insertion and a deletion, affecting 2 or more bases.
annotations.comment: Indels can have a different number of bases than the corresponding reference sequence. The term name was changed from indel to delins on 2/24/2019 to align with the HGVS nomenclature term for a deletion-insertion. Indel was causing confusion in the annotation community (github issue 445). The HGVS nomenclature definition of deletion-insertion (delins) is a sequence change where, compared to a reference sequence, one or more nucleotides are replaced by one or more other nucleotides and which is not a substitution, inversion or conversion.

annotations.label: duplication
annotations.definition: An insertion which derives from, or is identical in sequence to, nucleotides present at a known location in the genome.

annotations.label: inversion
annotations.definition: A continuous nucleotide sequence is inverted in the same position.

annotations.label: chromosomal_duplication
annotations.definition: An extra chromosome.

annotations.label: intrachromosomal_duplication
annotations.definition: A duplication that occurred within a chromosome.

annotations.label: direct_tandem_duplication
annotations.definition: A tandem duplication where the individual regions are in the same orientation.

annotations.label: inverted_tandem_duplication
annotations.definition: A tandem duplication where the individual regions are not in the same orientation.

annotations.label: intrachromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variation whereby a transposition occurred within a chromosome.

annotations.label: compound_chromosome
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variant where a monocentric element is caused by the fusion of two chromosome arms.

annotations.label: Robertsonian_fusion
annotations.definition: A non reciprocal translocation whereby the participating chromosomes break at their centromeres and the long arms fuse to form a single chromosome with a single centromere.

annotations.label: chromosomal_translocation
annotations.definition: A chromosomal mutation. Rearrangements that alter the pairing of telomeres are classified as translocations.

annotations.label: ring_chromosome
annotations.definition: A ring chromosome is a chromosome whose arms have fused together to form a ring, often with the loss of the ends of the chromosome.

annotations.label: pericentric_inversion
annotations.definition: A chromosomal inversion that includes the centromere.

annotations.label: paracentric_inversion
annotations.definition: A chromosomal inversion that does not include the centromere.

annotations.label: reciprocal_chromosomal_translocation
annotations.definition: A chromosomal translocation with two breaks; two chromosome segments have simply been exchanged.

annotations.label: autosynaptic_chromosome
annotations.definition: An autosynaptic chromosome is the aneuploid product of recombination between a pericentric inversion and a cytologically wild-type chromosome.

annotations.label: homo_compound_chromosome
annotations.definition: A compound chromosome whereby two copies of the same chromosomal arm attached to a common centromere. The chromosome is diploid for the arm involved.

annotations.label: hetero_compound_chromosome
annotations.definition: A compound chromosome whereby two arms from different chromosomes are connected through the centromere of one of them.

annotations.label: chromosome_fission
annotations.definition: A chromosome that occurred by the division of a larger chromosome.

annotations.label: dextrosynaptic_chromosome
annotations.definition: An autosynaptic chromosome carrying the two right (D = dextro) telomeres.
annotations.comment: Corrected spelling from dexstrosynaptic_chromosome to dextrosynaptic_chromosome on April 14, 2020 in response to GitHub request #447

annotations.label: laevosynaptic_chromosome
annotations.definition: LS is an autosynaptic chromosome carrying the two left (L = levo) telomeres.

annotations.label: free_duplication
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variation whereby the duplicated sequences are carried as a free centric element.

annotations.label: free_ring_duplication
annotations.definition: A ring chromosome which is a copy of another chromosome.

annotations.label: deficient_translocation
annotations.definition: A chromosomal deletion whereby a translocation occurs in which one of the four broken ends loses a segment before re-joining.

annotations.label: inversion_cum_translocation
annotations.definition: A chromosomal translocation whereby the first two breaks are in the same chromosome, and the region between them is rejoined in inverted order to the other side of the first break, such that both sides of break one are present on the same chromosome. The remaining free ends are joined as a translocation with those resulting from the third break.

annotations.label: bipartite_duplication
annotations.definition: An interchromosomal mutation whereby the (large) region between the first two breaks listed is lost, and the two flanking segments (one of them centric) are joined as a translocation to the free ends resulting from the third break.

annotations.label: cyclic_translocation
annotations.definition: A chromosomal translocation whereby three breaks occurred in three different chromosomes. The centric segment resulting from the first break listed is joined to the acentric segment resulting from the second, rather than the third.

annotations.label: bipartite_inversion
annotations.definition: A chromosomal inversion caused by three breaks in the same chromosome; both central segments are inverted in place (i.e., they are not transposed).

annotations.label: uninverted_insertional_duplication
annotations.definition: An insertional duplication where a copy of the segment between the first two breaks listed is inserted at the third break; the insertion is in cytologically the same orientation as its flanking segments.

annotations.label: inverted_insertional_duplication
annotations.definition: An insertional duplication where a copy of the segment between the first two breaks listed is inserted at the third break; the insertion is in cytologically inverted orientation with respect to its flanking segments.

annotations.label: insertional_duplication
annotations.definition: A chromosome duplication involving the insertion of a duplicated region (as opposed to a free duplication).

annotations.label: interchromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variation whereby a transposition occurred between chromosomes.

annotations.label: inverted_interchromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An interchromosomal transposition whereby a copy of the segment between the first two breaks listed is inserted at the third break; the insertion is in cytologically inverted orientation with respect to its flanking segment.

annotations.label: uninverted_interchromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An interchromosomal transition where the segment between the first two breaks listed is removed and inserted at the third break; the insertion is in cytologically the same orientation as its flanking segments.

annotations.label: inverted_intrachromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An intrachromosomal transposition whereby the segment between the first two breaks listed is removed and inserted at the third break; the insertion is in cytologically inverted orientation with respect to its flanking segments.

annotations.label: uninverted_intrachromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An intrachromosomal transposition whereby the segment between the first two breaks listed is removed and inserted at the third break; the insertion is in cytologically the same orientation as its flanking segments.

annotations.label: unoriented_insertional_duplication
annotations.definition: An insertional duplication where a copy of the segment between the first two breaks listed is inserted at the third break; the orientation of the insertion with respect to its flanking segments is not recorded.
annotations.comment: Flag - unknown in the definition.

annotations.label: unoriented_interchromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An interchromosomal transposition whereby a copy of the segment between the first two breaks listed is inserted at the third break; the orientation of the insertion with respect to its flanking segments is not recorded.
annotations.comment: FLAG - term describes an unknown.

annotations.label: unoriented_intrachromosomal_transposition
annotations.definition: An intrachromosomal transposition whereby the segment between the first two breaks listed is removed and inserted at the third break; the orientation of the insertion with respect to its flanking segments is not recorded.
annotations.comment: FLAG - definition describes an unknown.

annotations.label: uncharacterized_chromosomal_mutation
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variant that has not been characterized.

annotations.label: deficient_inversion
annotations.definition: A chromosomal deletion whereby three breaks occur in the same chromosome; one central region is lost, and the other is inverted.

annotations.label: tandem_duplication
annotations.definition: A duplication consisting of 2 identical adjacent regions.

annotations.label: partially_characterized_chromosomal_mutation
annotations.definition: A chromosome structure variant that has not been characterized fully.

annotations.label: chromosome_number_variation
annotations.definition: A kind of chromosome variation where the chromosome complement is not an exact multiple of the haploid number.

annotations.label: chromosome_structure_variation
annotations.definition: An alteration of the genome that leads to a change in the structure or number of one or more chromosomes.

annotations.label: alternatively_spliced_transcript
annotations.definition: A transcript that is alternatively spliced.

annotations.label: encodes_1_polypeptide
annotations.definition: A gene that is alternately spliced, but encodes only one polypeptide.

annotations.label: encodes_greater_than_1_polypeptide
annotations.definition: A gene that is alternately spliced, and encodes more than one polypeptide.

annotations.label: encodes_different_polypeptides_different_stop
annotations.definition: A gene that is alternately spliced, and encodes more than one polypeptide, that have overlapping peptide sequences, but use different stop codons.

annotations.label: encodes_overlapping_peptides_different_start
annotations.definition: A gene that is alternately spliced, and encodes more than one polypeptide, that have overlapping peptide sequences, but use different start codons.

annotations.label: encodes_disjoint_polypeptides
annotations.definition: A gene that is alternately spliced, and encodes more than one polypeptide, that do not have overlapping peptide sequences.

annotations.label: encodes_overlapping_polypeptides_different_start_and_stop
annotations.definition: A gene that is alternately spliced, and encodes more than one polypeptide, that have overlapping peptide sequences, but use different start and stop codons.

synonyms.text: encodes overlapping polypeptides different start and stop

annotations.label: encodes_overlapping_peptides
annotations.definition: A gene that is alternately spliced, and encodes more than one polypeptide, that have overlapping peptide sequences.

annotations.label: cryptogene
annotations.definition: A maxicircle gene so extensively edited that it cannot be matched to its edited mRNA sequence.

annotations.label: dicistronic_primary_transcript
annotations.definition: A primary transcript that has the quality dicistronic.

annotations.label: member_of_regulon
annotations.definition: A gene that is a member of a group of genes that are either regulated or transcribed together.

annotations.label: CDS_independently_known
annotations.definition: A CDS with the evidence status of being independently known.

annotations.label: orphan_CDS
annotations.definition: A CDS whose predicted amino acid sequence is unsupported by any experimental evidence or by any match with any other known sequence.

annotations.label: CDS_supported_by_domain_match_data
annotations.definition: A CDS that is supported by domain similarity.

synonyms.text: CDS supported by domain match data

annotations.label: CDS_supported_by_sequence_similarity_data
annotations.definition: A CDS that is supported by sequence similarity data.

synonyms.text: CDS supported by sequence similarity data

annotations.label: CDS_predicted
annotations.definition: A CDS that is predicted.

annotations.label: CDS_supported_by_EST_or_cDNA_data
annotations.definition: A CDS that is supported by similarity to EST or cDNA data.

synonyms.text: CDS supported by EST or cDNA data

annotations.label: internal_Shine_Dalgarno_sequence
annotations.definition: A Shine-Dalgarno sequence that stimulates recoding through interactions with the anti-Shine-Dalgarno in the RNA of small ribosomal subunits of translating ribosomes. The signal is only operative in Bacteria.

annotations.label: recoded_mRNA
annotations.definition: The sequence of a mature mRNA transcript, modified before translation or during translation, usually by special cis-acting signals.

annotations.label: minus_1_translationally_frameshifted
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a translational frameshift of -1.

annotations.label: plus_1_translationally_frameshifted
annotations.definition: An attribute describing a translational frameshift of +1.

annotations.label: mRNA_recoded_by_translational_bypass
annotations.definition: A recoded_mRNA where translation was suspended at a particular codon and resumed at a particular non-overlapping downstream codon.

annotations.label: mRNA_recoded_by_codon_redefinition
annotations.definition: A recoded_mRNA that was modified by an alteration of codon meaning.

annotations.label: recoding_stimulatory_region
annotations.definition: A site in an mRNA sequence that stimulates the recoding of a region in the same mRNA.

annotations.label: four_bp_start_codon
annotations.definition: A non-canonical start codon with 4 base pairs.

annotations.label: archaeal_intron
annotations.definition: An intron characteristic of Archaeal tRNA and rRNA genes, where intron transcript generates a bulge-helix-bulge motif that is recognised by a splicing endoribonuclease.
annotations.comment: Intron characteristic of tRNA genes; splices by an endonuclease-ligase mediated mechanism.

annotations.label: tRNA_intron
annotations.definition: An intron found in tRNA that is spliced via endonucleolytic cleavage and ligation rather than transesterification.
annotations.comment: Could be a cross product with Gene ontology, GO:0006388.

annotations.label: CTG_start_codon
annotations.definition: A non-canonical start codon of sequence CTG.

annotations.label: SECIS_element
annotations.definition: The incorporation of selenocysteine into a protein sequence is directed by an in-frame UGA codon (usually a stop codon) within the coding region of the mRNA. Selenoprotein mRNAs contain a conserved secondary structure in the 3' UTR that is required for the distinction of UGA stop from UGA selenocysteine. The selenocysteine insertion sequence (SECIS) is around 60 nt in length and adopts a hairpin structure which is sufficiently well-defined and conserved to act as a computational screen for selenoprotein genes.

annotations.label: retron
annotations.definition: Sequence coding for a short, single-stranded, DNA sequence via a retrotransposed RNA intermediate; characteristic of some microbial genomes.

annotations.label: three_prime_recoding_site
annotations.definition: The recoding stimulatory signal located downstream of the recoding site.

annotations.label: three_prime_stem_loop_structure
annotations.definition: A recoding stimulatory region, the stem-loop secondary structural element is downstream of the redefined region.

annotations.label: five_prime_recoding_site
annotations.definition: The recoding stimulatory signal located upstream of the recoding site.

annotations.label: flanking_three_prime_quadruplet_recoding_signal
annotations.definition: Four base pair sequence immediately downstream of the redefined region. The redefined region is a frameshift site. The quadruplet is 2 overlapping codons.

synonyms.text: flanking three prime quadruplet recoding signal

annotations.label: UAG_stop_codon_signal
annotations.definition: A stop codon signal for a UAG stop codon redefinition.

annotations.label: UAA_stop_codon_signal
annotations.definition: A stop codon signal for a UAA stop codon redefinition.

annotations.label: regulon
annotations.definition: A set of units of gene expression directly regulated by a common set of one or more common regulatory gene products.
annotations.comment: Definition updated with Mejia-Almonte et.al PMID:32665585 on Aug 5, 2020. Added relationship has_part SO:0002300

annotations.label: UGA_stop_codon_signal
annotations.definition: A stop codon signal for a UGA stop codon redefinition.

annotations.label: three_prime_repeat_recoding_signal
annotations.definition: A recoding stimulatory signal, downstream sequence important for recoding that contains repetitive elements.

annotations.label: distant_three_prime_recoding_signal
annotations.definition: A recoding signal that is found many hundreds of nucleotides 3' of a redefined stop codon.

annotations.label: stop_codon_signal
annotations.definition: A recoding stimulatory signal that is a stop codon and has effect on efficiency of recoding.
annotations.comment: This term does not include the stop codons that are redefined. An example would be a stop codon that partially overlapped a frame shifting site would be an example stimulatory signal.

annotations.label: databank_entry
annotations.definition: The sequence referred to by an entry in a databank such as GenBank or SwissProt.

annotations.label: gene_segment
annotations.definition: A gene component region which acts as a recombinational unit of a gene whose functional form is generated through somatic recombination.
annotations.comment: Requested by tracker 2021594, July 2008, by Alex.

annotations.label: associated_with
annotations.comment: This relationship is vague and up for discussion.

annotations.label: complete_evidence_for_feature
annotations.definition: B is complete_evidence_for_feature A if the extent (5' and 3' boundaries) and internal boundaries of B fully support the extent and internal boundaries of A.
annotations.comment: If A is a feature with multiple regions such as a multi exon transcript, the supporting EST evidence is complete if each of the regions is supported by an equivalent region in B. Also there must be no extra regions in B that are not represented in A. This relationship was requested by jeltje on the SO term tracker. The thread for the discussion is available can be accessed via tracker ID:1917222.

annotations.label: connects_on
annotations.definition: X connects_on Y, Z, R iff whenever Z is on a R, X is adjacent to a Y and adjacent to a Z.
annotations.comment: Example: A splice_junction connects_on exon, exon, mature_transcript.

annotations.label: contained_by
annotations.definition: X contained_by Y iff X starts after start of Y and X ends before end of Y.
annotations.comment: The inverse is contains. Example: intein contained_by immature_peptide_region.

annotations.label: contains
annotations.definition: The inverse of contained_by.
annotations.comment: Example: pre_miRNA contains miRNA_loop.

annotations.label: disconnected_from
annotations.definition: X is disconnected_from Y iff it is not the case that X overlaps Y.

annotations.label: evidence_for_feature
annotations.definition: B is evidence_for_feature A, if an instance of B supports the existence of A.
annotations.comment: This relationship was requested by nlw on the SO term tracker. The thread for the discussion is available can be accessed via tracker ID:1917222.

annotations.label: exemplar_of
annotations.definition: X is exemplar of Y if X is the best evidence for Y.
annotations.comment: Tracker id: 2594157.

annotations.label: finished_by
annotations.definition: Xy is finished_by Y if Y part of X, and X and Y share a 3' boundary.
annotations.comment: Example CDS finished_by stop_codon.

annotations.label: finishes
annotations.definition: X finishes Y if X is part_of Y and X and Y share a 3' or C terminal boundary.
annotations.comment: Example: stop_codon finishes CDS.

annotations.label: gained
annotations.definition: X gained Y if X is a variant_of X' and Y part of X but not X'.
annotations.comment: A relation with which to annotate the changes in a variant sequence with respect to a reference.\nFor example a variant transcript may gain a stop codon not present in the reference sequence.

annotations.label: has_integral_part
annotations.definition: X has_integral_part Y if and only if: X has_part Y and Y part_of X.
annotations.comment: Example: mRNA has_integral_part CDS.

annotations.label: integral_part_of
annotations.definition: X integral_part_of Y if and only if: X part_of Y and Y has_part X.
annotations.comment: Example: exon integral_part_of transcript.

annotations.label: is_consecutive_sequence_of
annotations.definition: R is_consecutive_sequence_of R iff every instance of R is equivalent to a collection of instances of U:u1, u2, un, such that no pair of ux uy is overlapping and for all ux, it is adjacent to ux-1 and ux+1, with the exception of the initial and terminal u1,and un (which may be identical).
annotations.comment: Example: region is consecutive_sequence of base.

annotations.label: lost
annotations.definition: X lost Y if X is a variant_of X' and Y part of X' but not X.
annotations.comment: A relation with which to annotate the changes in a variant sequence with respect to a reference.\nFor example a variant transcript may have lost a stop codon present in the reference sequence.

annotations.label: maximally_overlaps
annotations.definition: A maximally_overlaps X iff all parts of A (including A itself) overlap both A and Y.
annotations.comment: Example: non_coding_region_of_exon maximally_overlaps the intersections of exon and UTR.

annotations.label: member_of
annotations.comment: A subtype of part_of. Inverse is collection_of. Winston, M, Chaffin, R, Herrmann: A taxonomy of part-whole relations. Cognitive Science 1987, 11:417-444.

annotations.label: non_functional_homolog_of
annotations.definition: A relationship between a pseudogenic feature and its functional ancestor.

annotations.label: partial_evidence_for_feature
annotations.definition: B is partial_evidence_for_feature A if the extent of B supports part_of but not all of A.

annotations.label: processed_from
annotations.definition: Inverse of processed_into.
annotations.comment: Example: miRNA processed_from miRNA_primary_transcript.

annotations.label: processed_into
annotations.definition: X is processed_into Y if a region X is modified to create Y.
annotations.comment: Example: miRNA_primary_transcript processed into miRNA.

annotations.label: started_by
annotations.definition: X is strted_by Y if Y is part_of X and X and Y share a 5' boundary.
annotations.comment: Example: CDS started_by start_codon.

annotations.label: transcribed_from
annotations.definition: X is transcribed_from Y if X is synthesized from template Y.
annotations.comment: Example: primary_transcript transcribed_from gene.

annotations.label: transcribed_to
annotations.definition: Inverse of transcribed_from.
annotations.comment: Example: gene transcribed_to primary_transcript.

annotations.label: translates_to
annotations.definition: Inverse of translation _of.
annotations.comment: Example: codon translates_to amino_acid.

annotations.label: translation_of
annotations.definition: X is translation of Y if Y is translated by ribosome to create X.
annotations.comment: Example: Polypeptide translation_of CDS.

annotations.label: variant_of
annotations.definition: A' is a variant (mutation) of A = definition every instance of A' is either an immediate mutation of some instance of A, or there is a chain of immediate mutation processes linking A' to some instance of A.
annotations.comment: Added to SO during the immunology workshop, June 2007. This relationship was approved by Barry Smith.
